title.zh: 卷九十四上 匈奴傳
title.raw: 卷九十四上 匈奴傳 Volume 94a: Traditions of the Xiongnu 1

sentences.zh: 卷九十四上 匈奴傳第六十四上

translations.literal: Volume ninety-four, part one: Traditions of the Xiongnu, the sixty-fourth, part one.
translations.idiomatic: Volume 94a: "The Xiongnu," the sixty-fourth treatise, part one.

sentences.zh: 匈奴，其先夏後氏之苗裔，曰淳維。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu: their ancestors were scions of the Xia house, called Chunwei.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu traced their line to a Xia descendant named Chunwei.

sentences.zh: 唐、虞以上有山戎、獫允、薰粥，居於北邊，隨草畜牧而轉移。

translations.literal: Before Tang and Yu there were the Shanrong, Xianyun, and Xunyu, dwelling on the northern marches, following the grass and herding, shifting as they moved.
translations.idiomatic: Long before the sage-kings of high antiquity, peoples such as the Shanrong, Xianyun, and Xunyu already roamed the northern frontier, moving their herds with the seasons.

sentences.zh: 其畜之所多則馬、牛、羊，其奇畜則橐佗、驢、騾、駃騠、騊駼、驒奚。

translations.literal: Their herds were mostly horses, cattle, and sheep; their unusual beasts were camels, donkeys, mules, jüetí, táotú, and diānxī.
translations.idiomatic: They raised chiefly horses, cattle, and sheep, with oddities such as the Bactrian camel, donkey, mule, jüetí, táotú, and diānxī.

sentences.zh: 逐水草遷徙，無城郭常居耕田之業，然亦各有分地。

translations.literal: They followed the water and grass in migration, without walled cities, fixed dwellings, or the work of plowed fields, yet each group also had its allotted range.
translations.idiomatic: They drifted after pasture and water, building no cities and sowing no grain, yet each tribe still held its own grazing ground.

sentences.zh: 無文書，以言語為約束。

translations.literal: They had no documents; speech alone bound them.
translations.idiomatic: They kept no written records; a man's word was his contract.

sentences.zh: 兒能騎羊，引弓射鳥鼠，少長則射狐菟，肉食。

translations.literal: When boys could ride sheep they drew the bow to shoot birds and rats; as they grew they shot fox and hare and fed on flesh.
translations.idiomatic: A boy who could straddle a sheep was given a bow for birds and mice; soon he hunted fox and hare and lived on meat.

sentences.zh: 士力能彎弓，盡為甲騎。

translations.literal: Every man strong enough to bend a bow became armored horseman.
translations.idiomatic: Every able-bodied male became an armored rider.

sentences.zh: 其俗，寬則隨畜田獵禽獸為生業，急則人習戰攻以侵伐，其天性也。

translations.literal: Their custom: in ease they followed the herds, hunting birds and beasts for a living; in haste they drilled for war and raided—their inborn nature.
translations.idiomatic: In peace they followed the herds and hunted; in crisis they turned to raid and battle—it was second nature.

sentences.zh: 其長兵則弓矢，短兵則刀鋌。

translations.literal: For long weapons they had bow and arrow; for short, knife and spear.
translations.idiomatic: Long reach meant bow and arrow; close work meant knife and spear.

sentences.zh: 利則進，不利則退，不羞遁走。

translations.literal: If it profited them they advanced; if not they withdrew, and felt no shame in flight.
translations.idiomatic: They advanced when the odds were good and pulled back when they were not, with no shame in retreat.

sentences.zh: 苟利所在，不知禮義。

translations.literal: Wherever gain lay, they knew neither rites nor duty.
translations.idiomatic: Where profit pointed, ritual and right meant nothing.

sentences.zh: 自君王以下鹹食畜肉，衣其皮革，被旃裘。

translations.literal: From the ruler down all ate the flesh of their herds, wore hides and leather, and wrapped themselves in felt and fur.
translations.idiomatic: From the chanyu downward everyone ate herd meat, wore hides, and wrapped in felt and fur.

sentences.zh: 壯者食肥美，老者飲食其餘。

translations.literal: The strong ate the fat and fine; the old drank and ate what was left.
translations.idiomatic: The warriors took the best cuts; elders lived on the leavings.

sentences.zh: 貴壯健，賤老弱。

translations.literal: They honored the vigorous and slighted the old and weak.
translations.idiomatic: Youth and strength won respect; age and frailty counted for little.

sentences.zh: 父死，妻其後母；

translations.literal: When the father died, they married his junior wife after him;
translations.idiomatic: A son could take his late father's younger wives;

sentences.zh: 兄弟死，皆取其妻妻之。

translations.literal: when brothers died, they all took their wives and married them.
translations.idiomatic: when brothers died, the survivors married their widows.

sentences.zh: 其俗有名不諱而無字。

translations.literal: Their custom had personal names without taboo avoidance and without courtesy names.
translations.idiomatic: They used personal names without taboo or style names.

sentences.zh: 夏道衰，而公劉失其稷官，變於西戎，邑於豳。

translations.literal: When the way of Xia declined, Gong Liu lost his post as lord of millet, changed among the western Rong, and founded a town at Bin.
translations.idiomatic: When Xia rule faded, Gong Liu lost his charge over the grain sacrifice, went native among the western Rong, and built a settlement at Bin.

sentences.zh: 其後三百有餘歲，戎狄攻太王但父，但父亡走於岐下，豳人悉從但父而邑焉，作周。

translations.literal: More than three hundred years later the Rong and Di attacked Grand King Danfu; Danfu fled to the foot of Mount Qi, and the people of Bin all followed Danfu and built a town there, founding Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Three centuries on, Rong and Di attacked Grand King Danfu; he fled to the slopes of Mount Qi, the Bin people followed, and there they laid the foundations of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 其後百有餘歲，周西伯昌伐畎夷。

translations.literal: More than a hundred years later the western Earl of Zhou, Chang, attacked the Quanyi.
translations.idiomatic: A century after that, the western Earl Chang campaigned against the Quanyi.

sentences.zh: 後十有餘年，武王伐紂而營雒邑，復居於酆鎬，放逐戎夷涇、洛之北，以時入貢，名曰荒服。

translations.literal: After more than ten years King Wu attacked Zhou and laid out Luoyi, but dwelt again at Feng and Hao, driving the Rong and Yi north of the Jing and Luo to render tribute on schedule—this was called the "wild service."
translations.idiomatic: A dozen years later King Wu overthrew the Shang, planned Luoyi, yet kept his capital at Feng and Hao and pushed the outer tribes north of the Jing and Luo to pay seasonal tribute—the "wild" outer ring of submission.

sentences.zh: 其後二百有餘年，周道衰，而周穆王伐畎戎，得四白狼、四白鹿以歸。

translations.literal: More than two hundred years later the way of Zhou declined, and King Mu of Zhou attacked the Quanrong, taking four white wolves and four white deer home.
translations.idiomatic: Two centuries on, royal power slipped; King Mu struck the Quanrong and came home boasting four white wolves and four white deer.

sentences.zh: 自是之後，荒服不至。

translations.literal: From this the wild service no longer came.
translations.idiomatic: After that the outer tribes stopped sending tribute.

sentences.zh: 於是作《呂刑》之辟。

translations.literal: Thereupon they framed the punishments of the 《Lüxing》.
translations.idiomatic: The court responded by promulgating the penal code known as the 《Punishments of Lü》.

sentences.zh: 至穆王之孫懿王時，王室遂衰，戎狄交侵，暴虐中國。

translations.literal: By the time of Mu's grandson King Yi, the royal house had declined; Rong and Di raided in turn and ravaged the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Under King Yi, Mu's grandson, the house of Zhou withered while Rong and Di took turns plundering the heartland.

sentences.zh: 中國被其苦，詩人始作，疾而歌之，曰：「靡室靡家，獫允之故」；

translations.literal: The central states suffered their harm; poets first composed, hating and singing it, saying: "No chamber, no home—because of the Xianyun";
translations.idiomatic: The heartland reeled, and the 《Classic of Poetry》 caught its pain: "No roof, no hearth—blame the Xianyun";

sentences.zh: 「豈不日戒，獫允孔棘」。

translations.literal: "Could we not be daily on guard? The Xianyun press hard."
translations.idiomatic: "Could we not watch every day? The Xianyun give no rest."

sentences.zh: 至懿王曾孫宣王，興師命將以征伐之，詩人美大其功，曰：「薄伐獫允，至於太原」；

translations.literal: Down to King Yi's great-grandson King Xuan, he raised armies and ordered generals to campaign against them; the poets magnified the achievement, saying: "We punished the Xianyun as far as Taiyuan";
translations.idiomatic: Under King Xuan, Yi's great-grandson, royal armies marched again, and the odes sang the feat: "We struck the Xianyun clear to Taiyuan";

sentences.zh: 「出車彭彭」，「城彼朔方」。

translations.literal: "Our chariots boomed," "We walled that northern land."
translations.idiomatic: "Chariots rolled in thunder," "We raised walls on the northern frontier."

sentences.zh: 是時四夷賓服，稱為中興。

translations.literal: At that time the four quarters submitted and it was called the mid-dynasty restoration.
translations.idiomatic: The four quarters bent the knee—later ages called it the "royal revival."

sentences.zh: 至於幽王，用寵姬褒姒之故，與申侯有隙。

translations.literal: Under King You, because of the favorite Bao Si, he fell out with the Marquis of Shen.
translations.idiomatic: King You doted on Bao Si and broke with the lord of Shen.

sentences.zh: 申侯怒而與畎戎共攻殺幽王於麗山之下，遂取周之地，鹵獲而居於涇、渭之間，侵暴中國。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Shen in anger joined with the Quanrong to attack and kill King You below Mount Li, seized Zhou territory, plundered and dwelt between the Jing and Wei, invading and ravaging the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Shen allied with the Quanrong, slew You beneath Mount Li, seized royal ground, and camped between the Jing and Wei, raiding deep into the central plain.

sentences.zh: 秦襄公救周，於是周平王去酆鎬而東徙於雒邑。

translations.literal: Lord Xiang of Qin rescued Zhou; thereupon King Ping of Zhou left Feng and Hao and moved east to Luoyi.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Xiang of Qin rode to the rescue, and King Ping abandoned Feng-Hao for the eastern capital at Luoyi.

sentences.zh: 當時秦襄公伐戎至支阜，始列為諸侯。

translations.literal: At that time Lord Xiang of Qin campaigned against the Rong as far as Zhifou and first ranked among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Qin Xiang drove the Rong to the Zhifou hills and earned his place among the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 後六十有五年，而山戎越燕而伐齊，齊釐公與戰於齊郊。

translations.literal: Sixty-five years later the Shanrong crossed Yan to attack Qi, and Duke Xi of Qi fought them on Qi's border.
translations.idiomatic: Sixty-five years on, Shanrong swept past Yan into Qi; Duke Xi met them on his border.

sentences.zh: 後四十四年，而山戎伐燕。

translations.literal: Forty-four years later the Shanrong attacked Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Forty-four years after that they fell on Yan.

sentences.zh: 燕告急齊，齊桓公北伐山戎，山戎走。

translations.literal: Yan cried for help to Qi; Duke Huan of Qi marched north against the Shanrong, and the Shanrong fled.
translations.idiomatic: Yan begged Qi for aid; Duke Huan marched north and drove the Shanrong off.

sentences.zh: 後二十餘年，而戎翟至雒邑，伐周襄王，襄王出奔於鄭之汜邑。

translations.literal: More than twenty years later the Rong and Di reached Luoyi, attacked King Xiang of Zhou, and Xiang fled in exile to the Si district of Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Two decades later Rong and Di reached Luoyi, struck King Xiang, and chased him to Si in Zheng.

sentences.zh: 初，襄王欲伐鄭，故取翟女為後，與翟共伐鄭。

translations.literal: Earlier, because King Xiang wished to attack Zheng, he had taken a Di woman as queen and joined the Di in attacking Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier Xiang had married a Di princess to gain allies for a war on Zheng.

sentences.zh: 已而黜翟後，翟後怨，而襄王繼母曰惠後，有子帶，欲立之，於是惠後與翟後、子帶為內應，開戎翟，戎翟以故得入，破逐襄王，而立子帶為王。

translations.literal: Later he deposed the Di queen; the Di queen resented it, while Xiang's stepmother Queen Hui had a son Dai whom she wished to establish; thereupon Queen Hui, the Di queen, and Dai acted as inner agents, opening the gates to the Rong and Di, who for this reason entered, routed and expelled King Xiang, and set up Dai as king.
translations.idiomatic: He then cast the Di queen aside; she plotted with Queen Hui—who wanted her son Prince Dai on the throne—and they opened the city to the Di, who drove Xiang out and crowned Dai.

sentences.zh: 於是戎翟或居於陸渾，東至於衛，侵盜尤甚。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Rong and Di sometimes dwelt at Luhun, reaching east to Wei, and their pillaging grew worse.
translations.idiomatic: The invaders spread from Luhun east toward Wei, and their raiding grew fiercer.

sentences.zh: 周襄王既居外四年，乃使使告急於晉。

translations.literal: After King Xiang of Zhou had dwelt abroad four years, he sent envoys to cry for help to Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Four years in exile, Xiang sent pleading envoys to Jin.

sentences.zh: 晉文公初立，欲修霸業，乃興師伐戎翟，誅子帶，迎內襄王子雒邑。

translations.literal: When Duke Wen of Jin had just taken the throne he wished to repair hegemony, therefore raised troops, attacked the Rong and Di, executed Dai, and welcomed King Xiang to install him again at Luoyi.
translations.idiomatic: The new Duke Wen of Jin, building a hegemon's name, crushed the Di, killed Dai, and escorted Xiang home to Luoyi.

sentences.zh: 當是時，秦晉為強國。

translations.literal: At that time Qin and Jin were the strong states.
translations.idiomatic: Qin and Jin now towered over the other states.

sentences.zh: 晉文公攘戎翟，居於西河圜、洛之間，號曰赤翟、白翟。

translations.literal: Duke Wen of Jin pushed aside the Rong and Di and settled them between the western River's Yuan and Luo—called the Red Di and White Di.
translations.idiomatic: Jin cleared the Rong and Di west of the great river, between the Yuan and Luo streams—the "Red Di" and "White Di."

sentences.zh: 而秦穆公得由余，西戎八國服於秦。

translations.literal: Duke Mu of Qin obtained You Yu, and eight western Rong states submitted to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Qin Mugong won the defector You Yu and brought eight western tribes to heel.

sentences.zh: 故隴以西有綿諸、畎戎、狄獂之戎，在岐、梁、涇、漆之北有義渠、大荔、烏氏、朐衍之戎，而晉北有林胡、樓煩之戎，燕北有東胡、山戎。

translations.literal: Therefore west of Long were the Rong of Mianzhu, Quanrong, and Dīyuán; north of Qi, Liang, Jing, and Qi were the Rong of Yiqu, Dali, Wushi, and Quyǎn; north of Jin were the forest Hu and Loufan Rong; north of Yan were the Eastern Hu and Shanrong.
translations.idiomatic: West of the Long corridor lay Mianzhu, Quanrong, and Dīyuán; north of Qi, Liang, Jing, and Qi sat Yiqu, Dali, Wushi, and Quyǎn; beyond Jin stretched the forest Hu and Loufan; beyond Yan, the Eastern Hu and Shanrong.

sentences.zh: 各分散溪谷，自有君長，往往而聚者百有餘戎，然莫能相一。

translations.literal: Each scattered in ravines and valleys, with its own chiefs; often more than a hundred Rong bands gathered, yet none could unify the others.
translations.idiomatic: They split into hundreds of valley chiefdoms, none strong enough to unite the rest.

sentences.zh: 自是之後百有餘年，晉悼公使魏絳和戎翟，戎翟朝晉。

translations.literal: From this more than a hundred years later, Duke Dao of Jin sent Wei Jiang to make peace with the Rong and Di, and the Rong and Di came to court at Jin.
translations.idiomatic: A century later Duke Dao of Jin sent Wei Jiang to pacify the tribes, and their chiefs attended court at Jin.

sentences.zh: 後百有餘年，趙襄子逾句注而破之，並代以臨胡貉。

translations.literal: More than a hundred years later Zhao Xiangzi crossed Mount Gouzhu and broke them, annexing Dai to face the Hu and Mo.
translations.idiomatic: Another century on, Zhao Xiangzi crossed Gouzhu, shattered the border tribes, and annexed Dai to stare down the Hu.

sentences.zh: 後與韓、魏共滅知伯，分晉地而有之，則趙有代、句注以北，而魏有西河、上郡，以與戎界邊。

translations.literal: Later, together with Han and Wei, he destroyed the house of Zhi, divided Jin's territory and held it; then Zhao held Dai and the land north of Gouzhu, while Wei held the western River and Shang commandery, bordering the Rong.
translations.idiomatic: With Han and Wei he extirpated Zhi and split Jin; Zhao kept Dai and the north of Gouzhu, Wei the west-bank and Shang commanderies along the tribal frontier.

sentences.zh: 其後，義渠之戎築城郭以自守，而秦稍蠶食之，至於惠王，遂拔義渠二十五城。

translations.literal: Afterward the Yiqu Rong built walls to defend themselves, but Qin nibbled them away bit by bit; down to King Hui it finally took twenty-five cities of Yiqu.
translations.idiomatic: The Yiqu Rong walled their towns, but Qin gnawed them away until King Hui snapped up twenty-five Yiqu cities.

sentences.zh: 惠王伐魏，魏盡入西河及上郡於秦。

translations.literal: King Hui attacked Wei, and Wei ceded the entire western River and Shang commandery to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: King Hui of Qin then seized Wei's west-bank lands and Shang commandery wholesale.

sentences.zh: 秦昭王時，義渠戎王與宣太后亂，有二子。

translations.literal: In the time of King Zhaoxiang of Qin the Yiqu Rong king committed adultery with Queen Dowager Xuan and had two sons.
translations.idiomatic: Under King Zhaoxiang the Yiqu king kept a scandalous liaison with Queen Dowager Xuan and fathered two sons.

sentences.zh: 宣太后詐而殺義渠戎王於甘泉，遂起兵伐滅義渠。

translations.literal: Queen Dowager Xuan deceived and killed the Yiqu Rong king at Ganquan, then raised troops, attacked, and extinguished Yiqu.
translations.idiomatic: She lured the Yiqu king to Ganquan and had him murdered, then sent armies to wipe Yiqu out.

sentences.zh: 於是秦有隴西、北地、上郡，築長城以距胡。

translations.literal: Thereupon Qin held Longxi, Beidi, and Shang commandery, and built a long wall to hold off the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Qin thus took Longxi, Beidi, and Shang, and threw up a long wall against the Hu.

sentences.zh: 而趙武靈王亦變俗胡服，習騎射，北破林胡、樓煩，自代並陰山下至高闕為塞，而置雲中、雁門、代郡。

translations.literal: King Wuling of Zhao likewise changed custom to Hu dress, practiced riding and archery, broke the forest Hu and Loufan to the north, ran a barrier from Dai along the Yin range to Gaoque, and set up Yunzhong, Yanmen, and Dai commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wuling adopted Hu robes and mounted archery, crushed the forest Hu and Loufan, strung fortifications from Dai along the Yin Mountains to Gaoque, and founded Yunzhong, Yanmen, and Dai commanderies.

sentences.zh: 其後燕有賢將秦開，為質於胡，胡甚信之。

translations.literal: Later Yan had a worthy general, Qin Kai, who was hostage among the Hu and was greatly trusted by them.
translations.idiomatic: Yan later fielded Qin Kai, who lived as a hostage among the Hu and won their confidence.

sentences.zh: 歸而襲破東胡，東胡卻千餘里。

translations.literal: When he returned he raided and broke the Eastern Hu, who fell back more than a thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: He came home to ambush the Eastern Hu and drove them a thousand li.

sentences.zh: 與荊軻刺秦王秦舞陽者，開之孫也。

translations.literal: Qin Wuyang, who with Jing Ke stabbed the king of Qin, was Kai's grandson.
translations.idiomatic: The boy Qin Wuyang who accompanied Jing Ke was his grandson.

sentences.zh: 燕亦築長城，自造陽至襄平，置上谷、漁陽、右北平、遼西、遼東郡以距胡。

translations.literal: Yan also built a long wall from Zaoyang to Xiangping, establishing Shanggu, Yuyang, Youbeiping, Liaoxi, and Liaodong commanderies to hold off the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Yan ran its own wall from Zaoyang to Xiangping and carved out five northern commanderies against the Hu.

sentences.zh: 當是時，冠帶戰國七，而三國邊於匈奴。

translations.literal: At that time there were seven warring states in caps and sashes, and three of them bordered the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Seven great powers wore the silks of civilization; three shared a frontier with the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 其後趙將李牧時，匈奴不敢入趙邊。

translations.literal: Later, in the time of Zhao's general Li Mu, the Xiongnu did not dare enter Zhao's border.
translations.idiomatic: Under Zhao's general Li Mu the Xiongnu would not test the Zhao line.

sentences.zh: 後秦滅六國，而始皇帝使蒙恬將數十萬之眾北擊胡，悉收河南地，因河為塞，築四十四縣城臨河，徙適戍以充之。

translations.literal: Later Qin destroyed the six states, and the First Emperor sent Meng Tian at the head of several hundred thousand north to strike the Hu, recovered all the land south of the great river, took the river as a barrier, built forty-four county towns overlooking the river, and moved convict garrisons to fill them.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin swallowed the rivals, the First Emperor sent Meng Tian north with hundreds of thousands, reclaimed the whole south bank, used the river as a shield, raised forty-four riverside counties, and packed them with exiles in uniform.

sentences.zh: 而通直道，自九原至雲陽，因邊山險，塹溪谷，可繕者繕之，起臨洮至遼東萬餘裡。

translations.literal: He also opened a straight road from Jiuyuan to Yunyang; along the border mountains and defiles he cut ravines and gorges, repairing where repair was possible, raising from Lintao to Liaodong more than ten thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: He cut a straight highway from Jiuyuan to Yunyang and stitched a wall along defiles and cliffs—where earth could be piled it was—from Lintao to Liaodong, over ten thousand li.

sentences.zh: 又度河據陽山北假中。

translations.literal: He also crossed the river and held the north of Mount Yangshan in the Beijia belt.
translations.idiomatic: He crossed the river and planted garrisons north of Mount Yangshan in the Beijia loop.

sentences.zh: 當是時，東胡強而月氏盛。

translations.literal: At that time the Eastern Hu were strong and the Yuezhi flourished.
translations.idiomatic: The Eastern Hu were still fierce and the Yuezhi at their peak.

sentences.zh: 匈奴單于曰頭曼，頭曼不勝素，北徙。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu chanyu was called Touman; Touman could not withstand Qin and moved north.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu was Touman; unable to stand against Qin, he pulled his people northward.

sentences.zh: 十有餘年而蒙恬死，諸侯畔秦，中國擾亂，諸秦所徙適邊者皆復去，於是匈奴得寬，復稍度河南與中國界於故塞。

translations.literal: After more than ten years Meng Tian died, the feudal lords rebelled against Qin, the central states were in turmoil, and all the convict colonists Qin had moved to the border went back again; thereupon the Xiongnu had respite and gradually crossed south of the great river again, bordering the central states at the old barrier.
translations.idiomatic: A decade later Meng Tian was dead, rebels tore Qin apart, the border colonists drifted home, and the Xiongnu slipped back south of the river to the old picket line.

sentences.zh: 單于有太子，名曰冒頓。

translations.literal: The chanyu had a heir son named Modu.
translations.idiomatic: Touman's heir was named Modu.

sentences.zh: 後有愛閼氏，生少子，頭曼欲廢冒頓而立少子，乃使冒頓質於月氏。

translations.literal: Later he had a favorite Yan clan wife who bore a younger son; Touman wished to depose Modu and establish the younger son, so he sent Modu as hostage to the Yuezhi.
translations.idiomatic: A favored queen bore a second son; Touman meant to disinherit Modu and sent him as hostage to the Yuezhi.

sentences.zh: 冒頓既質，而頭曼急擊月氏。

translations.literal: After Modu had been sent as hostage, Touman urgently attacked the Yuezhi.
translations.idiomatic: No sooner was Modu delivered than Touman struck the Yuezhi hard.

sentences.zh: 月氏欲殺冒頓，冒頓盜其善馬，騎亡歸。

translations.literal: The Yuezhi wished to kill Modu; Modu stole their good horse and rode off in flight.
translations.idiomatic: The Yuezhi meant to execute him; he stole a prized stallion and galloped home.

sentences.zh: 頭曼以為壯，令將萬騎。

translations.literal: Touman thought him brave and ordered him to command ten thousand riders.
translations.idiomatic: Touman, impressed, gave him command of ten thousand horse.

sentences.zh: 冒頓乃作鳴鏑，習勒其騎射，令曰：「鳴鏑所射而不悉射者斬。

translations.literal: Modu then made whistling arrows, drilled his riders in shooting, and ordered: "Whoever does not shoot wherever the whistling arrow shoots is beheaded."
translations.idiomatic: Modu forged whistling arrows and drilled his men: "Every shaft I whistle to must be met with a volley—or you die."

sentences.zh: 」行獵獸，有不射鳴鏑所射輒斬之。

translations.literal: " When hunting beasts, whoever did not shoot where the whistling arrow shot was immediately beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: On hunts, anyone who held fire when his signal flew lost his head.

sentences.zh: 已而，冒頓以鳴鏑自射善馬，左右或莫敢射，冒頓立斬之。

translations.literal: After a time Modu shot his own good horse with the whistling arrow; some of those at his side did not dare shoot, and Modu immediately beheaded them.
translations.idiomatic: He whistled into his favorite mount; men who flinched were cut down at once.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，復以鳴鏑自射其愛妻，左右或頗恐，不敢射，復斬之。

translations.literal: A little later he again shot his beloved wife with the whistling arrow; some of those at his side were quite frightened and did not dare shoot, and he beheaded them again.
translations.idiomatic: He repeated the test on a favorite consort; those who hesitated followed the horse boys to the block.

sentences.zh: 頃之，冒頓出獵，以鳴鏑射單于善馬，左右皆射之。

translations.literal: Soon Modu went hunting, shot the chanyu's good horse with the whistling arrow, and those at his side all shot it.
translations.idiomatic: Finally he whistled into Touman's best horse, and every companion riddled it.

sentences.zh: 於是冒頓知其左右可用，從其父單于頭曼獵，以鳴鏑射頭曼，其左右皆隨鳴鏑而射殺頭曼，盡誅其後母與弟及大臣不聽從者。

translations.literal: Thereupon Modu knew those at his side could be used; following his father Chanyu Touman on a hunt, he shot Touman with the whistling arrow, and those at his side all followed the whistling arrow and shot Touman dead; he executed to the last his stepmother and younger brother and the great ministers who would not obey.
translations.idiomatic: Sure of his killers, he whistled Touman himself on a royal hunt; the same men emptied their quivers into the chanyu, then Modu slaughtered his stepmother, half-brother, and every minister who balked.

sentences.zh: 於是冒頓自立為單于。

translations.literal: Thereupon Modu established himself as chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Modu mounted the throne as chanyu.

sentences.zh: 冒頓既立，時東胡強，聞冒頓殺父自立，乃使使謂冒頓曰：「欲得頭曼時號千里馬。

translations.literal: After Modu had been established, at that time the Eastern Hu were strong; hearing that Modu had killed his father and set himself up, they sent envoys to say to Modu: "We wish to obtain the thousand-li horse of Touman's time."
translations.idiomatic: The Eastern Hu, still strong, heard he had murdered his way to power and demanded Touman's famous stallion.

sentences.zh: 」冒頓問群臣，群臣皆曰：「此匈奴寶馬也，勿予。

translations.literal: " Modu asked his ministers; all the ministers said: "This is a treasure horse of the Xiongnu; do not give it."
translations.idiomatic: His council said no—the horse was a national treasure.

sentences.zh: 」冒頓曰：「奈何與人鄰國愛一馬乎？

translations.literal: " Modu said: "How can we, neighboring a state, grudge one horse?"
translations.idiomatic: " "What neighbor hoards a single horse?" Modu asked.

sentences.zh: 」遂與之。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he gave it.
translations.idiomatic: " He sent the horse."

sentences.zh: 頃之，東胡以為冒頓畏之，使使謂冒頓曰：「欲得單于一閼氏。

translations.literal: Before long the Eastern Hu, thinking Modu feared them, sent envoys to say to Modu: "We wish to obtain one Yan-clan wife of the chanyu."
translations.idiomatic: Thinking him cowed, they next asked for one of his queens.

sentences.zh: 」冒頓復問左右，左右皆怒曰：「東胡無道，乃求閼氏！

translations.literal: " Modu again asked those at his side; all angrily said: "The Eastern Hu are without the Way—to ask for a Yan wife!
translations.idiomatic: The court erupted: "Outrageous—they want your queen!"

sentences.zh: 請擊之。

translations.literal: " We beg to strike them."
translations.idiomatic: " Let us attack!"

sentences.zh: 」冒頓曰：「奈何與人鄰國愛一女子乎？

translations.literal: " Modu said: "How can we, neighboring a state, grudge one woman?"
translations.idiomatic: " "Would I risk war for one woman?" he said.

sentences.zh: 」遂取所愛閼氏予東胡。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he took the Yan wife he loved and gave her to the Eastern Hu.
translations.idiomatic: " He handed over a favorite consort."

sentences.zh: 東胡王愈驕，西侵。

translations.literal: The Eastern Hu king grew prouder still and invaded westward.
translations.idiomatic: The Eastern Hu king grew arrogant and pushed west.

sentences.zh: 與匈奴中間有棄地莫居千餘里，各居其邊為甌脫。

translations.literal: Between them and the Xiongnu lay abandoned land where none dwelt for more than a thousand li; each side held its edge as a buffer strip.
translations.idiomatic: A thousand li of no-man's-land lay between them, each patrolling its own rim.

sentences.zh: 東胡使使謂冒頓曰：「匈奴所與我界甌脫外棄地，匈奴不能至也，吾欲有之。

translations.literal: The Eastern Hu sent envoys to say to Modu: "The abandoned land beyond the buffer strip that forms the border with us—the Xiongnu cannot reach it; we wish to possess it."
translations.idiomatic: They demanded that empty belt beyond the patrol zone—the land neither side used.

sentences.zh: 」冒頓問群臣，或曰：「此棄地，予之。

translations.literal: " Modu asked his ministers; some said: "This is abandoned land; give it."
translations.idiomatic: Half his advisers shrugged: "Waste ground—let them have it."

sentences.zh: 」於是冒頓大怒，曰：「地者，國之本也，奈何予人！

translations.literal: " Thereupon Modu was greatly angry and said: "Land is the root of a state—how can we give it to others!"
translations.idiomatic: " "Soil is the nation's bone," Modu roared. "Who gives away bone?"

sentences.zh: 」諸言與者，皆斬之。

translations.literal: " All who had spoken for giving it were beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: Everyone who had said "give" went to the headsman.

sentences.zh: 冒頓上馬，令國中有後者斬，遂東襲擊東胡。

translations.literal: Modu mounted his horse, ordered that in the state whoever lagged behind be beheaded, and thereupon struck east and attacked the Eastern Hu.
translations.idiomatic: He vaulted into the saddle, ordered stragglers decapitated, and fell on the Eastern Hu.

sentences.zh: 東胡初輕冒頓，不為備。

translations.literal: The Eastern Hu at first despised Modu and made no preparations.
translations.idiomatic: They had laughed at his concessions and left their guard down.

sentences.zh: 及冒頓以兵至，大破滅東胡王，虜其民眾、畜產。

translations.literal: When Modu arrived with troops he greatly broke and destroyed the Eastern Hu king, taking captive their people and herds.
translations.idiomatic: Modu's host rolled over them, killed their king, and swept off people and livestock.

sentences.zh: 既歸，西擊走月氏，南並樓煩、白羊河南王，悉復收秦所使蒙恬所奪匈奴地者，與漢關胡河南塞，至朝那、膚施，遂侵燕、代。

translations.literal: Having returned, he struck west, routed the Yuezhi, south annexed Loufan and the White Sheep king south of the great river, fully recovered all the Xiongnu lands that Qin had taken through Meng Tian, bordered Han at the barrier south of the river against the Hu, reaching Zhaona and Fushi, then invaded Yan and Dai.
translations.idiomatic: On his return he drove the Yuezhi west, swallowed Loufan and the White Sheep tribe south of the Yellow River, reclaimed every pasture Meng Tian had stripped from the Xiongnu, and pushed his picket line against Han from Zhaona to Fushi, raiding into Yan and Dai.

sentences.zh: 是時，漢方與項羽相距，中國罷於兵革，以故冒頓得自強，控弦之士三十餘萬。

translations.literal: At that time Han was locked with Xiang Yu; the central states were exhausted by arms, and for this reason Modu was able to strengthen himself—more than three hundred thousand men who could draw the bow.
translations.idiomatic: While Han and Xiang Yu tore at each other, China was bled white; Modu seized the moment to swell his host to three hundred thousand bowmen.

sentences.zh: 自淳維以至頭曼千有餘歲，時大時小，別散分離，尚矣，其世傳不可得而次。

translations.literal: From Chunwei down to Touman was more than a thousand years; at times large, at times small, splitting and scattering in separation—it is remote, and their generations cannot be ordered in sequence.
translations.idiomatic: From Chunwei to Touman stretched a thousand years of rise and fall, splinter tribes and lost genealogies—no orderly list survives.

sentences.zh: 然至冒頓，而匈奴最強大，盡服從北夷，而南與諸夏為敵國，其世姓官號可得而記雲。

translations.literal: Yet down to Modu the Xiongnu were strongest and largest, wholly subduing the northern Yi and facing south as a rival state to the various Xia; their lineage surnames and official titles can be recorded.
translations.idiomatic: Under Modu they peaked: every northern tribe bowed while they squared off against China as a peer power—at last their clans and titles can be set down.

sentences.zh: 單于姓攣鞮氏，其國稱之曰「撐犁孤塗單于」。

translations.literal: The chanyu's surname was Luandi; their state called him "Chanyu of the Chēnglí Gūtú."
translations.idiomatic: The royal clan was Luandi; the full style of the throne was "Chanyu, Son of Heaven, of the Chēnglí Gūtú line."

sentences.zh: 匈奴謂天為「撐犁」，謂子為「孤塗」，單于者，廣大之貌也，言其象天單于然也。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu called heaven "Chēnglí," called a son "Gūtú"; chanyu means a vast appearance, saying his likeness is as heaven alone is vast.
translations.idiomatic: In their tongue "Chēnglí" was heaven and "Gūtú" was the heir; "chanyu" meant "all-encompassing as the sky."

sentences.zh: 置左右賢王、左右谷蠡、左右大將、左右大都尉、左右大當戶、左右骨都侯。

translations.literal: They set up left and right Wise Kings, left and right Gūlì, left and right Grand Generals, left and right Grand Commandants, left and right Grand Household Heads, and left and right Gūdū Marquises.
translations.idiomatic: The court was staffed with paired wings: Wise Kings, Gūlì princes, grand generals, grand commandants, grand household heads, and Gūdū marquises left and right.

sentences.zh: 匈奴謂賢曰「屠耆」，故嘗以太子為左屠耆王。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu called the worthy "Túqí," therefore they often made the heir apparent left Túqí king.
translations.idiomatic: They called a worthy man "Túqí," so the crown prince usually held the left Túqí kingship.

sentences.zh: 自左右賢王以下至當戶，大者萬餘騎，小者數千，凡二十四長，立號曰「萬騎」。

translations.literal: From the left and right Wise Kings down to the household heads, the large had more than ten thousand riders, the small several thousand; in all twenty-four chiefs, each titled "ten thousand riders."
translations.idiomatic: Each of the twenty-four banners mustered from a few thousand to over ten thousand horse, all loosely called "ten-thousand" divisions.

sentences.zh: 其大臣皆世官。

translations.literal: Their great ministers were all hereditary offices.
translations.idiomatic: High office ran in the bloodline.

sentences.zh: 呼衍氏、蘭氏，其後有須卜氏，此三姓，其貴種也。

translations.literal: The Huyan and Lan surnames, and afterward the Xubu clan—these three surnames were their noble breeds.
translations.idiomatic: The noble houses were Huyan, Lan, and later Xubu.

sentences.zh: 諸左王將居東方，直上谷以東，接穢貉、朝鮮；

translations.literal: The various left kings and generals dwelt in the east, due north from Shanggu eastward, joining the Mohe and Chosŏn;
translations.idiomatic: The eastern wing held the ground from Shanggu east to Korea and the Mohe;

sentences.zh: 右王將居西方，直上郡以西，接氐、羌；

translations.literal: the right kings and generals dwelt in the west, due north from Shang commandery westward, joining the Di and Qiang;
translations.idiomatic: the western wing ran from Shang commandery west toward Di and Qiang country;

sentences.zh: 而單于庭直代、雲中。

translations.literal: while the chanyu's court lay due north of Dai and Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: the chanyu's headquarters sat due north of Dai and Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 各有分地，逐水草移徙。

translations.literal: Each had allotted territory, following the water and grass in migration.
translations.idiomatic: Each command kept its own pastures and moved with the seasons.

sentences.zh: 而左右賢王、左右谷蠡最大國，左右骨都侯輔政。

translations.literal: Among them the left and right Wise Kings and left and right Gūlì were the greatest states; the left and right Gūdū marquises assisted government.
translations.idiomatic: The Wise Kings and Gūlì princes were the greatest appanages; Gūdū marquises handled daily governance.

sentences.zh: 諸二十四長，亦各自置千長、百長、什長、裨小王、相、都尉、當戶、且渠之屬。

translations.literal: The twenty-four chiefs likewise each set up thousand-chiefs, hundred-chiefs, ten-chiefs, assistant lesser kings, chancellors, commandants, household heads, and qúqú and the like.
translations.idiomatic: Every "ten-thousand" chief ran his own pyramid of thousand-, hundred-, and ten-man leaders, petty kings, chancellors, commandants, and household heads.

sentences.zh: 歲正月，諸長小會單于庭，祠。

translations.literal: In the year's first month the various chiefs held a small assembly at the chanyu court for sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: Each new year the chiefs gathered at court for the small sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 五月，大會龍城，祭其先、天地、鬼神。

translations.literal: In the fifth month they held the great assembly at Dragon City, sacrificing to ancestors, heaven and earth, and spirits.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month they met at Dragon City for the great sacrifice to gods and ancestors.

sentences.zh: 秋，馬肥，大會蹛林，課校人畜計。

translations.literal: In autumn, when horses were fat, they held the great assembly at Dailín, reviewing and counting people and livestock.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, when the herds were fat, they mustered at Dailín to audit men and beasts.

sentences.zh: 其法，拔刃尺者死，坐盜者沒入其家；

translations.literal: Their law: whoever drew a blade a foot long died; sitting for theft meant confiscation of the household;
translations.idiomatic: Law was harsh: a foot of naked steel meant death; theft cost a family its freedom;

sentences.zh: 有罪，小者軋，大者死。

translations.literal: for crimes, the small were crushed on the wheel, the large executed.
translations.idiomatic: lesser felons were broken on the wheel, greater ones beheaded.

sentences.zh: 獄久者不滿十日，一國之囚不過數人。

translations.literal: Those in jail long did not fill ten days; prisoners in a whole state did not exceed several men.
translations.idiomatic: Trials were swift—few men languished ten days in a cell.

sentences.zh: 而單于朝出營，拜日之始生，夕拜月。

translations.literal: When the chanyu went out of camp in the morning he bowed to the sun's first rising; in the evening he bowed to the moon.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu saluted the newborn sun at dawn and the moon at dusk.

sentences.zh: 其坐，長左而北向。

translations.literal: In sitting, the elders were on the left facing north.
translations.idiomatic: At council the senior men sat left, all facing north.

sentences.zh: 日上戊己。

translations.literal: They honored the days wu and ji of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: They reckoned lucky days by the wu and ji stems.

sentences.zh: 其送死，有棺槨、金銀、衣裳，而無封樹喪服；

translations.literal: For sending off the dead there were inner and outer coffins, gold and silver, and clothing, but no heaped mounds, mourning garments, or trees;
translations.idiomatic: The dead took coffins, metal, and silks into the grave—no mound, no mourning grades, no marker trees;

sentences.zh: 近幸臣妾從死者，多至數十百人。

translations.literal: favored ministers and concubines who followed in death sometimes reached several tens or hundreds of people.
translations.idiomatic: favorite followers and wives often went into the tomb by the score or hundred.

sentences.zh: 舉事常隨月，盛壯以攻戰，月虧則退兵。

translations.literal: In undertaking affairs they always followed the moon: when full and strong they attacked; when the moon waned they withdrew troops.
translations.idiomatic: Campaigns tracked the moon—attack at the full, retire at the wane.

sentences.zh: 其攻戰，斬首虜賜一卮酒，而所得鹵獲因以予之，得人以為奴婢。

translations.literal: In attack and war, for beheading captives they granted one cup of wine, and what plunder was taken they thereupon gave them, taking people to be slaves and maids.
translations.idiomatic: A severed head won a cup of wine; loot stayed with the man who seized it, captives became private slaves.

sentences.zh: 故其戰，人人自為趨利，善為誘兵以包敵。

translations.literal: Therefore in their battles every man acted for himself in pursuing gain, skilled at decoy troops to envelop the enemy.
translations.idiomatic: So every warrior chased profit for himself, expert at feints that encircled foes.

sentences.zh: 故其逐利，如鳥之集；

translations.literal: Therefore in pursuing gain they were like birds gathering;
translations.idiomatic: When fortune smiled they flocked like birds to grain;

sentences.zh: 其困敗，瓦解雲散矣。

translations.literal: in distress and defeat they scattered like clouds breaking apart.
translations.idiomatic: when fortune turned they melted away like mist.

sentences.zh: 戰而扶轝死者，盡得死者家財。

translations.literal: In battle, whoever carried off the dead in the litter wagon received all the dead man's household property.
translations.idiomatic: Whoever dragged a fallen comrade from the field inherited that man's whole tent and herd.

sentences.zh: 後北服渾窳、屈射、丁零、隔昆、新{艸犁}之國。

translations.literal: Later he subdued to the north the states of Hunyu, Qushe, Dingling, Gekun, and Xinli.
translations.idiomatic: He then forced the northern realms of Hunyu, Qushe, Dingling, Gekun, and Xinli into submission.

sentences.zh: 於是匈奴貴人大臣皆服，以冒頓為賢。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Xiongnu nobles and great ministers all submitted, deeming Modu worthy.
translations.idiomatic: The nobles knelt to him and called him a true chanyu.

sentences.zh: 是時，漢初定，徙韓王信於代，都馬邑。

translations.literal: At that time Han had just been settled; they moved King Xin of Han to Dai with his capital at Mayi.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han empire was new, Gaozu shifted Han Xin to Dai with his seat at Mayi.

sentences.zh: 匈奴大攻圍馬邑，韓信降匈奴。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu greatly attacked and besieged Mayi; Han Xin surrendered to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu ringed Mayi until Han Xin opened the gates to them.

sentences.zh: 匈奴得信，因引兵南逾句注，攻太原，至晉陽下。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu, having obtained Xin, thereupon led troops south across Mount Gouzhu, attacked Taiyuan, and reached below Jinyang.
translations.idiomatic: With Xin as guide they crossed Gouzhu, stormed Taiyuan, and camped below Jinyang.

sentences.zh: 高帝自將兵往擊之。

translations.literal: The High Emperor himself led troops to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu marched in person to drive them back.

sentences.zh: 會冬大寒雨雪，卒之墮指者十二三，於是冒頓陽敗走，誘漢兵。

translations.literal: It happened that in winter there was great cold, rain, and snow; two or three tenths of the soldiers lost fingers to frostbite; thereupon Modu feigned defeat and fled, luring the Han troops.
translations.idiomatic: A blizzard froze one man in three; Modu pretended flight and baited the Han vanguard.

sentences.zh: 漢兵逐擊冒頓，冒頓匿其精兵，見其羸弱，於是漢悉兵三十二萬，北逐之。

translations.literal: The Han troops pursued and struck Modu; Modu hid his crack troops and displayed his weak; thereupon Han used all its troops, three hundred twenty thousand, and pursued them north.
translations.idiomatic: Han swallowed the lure and flung three hundred twenty thousand men after him while Modu hid his veterans and showed stragglers.

sentences.zh: 高帝先至平城，步兵未盡到，冒頓縱精兵三十餘萬騎圍高帝於白登，七日，漢兵中外不得相救餉。

translations.literal: The High Emperor first reached Pingcheng before the foot soldiers had all arrived; Modu loosed more than three hundred thousand picked riders and surrounded the High Emperor on Baideng seven days; Han troops inside and outside could not aid or supply each other.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu reached Pingcheng ahead of his infantry; Modu slammed three hundred thousand horse around Baideng for seven days, cutting the emperor off from relief.

sentences.zh: 匈奴騎，其西方盡白，東方盡駹，北方盡驪，南方盡騂馬。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu riders: on the west all white horses, on the east all máng horses, on the north all dark horses, on the south all chestnut horses.
translations.idiomatic: He ringed the hill with colored squadrons—white to the west, gray-blue to the east, black to the north, chestnut to the south.

sentences.zh: 高帝乃使使間厚遺閼氏，閼氏乃謂冒頓曰：「兩主不相困。

translations.literal: The High Emperor then sent envoys secretly with rich gifts to the Yan wife; the Yan wife thereupon said to Modu: "Two lords ought not trap each other.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu slipped bribes to Modu's chief queen; she whispered, "Two thrones should not strangle each other."

sentences.zh: 今得漢地，單于終非能居之。

translations.literal: " "Even if you take Han land, the chanyu in the end cannot dwell in it.
translations.idiomatic: " Even if you seize Han soil, you cannot live on it."

sentences.zh: 且漢主有神，單于察之。

translations.literal: " "Moreover the Han ruler has divine protection—let the chanyu examine it."
translations.idiomatic: " Besides, the Son of Heaven carries heaven's favor—think twice."

sentences.zh: 」冒頓與韓信將王黃、趙利期，而兵久不來，疑其與漢有謀，亦取閼氏之言，乃開圍一角。

translations.literal: " Modu had fixed a rendezvous with Xin's generals Wang Huang and Zhao Li, but the troops long did not come; he suspected they had plotted with Han, and also took the Yan wife's words, therefore opened one corner of the encirclement.
translations.idiomatic: Modu waited in vain for Han Xin's allies Wang Huang and Zhao Li; suspecting treason and swayed by his queen, he cracked the ring.

sentences.zh: 於是高皇帝令士皆持滿傅矢外鄉，從解角直出，得與大軍合，而冒頓遂引兵去。

translations.literal: Thereupon the High Emperor ordered the men all to draw bows full with arrows aimed outward, went straight out through the gap, was able to join the main army, and Modu thereupon led his troops away.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu told his men to face their drawn bows outward and punched through the gap to the main host; Modu then drew off.

sentences.zh: 漢亦引兵罷，使劉敬結和親之約。

translations.literal: Han also led troops away, sending Liu Jing to conclude a marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: Han pulled back and sent Liu Jing to seal a heqin pact.

sentences.zh: 是後，韓信為匈奴將，及趙利、王黃等數背約，侵盜代、雁門、雲中。

translations.literal: Afterward Han Xin was a Xiongnu general, and Zhao Li, Wang Huang, and others repeatedly broke the treaty, invading and plundering Dai, Yanmen, and Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin then rode for the Xiongnu, and Wang Huang and Zhao Li broke the truce again and again, chewing at Dai, Yanmen, and Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 居無幾何，陳豨反，與韓信合謀擊代。

translations.literal: Before long Chen Xi rebelled and joined Han Xin in plotting to strike Dai.
translations.idiomatic: Soon Chen Xi rose in revolt and plotted with Han Xin to hit Dai.

sentences.zh: 漢使樊噲往擊之，復收代、雁門、雲中郡縣，不出塞。

translations.literal: Han sent Fan Kuai to strike them, again recovered the counties of Dai, Yanmen, and Yunzhong, and did not go beyond the barrier.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Kuai retook the lost counties but stopped at the wall.

sentences.zh: 是時，匈奴以漢將數率眾往降，故冒頓常往來侵盜代地。

translations.literal: At that time, because Han generals repeatedly led hosts in surrender, Modu often came and went invading and plundering the Dai region.
translations.idiomatic: Han defectors kept feeding Modu intelligence, so he raided Dai at will.

sentences.zh: 於是高祖患之，乃使劉敬奉宗室女翁主為單于閼氏，歲奉匈奴絮繒酒食物各有數，約為兄弟以和親，冒頓乃少止。

translations.literal: Thereupon the High Ancestor worried over it and sent Liu Jing to present a royal clanswoman, a princess, as the chanyu's Yan wife, yearly presenting the Xiongnu with floss silk, fine silks, wine, and foodstuffs in set amounts, covenanting as sworn brothers in marriage alliance; Modu thereupon somewhat ceased.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu patched the leak with a princess bride, yearly silks and wine, and a sworn-brother treaty; the raids eased.

sentences.zh: 後燕王盧綰復後，率其黨且萬人降匈奴，往來苦上谷以東，終高祖世。

translations.literal: Later the king of Yan, Lu Wan, rebelled again, led his faction of nearly ten thousand in defecting to the Xiongnu, coming and going to harry east of Shanggu until the end of the High Ancestor's reign.
translations.idiomatic: Later Yan's king Lu Wan fled to the steppe with ten thousand followers and raided east of Shanggu for the rest of Gaozu's life.

sentences.zh: 考惠、高后時，冒頓浸驕，乃為書，使使遺高后曰：「孤僨之君，生於沮澤之中，長於平野牛馬之域，數至邊境，願游中國。

translations.literal: In the time of Emperor Hui and Empress Gao, Modu grew gradually arrogant and wrote a letter, sending envoys to present it to Empress Gao, saying: "I, the isolated and embittered lord, was born in the boggy marshes, grew up on the level wilds in the realm of cattle and horses, have several times reached the border, and wish to tour the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Under Hui and the empress dowager Modu turned insolent and sent a letter calling himself a lonely widower bred in the mud who wanted to "visit" China.

sentences.zh: 陛下獨立，孤僨獨居。

translations.literal: " "Your Majesty stands alone; I, isolated, dwell alone.
translations.idiomatic: " You are widowed on your throne; I am alone in my yurt."

sentences.zh: 兩主不樂，無以自虞，願以所有，易其所無。

translations.literal: " "The two lords are unhappy and have no way to amuse themselves—I wish to exchange what I have for what I lack."
translations.idiomatic: " Neither of us has a bedfellow—why not trade what each lacks?"

sentences.zh: 」高後大怒，召丞相平及樊噲、季布等，議斬其使者，發兵而擊之。

translations.literal: " Empress Gao was greatly angry, summoned Chancellor Chen Ping and Fan Kuai, Ji Bu, and others, deliberated beheading the envoys, and raising troops to attack them.
translations.idiomatic: Gao boiled over and wanted the envoys executed and an army raised.

sentences.zh: 樊噲曰：「臣愿得十萬眾，橫行匈奴中。

translations.literal: Fan Kuai said: "Your servant begs a hundred thousand host to rampage through the Xiongnu."
translations.idiomatic: Fan Kuai boasted he could ride roughshod over the steppe with a hundred thousand men.

sentences.zh: 」問季布，布曰：「噲可斬也！

translations.literal: " She asked Ji Bu; Bu said: "Kuai should be beheaded!
translations.idiomatic: Ji Bu answered, "Fan Kuai deserves the block."

sentences.zh: 前陳豨反於代，漢兵三十二萬，噲為上將軍，時匈奴圍高帝於平城，噲不能解圍。

translations.literal: " "Earlier when Chen Xi rebelled in Dai, Han troops were three hundred twenty thousand, Kuai was senior general, yet at the time the Xiongnu surrounded the High Emperor at Pingcheng, Kuai could not lift the siege.
translations.idiomatic: " When Chen Xi rose in Dai you fielded three hundred twenty thousand men and made Fan Kuai top general—yet at Pingcheng he never broke the ring around Gaozu."

sentences.zh: 天下歌之曰：『平城之下亦誠苦，七日不食，不能彀弩。

translations.literal: " The realm sang of it: 'Below Pingcheng it was bitter indeed—seven days without food, unable even to draw the crossbow."
translations.idiomatic: " The soldiers still sing how they starved below Pingcheng."

sentences.zh: 』今歌吟之聲未絕，傷痍者甫起，而噲欲搖動天下，妄言以十萬眾橫行，是面謾也。

translations.literal: " "Now the song has not died away, the wounded have barely risen, and Kuai wishes to shake the realm, rashly saying with a hundred thousand he will rampage—this is boasting to your face.
translations.idiomatic: " The wounds are still fresh and he brags again—pure bluff."

sentences.zh: 且夷狄璧如禽獸，得其善言不足喜，惡言不足怒也。

translations.literal: " "Moreover the Yi and Di are like birds and beasts—getting their fine words is not worth joy, their coarse words not worth anger."
translations.idiomatic: " Treat barbarian letters like animal noise—praise nor insult should move you."

sentences.zh: 」高后曰：「善。

translations.literal: " Empress Gao said: "Good."
translations.idiomatic: " The empress dowager said, "Well spoken."

sentences.zh: 」令大謁者張澤報書曰：「單于不忘弊邑，賜之以書，弊邑恐懼。

translations.literal: " She ordered Grand Usher Zhang Shi to answer the letter: "The chanyu has not forgotten our poor town and graces us with a letter; our poor town is fearful.
translations.idiomatic: She had Zhang Shi answer: "We are humbled that you remembered our petty court."

sentences.zh: 退而自圖，年老氣衰，發齒墮落，行步失度，單于過聽，不足以自污。

translations.literal: " "Retiring and reflecting, I am old, breath failing, hair and teeth falling, steps unsteady; the chanyu has listened too well—it would shame me to accept.
translations.idiomatic: " I am a crone whose teeth and hair are going—do not honor me with such talk."

sentences.zh: 弊邑無罪，宜在見赦。

translations.literal: " "Our poor town is without crime and ought to be seen as forgiven.
translations.idiomatic: " We have done no wrong; let the slate be clean."

sentences.zh: 竊有御車二乘，馬二駟，以奉常駕。

translations.literal: " "We privately have two pairs of chariots and two teams of four horses to offer for your constant driving."
translations.idiomatic: " We send two carriages and eight horses for your daily use."

sentences.zh: 」冒頓得書，復使使來謝曰：「未嘗聞中國禮義，陛下幸而赦之。

translations.literal: " When Modu obtained the letter, he again sent envoys to apologize, saying: "We had not heard of China's rites and duty; Your Majesty has favored us with forgiveness.
translations.idiomatic: Modu answered with an apology: "We did not know Han etiquette; thank you for the pardon."

sentences.zh: 」因獻馬，遂和親。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he presented horses, and there was again marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: " He sent horses and the peace marriage held."

sentences.zh: 至孝文即位，復修和親。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wen took the throne, he again cultivated marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen renewed the heqin policy.

sentences.zh: 其三年夏，匈奴右賢王入居河南地為寇，於是文帝下詔曰：「漢與匈奴約為昆弟，無侵害邊境，所以輸遺匈奴甚厚。

translations.literal: In his third year, summer, the Xiongnu right Wise King entered and dwelt south of the great river as a bandit; thereupon Emperor Wen issued an edict saying: "Han and the Xiongnu covenanted as sworn brothers, not to harm the border, for which reason the gifts sent to the Xiongnu have been very generous.
translations.idiomatic: In his third summer the right Wise King camped south of the Yellow River to raid; Wen published an edict: "We have paid lavish bride-price while you pledged brotherhood."

sentences.zh: 今右賢王離其國，將眾居河南地，非常故。

translations.literal: " "Now the right Wise King has left his state, leading the host to dwell south of the great river—this is not the former circumstance.
translations.idiomatic: " Now the right Wise King has moved his host inside the river without cause."

sentences.zh: 往來入塞，捕殺吏卒，驅侵上郡保塞蠻夷，令不得居其故。

translations.literal: " "Coming and going through the barrier, capturing and killing clerks and soldiers, driving and invading the barrier-keeping Yi of Shang commandery so they cannot dwell in their old places.
translations.idiomatic: " He crosses the wall, murders our officers, drives the garrison tribes of Shang from their homes."

sentences.zh: 陵轢邊吏，入盜，甚驁無道，非約也。

translations.literal: " "He tramples border officials, enters to steal, is very arrogant and without the Way—it is not the covenant."
translations.idiomatic: " This is arrogance and lawlessness, not the treaty we signed."

sentences.zh: 其發邊吏車騎八萬詣高奴，遣丞相灌嬰將擊右賢王。

translations.literal: " "Let border officials' chariots and cavalry, eighty thousand, proceed to Gaonu; send Chancellor Guan Ying to lead them in striking the right Wise King."
translations.idiomatic: " I order eighty thousand riders to Gaonu under Chancellor Guan Ying to punish the right Wise King."

sentences.zh: 」右賢王走出塞，文帝幸太原。

translations.literal: " The right Wise King ran out of the barrier; Emperor Wen favored Taiyuan.
translations.idiomatic: The right Wise King fled beyond the wall while Wen inspected Taiyuan.

sentences.zh: 是時，濟北王反，文帝歸，罷丞相擊胡之兵。

translations.literal: At that time the king of Jibei rebelled; Emperor Wen returned and dismissed the chancellor's troops for striking the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: The prince of Jibei then mutinied; Wen rushed home and called off the northern expedition.

sentences.zh: 其明年，單于遺漢書曰：「天所立匈奴大單于敬問皇帝無恙。

translations.literal: The next year the chanyu sent a letter to Han saying: "The great chanyu established by heaven respectfully asks whether the emperor is without illness.
translations.idiomatic: Next year Modu's letter opened: "The heaven-appointed great chanyu greets the Son of Heaven."

sentences.zh: 前時皇帝言和親事，稱書意合歡。

translations.literal: " "Earlier the emperor spoke of marriage alliance matters, saying the letter's intent matched joyful union.
translations.idiomatic: " You spoke of peace and kinship in good faith."

sentences.zh: 漢邊吏侵侮右賢王，右賢王不請，聽後義盧侯難支等計，與漢吏相恨，絕二主之約，離昆弟之親。

translations.literal: " "Han border clerks insulted the right Wise King; the right Wise King did not report it but listened to the plan of Marquis of Rear Yilu Hou Nanzhi and others, hated Han clerks, broke the covenant of the two lords, and sundered the closeness of sworn brothers.
translations.idiomatic: " Petty officers provoked the right Wise King; he listened to hotheads, broke our pact, and severed brotherhood."

sentences.zh: 皇帝讓書再至，發使以書報，不來，漢使不至。

translations.literal: " "The emperor's letters of blame came twice; We sent envoys with letters in reply, but they did not come, and Han envoys did not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: " You sent reproaches twice; our answers never reached you, nor yours us."

sentences.zh: 漢以其故不和，鄰國不附。

translations.literal: " "Han for this reason was not harmonious, and neighboring states did not attach.
translations.idiomatic: " So neighborliness failed."

sentences.zh: 今以少吏之敗約，故罰右賢王，使至西方求月氏擊之。

translations.literal: " "Now because petty clerks spoiled the covenant, We therefore punished the right Wise King, sending him west to seek the Yuezhi and strike them.
translations.idiomatic: " We punished the right Wise King by ordering him west against the Yuezhi."

sentences.zh: 以天之福，吏卒良，馬力強，以滅夷月氏，盡斬殺降下定之。

translations.literal: " "By heaven's blessing, clerks and soldiers were good, horse strength strong, and We destroyed and leveled the Yuezhi, beheading, killing, surrendering, and settling them all.
translations.idiomatic: " Heaven favored us—soldiers and mounts were strong and the Yuezhi were crushed."

sentences.zh: 樓蘭、烏孫、呼揭及其旁二十六國皆已為匈奴。

translations.literal: " "Loulan, Wusun, Hujie, and the twenty-six states beside them have all submitted to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: " Loulan, Wusun, Hujie, and twenty-six neighbors now answer to us."

sentences.zh: 諸引弓之民並為一家，北州以定。

translations.literal: " "All bow-bearing peoples have become one family; the northern quarter is settled.
translations.idiomatic: " Every archer on the steppe is one tribe again; the north is quiet."

sentences.zh: 願寢兵休士養馬，除前事，復故約，以安邊民，以應古始，使少者得成其長，老者得安其處，世世平樂。

translations.literal: " "We wish to rest weapons, spare soldiers, and fatten horses, clear away former affairs, restore the old covenant, secure the border people, answer to antiquity's beginning, let the young finish growing and the old rest in their places, generation on generation in peace and joy.
translations.idiomatic: " We ask to sheathe swords, rest men and horses, wipe the slate, renew the old treaty, and let border folk grow old in peace."

sentences.zh: 未得皇帝之志，故使郎中系虖淺奉書請，獻橐佗一，騎馬二，駕二駟。

translations.literal: " "We have not obtained the emperor's intent, therefore We send Gentleman of the Palace Xihujian to present this letter and request, offering one camel, two riding horses, and two teams of four for driving.
translations.idiomatic: " Not knowing your mind, we send Xihujian with one camel, two mounts, and two chariot teams."

sentences.zh: 皇帝即不欲匈奴近塞，則且詔吏民遠捨。

translations.literal: " "If the emperor does not wish the Xiongnu near the barrier, then let him order clerks and people to move their dwellings far away.
translations.idiomatic: " If you do not want us near the wall, order your people to pull back their farms."

sentences.zh: 使者至，即遣之。

translations.literal: " "When the envoy arrives, send him off at once."
translations.idiomatic: " Dispatch our messenger the moment he arrives."

sentences.zh: 」六月中，來至新望之地。

translations.literal: " In the sixth month they came to the place of Xinwang.
translations.idiomatic: They reached Xinwang in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 書至，漢議擊與和親孰便，公卿皆曰：「單于新破月氏，乘勝，不可擊也。

translations.literal: " When the letter arrived, Han debated whether striking or marriage alliance was more advantageous; the dukes and ministers all said: "The chanyu has newly broken the Yuezhi and rides victory—he cannot be struck.
translations.idiomatic: The court debated war versus peace; the ministers said Modu was flush from crushing the Yuezhi and should not be attacked.

sentences.zh: 且得匈奴地，澤鹵非可居也，和親甚便。

translations.literal: " "Moreover, even if we took Xiongnu land, the marshes and brine are not habitable—marriage alliance is very advantageous."
translations.idiomatic: " Their deserts are salt and useless; heqin is cheaper than blood."

sentences.zh: 」漢許之。

translations.literal: " Han granted it.
translations.idiomatic: " Wen agreed."

sentences.zh: 孝文前六年，遺匈奴書曰：「皇帝敬問匈奴大單于無恙。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Emperor Wen's Former Yuan reign, Han sent a letter to the Xiongnu saying: "The emperor respectfully asks whether the great chanyu of the Xiongnu is without illness.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of Emperor Wen's early reign era he wrote: "The emperor greets the great chanyu in health."

sentences.zh: 使系虖淺遺朕書，雲『愿寢兵休士，除前事，復故約，以安邊民，世世平樂』，朕甚嘉之。

translations.literal: " "You had Gentleman Xihujian present Us a letter saying, 'We wish to rest weapons and spare soldiers, clear away former affairs, restore the old covenant, secure the border people, generation on generation in peace and joy'—We greatly praise this.
translations.idiomatic: " Your envoy Xihujian brought word that you wished to disarm, forget old quarrels, renew the treaty, and let frontier folk live in peace—we applaud that."

sentences.zh: 此古聖王之志也。

translations.literal: This is the intent of the ancient sage-kings.
translations.idiomatic: That is the mind of the ancient sage-kings.

sentences.zh: 漢與匈奴約為兄弟，所以遺單于甚厚。

translations.literal: Han and the Xiongnu covenanted as brothers, for which reason the gifts sent to the chanyu have been very generous.
translations.idiomatic: Han and the Xiongnu swore brotherhood, and the bride-gifts to the court at the steppe have always been rich.

sentences.zh: 背約離兄弟之親者，常在匈奴。

translations.literal: Those who betrayed the covenant and sundered brotherly closeness have usually been on the Xiongnu side.
translations.idiomatic: Yet it is usually the Xiongnu who tear up the pact and the kinship.

sentences.zh: 然右賢王事已在赦前，勿深誅。

translations.literal: However the right Wise King's affair is already before the amnesty—do not punish deeply.
translations.idiomatic: The right Wise King's raid predates your amnesty; let there be no harsh reckoning.

sentences.zh: 單于若稱書意，明告諸吏，使無負約，有信，敬如單于書。

translations.literal: If the chanyu accords with the letter's intent, clearly instruct your officers so they do not betray the covenant, have good faith, and We will respect it as in the chanyu's letter.
translations.idiomatic: If you mean what you wrote, drill your officers not to break faith again, and we will answer in kind.

sentences.zh: 使者言單于自將並國有功，甚苦兵事。

translations.literal: The envoy said the chanyu himself had led the annexation of states with merit and greatly suffered the hardships of war.
translations.idiomatic: Your envoy adds that you personally welded the tribes by arms and are weary of campaigning.

sentences.zh: 服繡袷綺衣、長襦、錦袍各一，比疏一，黃金飭具帶一，黃金犀毘一，繡十匹，錦二十匹，赤綈、綠繒各四十匹，使中大夫意、謁者令肩遺單于。」

translations.literal: Let him wear one embroidered lined brocade robe, one long jacket, and one brocade gown each; one comb; one gold-inlaid belt set; one gold-inlaid rhinoceros-horn belt hook; ten bolts of embroidery, twenty of brocade, forty each of red silk and green fine silk, sending Palace Grandee Yi and Usher-in-Chief Jian to present them to the chanyu."
translations.idiomatic: We therefore send a lined brocade coat, a long robe, a brocade surcoat, a comb, a gold-mounted belt set, a gold rhinoceros belt hook, ten rolls of figured silk, twenty of brocade, and forty each of crimson and green silk, by Palace Grandee Yi and Usher-in-Chief Jian, as gifts to you."

sentences.zh: 後頃之，冒頓死，子稽粥立，號曰老上單于。

translations.literal: Not long afterward Modu died; his son Jiyu succeeded, titled the Old Superior Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Modu soon died; his son Jiyu took the throne as the "Old Superior" chanyu.

sentences.zh: 老上稽粥單于初立，文帝復遣宗人女翁主為單于閼氏，使宦者燕人中行說傅翁主。

translations.literal: When Old Superior Jiyu Chanyu had just been established, Emperor Wen again sent a royal clanswoman, a princess, as the chanyu's Yan wife, ordering the eunuch Yan man Zhonghang Yue to tutor the princess.
translations.idiomatic: At his accession Wen sent another imperial princess as bride and assigned the Yan eunuch Zhonghang Yue to attend her.

sentences.zh: 說不欲行，漢強使之。

translations.literal: Yue did not wish to go; Han forced him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhonghang Yue refused the mission until the court compelled him.

sentences.zh: 說曰：「必我也，為漢患者」。

translations.literal: Yue said: "If it must be me, I will be trouble for Han."
translations.idiomatic: He swore, "Send me, and I will be Han's curse."

sentences.zh: 中行說既至，因降單于，單于愛幸之。

translations.literal: After Zhonghang Yue arrived, he therefore surrendered to the chanyu, and the chanyu loved and favored him.
translations.idiomatic: Once north of the wall he defected to the chanyu, who took him as a favorite adviser.

sentences.zh: 初，單于好漢繒絮食物，中行說曰：「匈奴人眾不能當漢之一郡，然所以強之者，以衣食異，無仰於漢。

translations.literal: Earlier the chanyu loved Han floss silks and food; Zhonghang Yue said: "The Xiongnu people cannot match one Han commandery, yet the reason they are strong is that dress and food differ and they do not look up to Han.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu doted on Chinese silks and delicacies until Zhonghang Yue warned him: "Your herdsmen are fewer than one Han county, yet you stay free because you live apart from Han goods."

sentences.zh: 今單于變俗好漢物，漢物不過什二，則匈奴盡歸於漢矣。

translations.literal: " "Now if the chanyu changes custom and loves Han goods, when Han goods do not reach two parts in ten, the Xiongnu will wholly belong to Han.
translations.idiomatic: " If you grow fond of Han luxuries and they stop coming, two men in ten will desert you for the south."

sentences.zh: 其得漢絮繒，以馳草棘中，衣褲皆裂弊，以視不如旃裘堅善也；

translations.literal: " "When they obtain Han floss and silk, riding through brambles and thorns, trousers and coats all tear and wear out, showing they are not as firm and good as felt and fur;
translations.idiomatic: " Your riders tear Han gauze to shreds in the brush—it is weaker than felt."

sentences.zh: 得漢食物皆去之，以視不如重酪之便美也。

translations.literal: " "When they obtain Han food they all cast it away, showing it is not as convenient and tasty as heavy kumiss."
translations.idiomatic: " Han rations spoil on the trail beside your kumiss."

sentences.zh: 」於是說教單于左右疏記，以計識其人眾畜牧。

translations.literal: " Thereupon Yue taught the chanyu's attendants rough accounting to reckon people and herds.
translations.idiomatic: He then taught the court clerks tallies and counts for men and beasts.

sentences.zh: 漢遺單于書，以尺一牘，辭曰「皇帝敬問匈奴大單于無恙」，所以遺物及言語云云。

translations.literal: When Han sent a letter to the chanyu, it used a one-foot wooden slip, the phrasing saying "The emperor respectfully asks whether the great chanyu of the Xiongnu is without illness," and so on for the gifts sent and the words.
translations.idiomatic: Han used a one-foot tablet opening with the polite formula and listing tribute.

sentences.zh: 中行說令單于以尺二寸牘，及印封皆令廣長大，倨驁其辭曰「天地所生、日月所置匈奴大單于，敬問漢皇帝無恙」，所以遺物言語亦云雲。

translations.literal: Zhonghang Yue ordered the chanyu to use a one-foot-two slip, and for the seal and closure to be made broad and long, arrogantly wording it "The great chanyu of the Xiongnu begotten by heaven and earth, established by sun and moon, respectfully asks whether the Han emperor is without illness," and so on for the gifts sent and words.
translations.idiomatic: Zhonghang Yue had Modu reply on a longer tablet with an outsized seal, styling him heaven-born rival to the Han emperor.

sentences.zh: 漢使或言匈奴俗賤老，中行說窮漢使曰：「而漢俗屯戍從軍當發者，其親豈不自奪溫厚肥美繼送飲食行者乎？

translations.literal: When Han envoys said the Xiongnu custom slighted the old, Zhonghang Yue pressed the Han envoys: "In Han custom, when those bound for garrison duty or military service are about to depart, do not their kin seize from them the warm, thick, fat, fine provisions sent along for the traveler's food?"
translations.idiomatic: When Han men sneered at Xiongnu disrespect for elders, Zhonghang Yue snapped back: "Do not Han parents strip their larder to pack a soldier's kit?"

sentences.zh: 」漢使曰：「然。

translations.literal: " The Han envoy said: "Yes."
translations.idiomatic: " The envoys admitted it."

sentences.zh: 」說曰：「匈奴明以攻戰為事，老弱不能鬥，故以其肥美飲食壯健以自衛，如此父子各得相保，何以言匈奴輕老也？

translations.literal: " Yue said: "The Xiongnu plainly make attack and war their business; the old and weak cannot fight, therefore they give them the fat, fine food and drink of the strong and hale to guard themselves—thus father and son each can protect the other—how can you say the Xiongnu slight the old?"
translations.idiomatic: " We feed warriors first so families survive raids—that is filial piety in felt tents, not contempt."

sentences.zh: 」漢使曰：「匈奴父子同穹廬臥。

translations.literal: " The Han envoy said: "Xiongnu father and son sleep in the same round tent.
translations.idiomatic: " But," said the Han men, "you sleep father and son under one roof."

sentences.zh: 父死，妻其後母；

translations.literal: " "When the father dies, they marry his junior wife after him;
translations.idiomatic: " A son inherits his father's junior wives."

sentences.zh: 兄弟死，盡妻其妻。

translations.literal: " "when brothers die, they all take their wives and marry them."
translations.idiomatic: " Brothers marry each other's widows."

sentences.zh: 無冠帶之節、闕庭之禮。

translations.literal: " "They lack the restraint of caps and sashes and the rites of the court."
translations.idiomatic: " You have no caps, no court ritual."

sentences.zh: 」中行說曰：「匈奴之俗，食畜肉，飲其汁，衣其皮；

translations.literal: " Zhonghang Yue said: "The Xiongnu custom eats herd flesh, drinks their juices, wears their skins;
translations.idiomatic: " We eat meat, drink milk, wear hides."

sentences.zh: 畜食草飲水，隨時轉移。

translations.literal: " "The herds eat grass and drink water, shifting with the seasons.
translations.idiomatic: " Our herds move with the grass."

sentences.zh: 故其急則人習騎射，寬則人樂無事。

translations.literal: " "Therefore in haste the people practice riding and shooting; in ease the people delight in having nothing to do.
translations.idiomatic: " In crisis every man is a horse-archer; in peace we rest."

sentences.zh: 約束徑，易行；

translations.literal: " "Their restraints are direct and easy to practice;
translations.idiomatic: " Our laws are few and swift."

sentences.zh: 君臣簡，可久。

translations.literal: " "lord and minister are simple and can long endure.
translations.idiomatic: " Ruler and ruled stay lean and last."

sentences.zh: 一國之政猶一體也。

translations.literal: " "The government of one state is like one body."
translations.idiomatic: " The realm moves as one body."

sentences.zh: 父兄死，則妻其妻，惡種姓之失也。

translations.literal: " "When fathers and elder brothers die, then they marry their wives—it is loathing to lose the seed surnames.
translations.idiomatic: " Levirate marriage keeps bloodlines from dying out."

sentences.zh: 故匈奴雖亂，必立宗種。

translations.literal: " "Therefore although the Xiongnu are in disorder, they invariably establish the ancestral seed.
translations.idiomatic: " Even in civil strife a kinsman of the royal line always rises."

sentences.zh: 今中國雖陽不取其父兄之妻，親屬益疏則相殺，至到易姓，皆從此類也。

translations.literal: " "Now although China outwardly does not take its fathers' and elder brothers' wives, when kin grow distant they kill one another, even to changing surnames—all from this sort of thing."
translations.idiomatic: " Han forbids levirate, yet kin grow cold and kill each other until names change—who is more humane?"

sentences.zh: 且禮義之弊，上下交怨，而室屋之極，生力屈焉。

translations.literal: " "Moreover where rites and duty are flawed, high and low exchange resentments, and where house building reaches its extreme, the strength of life is bent.
translations.idiomatic: " Your ritual maze breeds hatred from roof to cellar."

sentences.zh: 夫力耕桑以求衣食，築城郭以自備，故其民急則不習戰攻，緩則罷於作業，嗟土室之人，顧無喋喋占占，冠固何當！

translations.literal: " "They force plowing and mulberry to seek clothing and food, build walls to guard themselves, therefore their people in haste do not practice war and attack, in ease are worn out by labor—alas, men of earthen chambers, look not to nag and chirp; what do caps and sashes really avail!"
translations.idiomatic: " You toil at farms and walls until you cannot fight—mud-house pedants, spare us the sermon on hats and sashes!"

sentences.zh: 」自是之後，漢使欲辯論者，中行說輒曰：「漢使毋多言，顧漢所輸匈奴繒絮米蘖，令其量中，必善美而已，何以言為乎？

translations.literal: " From this on, whenever Han envoys wished to debate, Zhonghang Yue always said: "Han envoys speak no more—only see that the floss silk, rice, and maltose Han conveys to the Xiongnu meets measure and must be good and fine; what need of words?
translations.idiomatic: After that any Han envoy who argued met the reply: "Check the quality of the silk and grain you bring—talk is useless."

sentences.zh: 且所給備善則已，不備善而苦惡，則候秋孰，以騎馳蹂乃稼穡也。

translations.literal: " "Moreover if what is supplied is complete and good, then fine; if not complete and good but clearly vile, then watch for the autumn ripening—We will ride and trample your crops."
translations.idiomatic: " Send shoddy goods and we will wait for harvest season to ride down your fields."

sentences.zh: 」日夜教單于候利害處。

translations.literal: " "Day and night he taught the chanyu to watch advantageous and harmful places."
translations.idiomatic: He drilled the chanyu night and day on where to strike Han.

sentences.zh: 孝文十四年，匈奴單于十四萬騎入朝那蕭關，殺北地都尉卬，虜人民畜產甚多，遂至彭陽。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year of Emperor Wen, the Xiongnu chanyu with a hundred forty thousand riders entered Zhaona and Xiaoguan, killed the Beidi commandant Ang, captured very many people and livestock, and reached Pengyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Wen's fourteenth year the chanyu poured a hundred forty thousand horsemen through Zhaona and Xiaoguan, slew the Beidi commandant Ang, swept up people and herds, and pushed to Pengyang.

sentences.zh: 使騎兵入燒回中宮，候騎至雍甘泉。

translations.literal: He sent cavalry in to burn the Huizhong palace; mounted scouts reached Yong and Ganquan.
translations.idiomatic: His raiders torched the Huizhong lodge and patrols probed toward Yong and Ganquan.

sentences.zh: 於是文帝以中尉周捨、郎中令張武為將軍，發車千乘，十萬騎，軍長安旁以備胡寇。

translations.literal: Thereupon Emperor Wen made Commandant of the capital Zhou She and Prefect of the Gentlemen of the Palace Zhang Wu generals, raised a thousand chariots and a hundred thousand riders, and encamped beside Chang'an to guard against Hu raiders.
translations.idiomatic: Wen mobilized a thousand chariots and a hundred thousand horse under Zhou She and Zhang Wu to ring the capital.

sentences.zh: 而拜昌侯盧卿為上郡將軍，甯侯魏脩為北地將軍，隆慮侯周灶為隴西將軍，東陽侯張相如為大將軍，成侯董赤為將軍，大發車騎往擊胡。

translations.literal: He appointed Marquis of Chang Lu Qing as general of Shang commandery, Marquis of Ning Wei Xiu as general of Beidi, Marquis of Longlü Zhou Zao as general of Longxi, Marquis of Dongyang Zhang Xiangru as grand general, Marquis of Cheng Dong Chi as general, and greatly raised chariots and horse to go strike the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: He named Lu Qing, Wei Xiu, Zhou Zao, Zhang Xiangru, and Dong Chi to lead the relief columns north.

sentences.zh: 單于留塞內月餘，漢逐出塞即還，不能有所殺。

translations.literal: The chanyu remained inside the barrier over a month; Han drove him out of the barrier and he returned, unable to kill anyone in particular.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu stayed inside the wall a month; Han chased him out without a decisive kill.

sentences.zh: 匈奴日以驕，歲入邊，殺略人民甚眾，雲中、遼東最甚，郡萬餘人。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu grew prouder by the day, yearly entering the border, killing and plundering very many people; Yunzhong and Liaodong were worst, counties losing more than ten thousand each.
translations.idiomatic: Year after year the raids swelled; Yunzhong and Liaodong lost ten thousand souls apiece in a season.

sentences.zh: 漢甚患之，乃使使遺匈奴書，單于亦使當戶報謝，復言和親事。

translations.literal: Han greatly worried over it, therefore sent envoys presenting a letter to the Xiongnu, and the chanyu also sent a household head to answer with thanks, again speaking of marriage alliance matters.
translations.idiomatic: Wen sent another letter; the chanyu answered through a household chief and reopened peace talks.

sentences.zh: 孝文後二年，使使遺匈奴書曰：「皇帝敬問匈奴大單于無恙。

translations.literal: In the second year of Emperor Wen's Later Yuan reign, he sent envoys presenting a letter to the Xiongnu saying: "The emperor respectfully asks whether the great chanyu of the Xiongnu is without illness.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Wen's Later Yuan era he wrote again: "The emperor greets the great chanyu."

sentences.zh: 使當戶且渠雕渠難、郎中韓遼遺朕馬二匹，已至，敬愛。

translations.literal: " "Household head and qúqú Diaoqu Nan and Gentleman of the Palace Han Liao presented Us two horses; they have arrived—We respect and cherish them.
translations.idiomatic: " Your envoys Diaoqu Nan and Han Liao brought two horses; we received them with thanks."

sentences.zh: 先帝制，長城以北引弓之國受令單于，長城以內冠帶之室朕亦制之，使萬民耕織，射獵衣食，父子毋離，臣主相安，俱無暴虐。

translations.literal: " "The former emperor's institutions: north of the Great Wall the bow-drawing states take orders from the chanyu; south of the Great Wall the cap-and-sash households We also rule, letting the myriad people plow and weave, hunt and shoot for clothing and food, father and son not parted, lord and minister mutually secure, both without violence.
translations.idiomatic: " Our fathers drew the line: beyond the wall you command the archers; within it we rule the silken towns so farmers and hunters may live without fear."

sentences.zh: 今聞渫惡民貪降其趨，背義絕約，忘萬民之命，離兩主之歡，然其事已在前矣。

translations.literal: " "Now We hear wicked people, greedy for surrender's gain, have turned their steps, betrayed duty, broken the covenant, forgotten the myriad people's lives, sundered the joy of the two lords—yet the affair is already in the past.
translations.idiomatic: " Rebels once lured us both into quarrel; that is behind us."

sentences.zh: 書云『二國已和親，兩主歡說，寢兵休卒養馬，世世昌樂，翕然更始』，朕甚嘉之。

translations.literal: " "The letter says, 'The two states have already made marriage alliance; the two lords rejoice, rest weapons and spare soldiers, fatten horses, generation on generation prosperous and joyful, harmoniously making a new beginning'—We greatly praise this.
translations.idiomatic: " Your pledge of disarmament and shared prosperity wins our praise."

sentences.zh: 聖者日新，改作更始，使老者得息，幼者得長，各保其首領，而終其天年。

translations.literal: " "Sages renew daily, reforming and making a new beginning, letting the old rest and the young grow long, each guarding his head, and finishing his natural years.
translations.idiomatic: " The wise renew the world so graybeards rest and children mature under one peaceful sky."

sentences.zh: 朕與單於俱由此道，順天恤民，世世相傳，施之無窮，天下莫不鹹便。

translations.literal: " "We and the Chanyu shall both follow this way, obeying heaven and pitying the people, handing down generation on generation, applying it without end, so that none under heaven is not thereby benefited.
translations.idiomatic: " You and we will walk this road forever, to the good of all under heaven."

sentences.zh: 漢與匈奴鄰敵之國，匈奴處北地，寒，殺氣早降，故詔吏遺單于秫蘖金帛綿絮它物歲有數。

translations.literal: " "Han and the Xiongnu are neighboring rival states; the Xiongnu dwell in the northern land, cold, killing qi descending early, therefore We order clerks yearly to send the chanyu malt, gold, silk, floss, and other goods in set amounts.
translations.idiomatic: " The steppe is bitter cold, so yearly gifts of malt, gold, silk, and floss will continue as of old."

sentences.zh: 今天下大安，萬民熙熙，獨朕與單于為之父母。

translations.literal: " "Now the realm is greatly at peace and the myriad people joyful; only We and the chanyu are their father and mother.
translations.idiomatic: " The empire is calm; we two thrones are its parents."

sentences.zh: 朕追念前事，薄物細故，謀臣計失，皆不足以離昆弟之歡。

translations.literal: " "We recall former affairs—trifling goods and petty causes, counsels' plans mistaken—none are enough to sunder sworn brothers' joy.
translations.idiomatic: " Past slights and bad advice shall not divide us again."

sentences.zh: 朕聞天不頗覆，地不偏載。

translations.literal: " "We have heard heaven does not lean its cover, earth does not load one-sidedly.
translations.idiomatic: " Heaven roofs all impartially; earth bears all without favor."

sentences.zh: 朕與單于皆捐細故，俱蹈大道，墮壞前惡，以圖長久，使兩國之民若一家子。

translations.literal: " "We and the chanyu both cast aside petty causes, together tread the great way, cast down former evils, to plan long endurance, making the people of the two states like children of one family.
translations.idiomatic: " Let us bury old grudges and make our folk one household."

sentences.zh: 元元萬民，下及魚鱉，上及飛鳥，跂行喙息蠕動之類，莫不就安利，避危殆。
