translations.literal: " "The teeming myriad people, down to fish and turtles, up to flying birds, all who walk on heels, breathe through beaks, or wriggle crawling, none do not approach safety and profit and avoid peril.
translations.idiomatic: " From fish to fowl, every creature seeks ease and flees harm."

sentences.zh: 故來者不止，天之道也。

translations.literal: " "Therefore those who come do not stop—this is heaven's way.
translations.idiomatic: " So refugees keep crossing the wall—such is nature."

sentences.zh: 俱去前事，朕釋逃虜民，單于毋言章尼等。

translations.literal: " "Let us both put away former affairs: We release escaped captives and people; let the chanyu not speak of Zhang Ni and the like.
translations.idiomatic: " We forgive border fugitives; forget the names of minor defectors."

sentences.zh: 朕聞古之帝王，約分明而不食言。

translations.literal: " "We have heard that ancient emperors and kings made covenants clear and did not eat their words.
translations.idiomatic: " The ancients kept clear treaties and never broke their word."

sentences.zh: 單于留志，天下大安，和親之後，漢過不先。

translations.literal: " "Chanyu, keep this in mind: when the realm is greatly at peace, after marriage alliance, Han will not be first to fault.
translations.idiomatic: " After heqin, Han will not strike the first blow."

sentences.zh: 單于其察之。」

translations.literal: " "Chanyu, examine this."
translations.idiomatic: " Think on these terms."

sentences.zh: 單于既約和親，於是制詔御史：「匈奴大單于遺朕書，和親已定，亡人不足以益眾廣地，匈奴無入塞，漢無出塞，犬己今約者殺之，可以久親，後無咎，俱便。

translations.literal: After the chanyu had covenanted marriage alliance, thereupon an edict ordered the imperial clerk: "The great chanyu of the Xiongnu has sent Us a letter; marriage alliance is already settled. Fugitives are not enough to increase hosts or widen territory; the Xiongnu shall not enter the barrier, Han shall not go out of the barrier; whoever from now on violates the covenant shall be put to death—thus kinship can long endure, afterward without blame, both benefited.
translations.idiomatic: When the treaty was sealed Wen proclaimed: "Fugitives win you no land; neither side crosses the wall; whoever breaks this pact hereafter dies—so peace may last."

sentences.zh: 朕已許。

translations.literal: " We have granted it.
translations.idiomatic: " We consent."

sentences.zh: 其佈告天下，使明知之。」

translations.literal: " "Proclaim it to the realm, making all clearly know it."
translations.idiomatic: " Publish this to the empire."

sentences.zh: 後四年，老上單于死，子軍臣單于立，而中行說復事之。

translations.literal: Four years later Old Superior Chanyu died; his son Jun Chen Chanyu was established, and Zhonghang Yue again served him.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later the Old Superior died; his son Junchen succeeded, and Zhonghang Yue stayed at his ear.

sentences.zh: 漢復與匈奴和親。

translations.literal: Han again made marriage alliance with the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Han renewed heqin.

sentences.zh: 軍臣單于立歲餘，匈奴復絕和親，大入上郡、雲中各三萬騎，所殺略甚眾。

translations.literal: More than a year after Jun Chen Chanyu was established, the Xiongnu again broke marriage alliance, greatly entering Shang commandery and Yunzhong with thirty thousand riders each, killing and plundering very many.
translations.idiomatic: Within a year Junchen scrapped the treaty and sent thirty thousand horse into both Shang and Yunzhong, slaughtering and looting.

sentences.zh: 於是漢使三將軍軍屯北地，代屯句注，趙屯飛狐口，緣邊亦各堅守以備胡寇。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han stationed three generals' armies at Beidi, Dai at Gouzhu, and Zhao at Feihu Pass, while along the border each also held fast to prepare against Hu raiders.
translations.idiomatic: Han planted three armies at Beidi, Dai, and Feihu while every sector manned the wall.

sentences.zh: 又置三將軍，軍長安西細柳、渭北棘門、霸上以備胡。

translations.literal: He also set three generals, encamping west of Chang'an at Xiliu, north of the Wei at Jimen, and at Bashang to guard against the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Three more camps went up at Xiliu, Jimen, and Bashang outside the capital.

sentences.zh: 胡騎入代句注邊，烽火通於甘泉、長安。

translations.literal: Hu riders entered Dai at the Gouzhu border; beacon fires reached Ganquan and Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Signal fires from the Gouzhu sector lit the sky as far as Ganquan and Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 數月，漢兵至邊，匈奴亦遠塞，漢兵亦罷。

translations.literal: Several months later Han troops reached the border; the Xiongnu also went far from the barrier, and Han troops also stood down.
translations.idiomatic: Months later Han reached the line; the Xiongnu had already slipped away, and both sides demobilized.

sentences.zh: 後歲餘，文帝崩，景帝立，而趙王遂乃陰使於匈奴。

translations.literal: More than a year later Emperor Wen died; Emperor Jing was established, and the king of Zhao, Sui, secretly sent to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: A year after Wen's death Jing took the throne while Zhao's king Sui courted the Xiongnu in secret.

sentences.zh: 吳、楚反，欲與趙合謀入邊。

translations.literal: Wu and Chu rebelled, wishing to join Zhao in plotting to enter the border.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu and Chu rose, they meant to pull Zhao and the Xiongnu into the war.

sentences.zh: 漢圍破趙，匈奴亦止。

translations.literal: Han besieged and broke Zhao, and the Xiongnu also stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Han crushed Zhao and the nomad threat stalled.

sentences.zh: 自是後，景帝復與匈奴和親，通關市，給遺單于，遣翁主如故約。

translations.literal: From this on, Jing again made marriage alliance with the Xiongnu, opened barrier markets, supplied gifts to the chanyu, and sent a princess as before in the covenant.
translations.idiomatic: Jing restored heqin, opened border markets, paid tribute, and sent an imperial daughter as before.

sentences.zh: 終景帝世，時時小入盜邊，無大寇。

translations.literal: Through Emperor Jing's reign they sometimes made small entries to pillage the border, without great incursions.
translations.idiomatic: For the rest of Jing's reign there were only pinprick raids, no great invasions.

sentences.zh: 武帝即位，明和親約束，厚遇關市，饒給之。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu took the throne, he clarified marriage alliance covenants, treated barrier markets generously, and supplied them richly.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu at first honored the old treaty, fattened the border trade, and showered gifts on the court.

sentences.zh: 匈奴自單于以下皆親漢，往來長城下。

translations.literal: From the chanyu down the Xiongnu were all close to Han, coming and going below the Great Wall.
translations.idiomatic: From the chanyu downward the tribes courted Han and traded along the wall.

sentences.zh: 漢使馬邑人聶翁壹間闌出物與匈奴交易，陽為賣馬邑城以誘單于。

translations.literal: Han had Nie Yi of Mayi smuggle goods out at the barrier to trade with the Xiongnu, falsely offering to sell the city of Mayi to lure the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: A Mayi merchant named Nie Yi slipped through the gate to deal with the Xiongnu, pretending he would sell them the walled town of Mayi.

sentences.zh: 單于信之，而貪馬邑財物，乃以十萬騎入武州塞。

translations.literal: The chanyu believed it and, greedy for Mayi's goods, thereupon entered with a hundred thousand riders at the Wuzhou barrier.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu swallowed the bait and drove a hundred thousand riders through Wuzhou Pass.

sentences.zh: 漢伏兵三十餘萬馬邑旁，御史大夫韓安國為護軍將軍，護國將軍以伏單于。

translations.literal: Han ambushed more than three hundred thousand beside Mayi; Imperial Counsellor Han Anguo was colonel-protector of the army, and colonel-protector of the state commanded the generals who were to ambush the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Three hundred thousand men lay concealed near Mayi while Han Anguo, imperial counsellor, directed the generals assigned to spring the trap on the chanyu.

sentences.zh: 單于既入漢塞，未至馬邑百餘里，見畜布野而無人牧者，怪之，乃攻亭。

translations.literal: After the chanyu had entered the Han barrier, not yet a hundred li from Mayi, he saw livestock spread over the wilds with none herding them, thought it strange, and attacked a post station.
translations.idiomatic: Inside the wall he found herds untended a hundred li short of Mayi, smelled a rat, and seized a watch post.

sentences.zh: 時雁門尉史行徼，見寇，保此亭，單于得，欲刺之。

translations.literal: At that time a Yanmen post clerk was on patrol, saw the raiders, and held this post; the chanyu captured him and wished to execute him.
translations.idiomatic: A Yanmen clerk on rounds barricaded the tower until the Xiongnu took him and lifted a blade.

sentences.zh: 尉史知漢謀，乃下，具告單于。

translations.literal: The post clerk knew Han's plot, therefore came down and fully told the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: To save his neck he revealed the ambush plan.

sentences.zh: 單于大驚，曰：「吾固疑之。

translations.literal: The chanyu was greatly startled and said: "We indeed suspected it.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu cried, "I knew it smelled wrong."

sentences.zh: 」乃引兵還。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he led troops back.
translations.idiomatic: " He wheeled his host about."

sentences.zh: 出曰：「吾得尉史，天也。

translations.literal: Going out he said: "We have obtained the post clerk—heaven."
translations.idiomatic: At the border he shouted, "Heaven sent me that clerk."

sentences.zh: 」以尉史為天王。

translations.literal: " He made the post clerk "Heaven King."
translations.idiomatic: He named the clerk "King of Heaven."

sentences.zh: 漢兵約單于入馬邑而縱，單于不至，以故無所得。

translations.literal: Han troops had covenanted with the chanyu to enter Mayi and then loose them; the chanyu did not arrive, therefore nothing was obtained.
translations.idiomatic: The ambush never sprang because the chanyu never reached the kill zone.

sentences.zh: 將軍王恢部出代擊胡輜重，聞單于還，兵多，不敢出。

translations.literal: General Wang Hui's division went out from Dai to strike the Hu baggage train; hearing the chanyu had returned and the troops were many, he did not dare go out.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hui was to slash the wagon train from Dai but froze when he heard the main horde was returning.

sentences.zh: 漢以恢本建造兵謀而不進，誅恢。

translations.literal: Han because Hui had originally devised the military plot but did not advance, executed Hui.
translations.idiomatic: The court beheaded Wang Hui for drafting the scheme and then hanging back.

sentences.zh: 自是後，凶奴絕和親，攻當路塞，往往入盜於邊，不可勝數。

translations.literal: From this on, the Xiongnu broke marriage alliance, attacked the passes on the main roads, and often entered to pillage the border beyond counting.
translations.idiomatic: After Mayi the Xiongnu tore up heqin, stormed the frontier gates, and raided beyond count.

sentences.zh: 然匈奴貪，尚樂關市，嗜漢財物，漢亦通關市不絕以中之。

translations.literal: Yet the Xiongnu were greedy, still delighted with barrier markets and addicted to Han goods; Han also kept barrier markets unbroken to hook them.
translations.idiomatic: Still they loved the border bazaars and Han goods, and Han kept the markets open to string them along.

sentences.zh: 自馬邑軍後五歲之秋，漢使四將各萬騎擊胡關市下。

translations.literal: In the autumn five years after the Mayi army, Han sent four generals, each with ten thousand riders, to strike the Hu below the barrier markets.
translations.idiomatic: Five autumns after Mayi, Wu sent four columns of ten thousand horse to hit the nomads at the trading posts.

sentences.zh: 將軍衛青出上谷，至龍城，得胡首虜七百人。

translations.literal: General Wei Qing went out from Shanggu, reaching Dragon City, obtaining seven hundred Hu heads and captives.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Qing rode from Shanggu to Dragon City and took seven hundred heads.

sentences.zh: 公孫賀出雲中，無所得。

translations.literal: Gongsun He went out from Yunzhong and obtained nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun He left Yunzhong and found empty steppe.

sentences.zh: 公孫敖出代郡，為胡所敗七千。

translations.literal: Gongsun Ao went out from Dai commandery and was defeated by the Hu, losing seven thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Ao marched from Dai and lost seven thousand men to the nomads.

sentences.zh: 李廣出雁門，為胡所敗，匈奴生得廣，廣道亡歸。

translations.literal: Li Guang went out from Yanmen and was defeated by the Hu; the Xiongnu captured Guang alive, and Guang by stratagem fled back.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guang sortied from Yanmen, was taken alive, then broke out and rode home.

sentences.zh: 漢囚敖、廣，敖、廣贖為庶人。

translations.literal: Han imprisoned Ao and Guang; Ao and Guang ransomed themselves to become commoners.
translations.idiomatic: The court jailed both commanders until they bought their way back to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 其冬，匈奴數千人盜邊，漁陽尤甚。

translations.literal: That winter several thousand Xiongnu pillaged the border; Yuyang suffered worst.
translations.idiomatic: That winter thousands of horsemen raided the line; Yuyang took the brunt.

sentences.zh: 漢使將軍韓安國屯漁陽備胡。

translations.literal: Han sent General Han Anguo to encamp at Yuyang to guard against the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Han Anguo was posted to Yuyang to stiffen the defense.

sentences.zh: 其明年秋，匈奴二萬騎入漢，殺遼西太守，略二千餘人。

translations.literal: The next autumn twenty thousand Xiongnu riders entered Han, killed the Liaoxi grand administrator, and plundered more than two thousand people.
translations.idiomatic: Next autumn twenty thousand riders hit Liaoxi, killed its governor, and dragged off two thousand captives.

sentences.zh: 又敗漁陽太守軍千餘人，圍將軍安國。

translations.literal: They also defeated the Yuyang grand administrator's army of more than a thousand men and besieged General Anguo.
translations.idiomatic: They crushed a thousand men under Yuyang's governor and penned Han Anguo in.

sentences.zh: 安國時千餘騎亦且盡，會燕救之，至，匈奴乃去，又入雁門殺略千餘人。

translations.literal: Anguo at the time had not a thousand riders left and was nearly spent; it happened that Yan came to rescue him, and when they arrived the Xiongnu then departed, and they also entered Yanmen, killing and plundering more than a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Anguo had fewer than a thousand horse left when Yan reinforcements lifted the siege; the Xiongnu withdrew only to slash Yanmen for another thousand victims.

sentences.zh: 於是漢使將軍衛青將三萬騎出雁門，李息出代郡，擊胡，得首虜數千。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han sent General Wei Qing to lead thirty thousand riders out from Yanmen, Li Xi out from Dai commandery, to strike the Hu, obtaining several thousand heads and captives.
translations.idiomatic: Han answered with Wei Qing's thirty thousand from Yanmen and Li Xi from Dai, taking several thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 其明年，衛青復出雲中以西至隴西，擊胡之樓煩、白羊王子河南，得胡首虜數千，羊百餘萬。

translations.literal: The next year Wei Qing again went out from Yunzhong west to Longxi, attacked the Xiongnu Loufan and White Sheep princes south of the river, obtaining several thousand Hu heads and captives, and more than a million sheep.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Qing swept from Yunzhong to Longxi, broke the Loufan and White Sheep bands south of the Yellow River, counted thousands of kills, and drove off a million sheep.

sentences.zh: 於是漢遂取河南地，築朔方，復繕故秦時蒙恬所為塞，因河而為固。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han took the land south of the river, built Shuofang, repaired again the barrier Meng Tian had made in Qin times, and took the river as a bulwark.
translations.idiomatic: Han annexed the south-bank pasturelands, founded Shuofang commandery, rebuilt Meng Tian's Qin wall, and anchored the line on the river.

sentences.zh: 漢亦棄上谷之鬥辟縣造陽地以予胡。

translations.literal: Han also abandoned the crooked salient of Zaoyang county in Shanggu, ceding that ground to the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: To sweeten the deal Han yielded the Zaoyang salient in Shanggu.

sentences.zh: 是歲，元朔二年也。

translations.literal: This year was the second year of Yuanshuo.
translations.idiomatic: The calendar read Yuanshuo 2.

sentences.zh: 其後冬，軍臣單于死，其弟左右蠡王伊稚斜自立為單于，攻敗軍臣單于太子於單。

translations.literal: The following winter Chanyu Junchen died; his younger brother the left and right Guli king Yizhixie set himself up as chanyu, attacked and defeated crown prince Yudan of Chanyu Junchen.
translations.idiomatic: The next winter Junchen died; his brother Yizhixie, a Guli prince, seized the throne and drove off the heir Yudan.

sentences.zh: 於單亡降漢，漢封於單為陟安侯，數月死。

translations.literal: Yudan fled and surrendered to Han; Han enfeoffed Yudan as marquis of Zhi'an, and he died after several months.
translations.idiomatic: Yudan defected to Han, received the marquisate of Zhi'an, and died within months.

sentences.zh: 伊稚斜單于既立，其夏，匈奴數萬騎入代郡，殺太守共友，略千餘人。

translations.literal: After Yizhixie Chanyu was established, that summer several tens of thousands of Xiongnu riders entered Dai commandery, killed Grand Administrator Gong You, and plundered more than a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Yizhixie's first summer brought tens of thousands of riders through Dai; Governor Gong You died and a thousand people vanished into slavery.

sentences.zh: 秋，又入雁門，殺略千餘人。

translations.literal: In autumn they again entered Yanmen, killing and plundering more than a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: That fall they repeated the performance at Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 其明年，又入代郡、定襄、上郡，各三萬騎，殺略數千人。

translations.literal: The next year they again entered Dai commandery, Dingxiang, and Shang commandery, thirty thousand riders each, killing and plundering several thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The year after they hit Dai, Dingxiang, and Shang simultaneously, thirty thousand horse per sector and thousands of casualties.

sentences.zh: 匈奴右賢王怨漢奪之河南地而築朔方，數寇盜邊，及入河南，侵擾朔方，殺略吏民甚眾。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu right Wise King resented Han for seizing from him the land south of the river and building Shuofang, repeatedly raiding the border; entering south of the river he harassed Shuofang, killing and plundering very many clerks and commoners.
translations.idiomatic: The right Wise King fumed over the lost south bank and new Shuofang fort; he plundered inside the loop and ravaged Shuofang's people.

sentences.zh: 其明年春，漢遣衛青將六將軍十餘萬人出朔方高闕。

translations.literal: The next spring Han dispatched Wei Qing to lead six generals and more than a hundred thousand men out from Shuofang toward Gaoque.
translations.idiomatic: Next spring Wei Qing took six generals and over a hundred thousand men out of Shuofang toward Gaoque.

sentences.zh: 右賢王以為漢兵不能至，飲酒醉。

translations.literal: The right Wise King thought Han troops could not reach him and got drunk on wine.
translations.idiomatic: The right Wise King assumed Han could not cross the waste and drank himself senseless.

sentences.zh: 漢兵出塞六七百里，夜圍右賢王。

translations.literal: Han troops went out of the barrier six or seven hundred li and by night surrounded the right Wise King.
translations.idiomatic: Qing's columns marched six or seven hundred li beyond the wall and ringed him by night.

sentences.zh: 右賢王大驚，脫身逃走，精騎往往隨後去。

translations.literal: The right Wise King was greatly alarmed, broke away and fled; crack riders often followed him and left.
translations.idiomatic: He bolted in his nightclothes while picked bodyguards galloped after him.

sentences.zh: 漢將軍得右賢王人眾男女萬五千人，裨小王十餘人。

translations.literal: The Han generals took fifteen thousand men and women of the right Wise King's people and more than ten assistant lesser kings.
translations.idiomatic: Han bagged fifteen thousand captives and a dozen petty kings of his clan.

sentences.zh: 其秋，匈奴萬騎入代郡，殺都尉硃央，略千餘人。

translations.literal: That autumn ten thousand Xiongnu riders entered Dai commandery, killed Commandant Zhu Yang, and plundered more than a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn ten thousand riders struck Dai again, killed Commandant Zhu Yang, and lifted another thousand souls.

sentences.zh: 其明年春，漢復遣大將軍衛青將六將軍，十餘萬騎，仍再出定襄數百里擊匈奴，得首虜前後萬九千餘級，而漢亦亡兩將軍，三千餘騎。

translations.literal: The next spring Han again dispatched Grand General Wei Qing with six generals and more than a hundred thousand riders, again going out from Dingxiang several hundred li to strike the Xiongnu, obtaining before and after more than nineteen thousand heads and ranks; Han also lost two generals and more than three thousand riders.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring the grand general repeated the formula from Dingxiang, claiming nineteen thousand heads for the loss of two generals and three thousand Han cavalry.

sentences.zh: 右將軍建得以身脫，而前將軍翕侯趙信兵不利，降匈奴。

translations.literal: General of the Right Su Jian escaped with his life, but General of the Front Marquis Xi Zhao Xin's troops fared badly and he surrendered to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Su Jian limped home; Zhao Xin, Marquis of Xi, went over to the enemy.

sentences.zh: 趙信者，故胡小王，降漢，漢封為翕侯，以前將軍與右將軍並軍，介獨遇單于兵，故盡沒。

translations.literal: Zhao Xin had been a lesser Hu king who surrendered to Han; Han enfeoffed him Marquis of Xi, and as general of the front with the general of the right in combined army, his detachment alone met the chanyu's troops, therefore it was entirely destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xin had once been a steppe chieftain who came over for a Han marquisate; his column alone met the chanyu and was wiped out.

sentences.zh: 單于既得翕侯，以為自次王，用其姊妻之，與謀漢。

translations.literal: After the chanyu obtained Marquis of Xi he made him secondary king by his own title, married him to his elder sister, and plotted Han with him.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu ennobled him as a "sub-king" below the throne, married him to a princess of the blood, and took his counsel against Han.

sentences.zh: 信教單于益北絕幕，以誘罷漢兵，徼極而取之，毋近塞。

translations.literal: Xin taught the chanyu to move further north beyond the desert to lure and exhaust Han troops, strike when they were spent at the limit, and not approach the barrier.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xin urged him to hug the northern desert, let Han exhaust its supply lines, then strike—never camp near the wall again.

sentences.zh: 單于從之。

translations.literal: The chanyu followed him.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu adopted the strategy.

sentences.zh: 其明年，胡數萬騎入上谷，殺數百人。

translations.literal: The next year several tens of thousands of Hu riders entered Shanggu and killed several hundred.
translations.idiomatic: The next year tens of thousands swept Shanggu for a few hundred heads.

sentences.zh: 明年春，漢使票騎將軍去病將萬騎出隴西，過焉耆山千餘里，得胡首虜八千餘級，得休屠王祭天金人。

translations.literal: The next spring Han sent General-in-Chief of Agile Cavalry Huo Qubing to lead ten thousand riders out from Longxi, passed Mount Yanqi more than a thousand li, and obtained more than eight thousand Hu heads and ranks, capturing the King of Xiutu's heaven-sacrificing gold images.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Qubing raced a thousand li beyond Longxi, stacked eight thousand Xiongnu dead, and seized the Xiutu king's golden heaven-god statues.

sentences.zh: 其夏，票騎將軍復與合騎侯數萬騎出隴西、北地二千里，過居延，攻祁連山，得胡首虜三萬餘級，裨小王以下十餘人。

translations.literal: That summer the agile cavalry general again with Marquis of Heqi several tens of thousands of riders went out from Longxi and Beidi two thousand li, passed Juyan, attacked the Qilian range, obtaining more than thirty thousand Hu heads and ranks, and more than ten men from assistant lesser kings downward.
translations.idiomatic: That summer he and the Heqi marquis took tens of thousands from Longxi and Beidi, crossed Juyan, scoured the Qilian slopes, and killed thirty thousand from petty kings downward.

sentences.zh: 是時，匈奴亦來入代郡、雁門，殺略數百人。

translations.literal: At that time the Xiongnu also came in to Dai commandery and Yanmen, killing and plundering several hundred.
translations.idiomatic: While Han struck west, smaller bands nipped Dai and Yanmen for hundreds more.

sentences.zh: 漢使博望侯及李將軍廣出右北平，擊匈奴左賢王。

translations.literal: Han sent Marquis of Bowang Zhang Qian and General Li Guang out of Youbeiping to strike the Xiongnu left Wise King.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qian and Li Guang hit Youbeiping against the left Wise King.

sentences.zh: 左賢王圍李廣，廣軍四千人死者過半，殺虜亦過當。

translations.literal: The left Wise King besieged Li Guang; of Guang's four thousand men more than half died, but kills and captures also exceeded the norm.
translations.idiomatic: The left Wise King nearly annihilated Li Guang's four thousand, though Guang still gave as good as he got.

sentences.zh: 會博望侯軍救至，李將軍得脫，盡亡其軍。

translations.literal: When Marquis of Bowang's army arrived to save them, General Li obtained escape but lost his entire command.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qian's relief let Li Guang escape; the division was gone.

sentences.zh: 合騎侯後票騎將軍期，及博望侯皆當死，贖為庶人。

translations.literal: Marquis of Heqi was late to the agile cavalry general's rendezvous, and Marquis of Bowang both deserved death, ransoming to commoners.
translations.idiomatic: The Heqi and Bowang marquises missed their deadlines, faced execution, and bought commoner status.

sentences.zh: 其秋，單于怒昆邪王、休屠王居西方為漢所殺虜數萬人，欲召誅之。

translations.literal: That autumn the chanyu was angry that Kings Hunye and Xiutu in the west had been killed and captured in the tens of thousands by Han, and wished to summon and execute them.
translations.idiomatic: That fall the chanyu blamed the western kings Hunye and Xiutu for tens of thousands lost and meant to execute them at court.

sentences.zh: 昆邪、休屠王恐，謀降漢，漢使票騎將軍迎之。

translations.literal: Kings Hunye and Xiutu feared and plotted to surrender to Han; Han sent the agile cavalry general to receive them.
translations.idiomatic: The two kings chose defection; Han sent Huo Qubing to escort them in.

sentences.zh: 昆邪王殺休屠王，並將其眾降漢，凡四萬餘人，號十萬。

translations.literal: King Hunye killed King Xiutu and combined leading their hosts in surrender to Han, in all more than forty thousand, claiming a hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Hunye murdered Xiutu, then led more than forty thousand followers—claiming a hundred thousand—across the line.

sentences.zh: 於是漢已得昆邪，則隴西、北地、河西益少胡寇，徙關東貧民處所奪匈奴河南地新秦中以實之，而減北地以西戍卒半。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han having obtained Hunye, in Longxi, Beidi, and west of the river there were gradually fewer Hu raids; they moved poor people from east of the passes to dwell in the new Qin region of the land south of the river taken from the Xiongnu to fill it, and halved the garrison troops west of Beidi.
translations.idiomatic: Their surrender emptied the western frontier of raiders; Han colonized the new "Qin" belt south of the river with eastern paupers and cut the western garrisons by half.

sentences.zh: 明年春，匈奴入右北平、定襄各數萬騎，殺略千餘人。

translations.literal: The next spring the Xiongnu entered Youbeiping and Dingxiang with several tens of thousands of riders each, killing and plundering more than a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring they lashed Youbeiping and Dingxiang again, several ten-thousand columns each.

sentences.zh: 其明年春，漢謀以為「翕侯信為單于計，居幕北，以為漢兵不能至」。

translations.literal: The next spring Han planned, thinking "Marquis of Xi Xin counseled the chanyu to dwell north of the desert, believing Han troops could not reach."
translations.idiomatic: The next spring the strategists recalled Zhao Xin's advice: hug the far desert beyond Han's reach.

sentences.zh: 乃粟馬，發十萬騎，私負從馬凡十四萬匹，糧重不與焉。

translations.literal: They fed the horses grain and raised a hundred thousand riders, privately following mounts totaling a hundred forty thousand, not counting baggage trains.
translations.idiomatic: Courts loaded grain into horses, mustered a hundred thousand riders and a hundred forty thousand remounts, baggage not counted.

sentences.zh: 令大將軍青、票騎將軍去病中分軍，大將軍出定襄，票騎將軍出代，鹹約絕幕擊匈奴。

translations.literal: They ordered Grand General Qing and General of Agile Cavalry Huo Qubing to split the army; the grand general went out from Dingxiang, the agile cavalry general out from Dai, both covenanted to cross the desert and strike the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Qing would spearhead from Dingxiang, Huo Qubing from Dai, both sworn to cross the Gobi and hunt the chanyu.

sentences.zh: 單于聞之，遠其輜重，以精兵待於幕北。

translations.literal: When the chanyu heard it, he moved his baggage train far away and with crack troops awaited them north of the desert.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu parked his wagons southward and waited north of the sand with veterans.

sentences.zh: 與漢大將軍接戰一日，會暮，大風起，漢兵縱左右翼圍單于。

translations.literal: He fought Han's grand general one day; at dusk a great wind rose, and Han troops loosed the left and right wings to surround the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: He traded blows with Wei Qing until dusk and a sandstorm, when Han's wings tried to close the ring.

sentences.zh: 單于自度戰不能與漢兵，遂獨與壯騎數百潰漢圍西北遁走。

translations.literal: The chanyu judged he could not match Han troops in battle, therefore alone with several hundred picked riders broke through Han's encirclement toward the northwest and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing the odds, he punched through the northwest face with a few hundred guards and ran.

sentences.zh: 漢兵夜追之不得，行捕斬首虜凡萬九千級，北至窴顏山趙信城而還。

translations.literal: Han troops pursued by night but could not catch him; in pursuit they captured and beheaded in all nineteen thousand ranks, reaching Mount Xieyan and Zhao Xin's fort before returning.
translations.idiomatic: Qing's pursuit missed the chanyu but picked up nineteen thousand heads on the way to Xieyan Mountain and Zhao Xin's old fort.

sentences.zh: 單于之走，其兵往往與漢軍相亂而隨單于。

translations.literal: In the chanyu's flight his troops were often tangled with Han armies while following the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Stragglers trying to follow their ruler stumbled into Han units.

sentences.zh: 單于久不與其大眾相得，右谷蠡王以為單于死，乃自立為單于。

translations.literal: The chanyu long did not rejoin his great host; the right Guli king thought the chanyu dead and set himself up as chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: When he failed to reappear, the right Guli king declared himself chanyu.

sentences.zh: 真單于復得其眾，右谷蠡乃去號，復其故位。

translations.literal: When the true chanyu regained his host, the right Guli king then dropped his title and resumed his former position.
translations.idiomatic: The real chanyu reclaimed his army and the Guli king meekly gave back the title.

sentences.zh: 票騎之出代二千餘里，與左王接戰，漢兵得胡首虜凡七萬餘人，左王將皆遁走。

translations.literal: The agile cavalry's sortie from Dai more than two thousand li, engaging the left kings in battle, Han troops obtained in all more than seventy thousand Hu heads and ranks, and the left kings' generals all fled.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Qubing drove two thousand li from Dai, shattered the eastern wing for seventy thousand counted dead, and sent its generals flying.

sentences.zh: 票騎封於狼居胥山，禪姑衍，臨翰海而還。

translations.literal: The agile cavalry performed the feng on Mount Langjuxu, the shan at Guyan, looked on the Han sea and returned.
translations.idiomatic: He capped the campaign with the feng sacrifice on Langjuxu, the shan at Guyan, and a sight of the northern sea.

sentences.zh: 是後，匈奴遠遁，而幕南無王庭。

translations.literal: After this the Xiongnu fled far away, and south of the desert there was no royal court.
translations.idiomatic: After that the court of the chanyu never again pitched south of the Gobi.

sentences.zh: 漢度河自朔方以西至令居，往往通渠置田官，吏卒五六萬人，稍蠶食，地接匈奴以北。

translations.literal: Han crossed the river from Shuofang west to Lingju, often opening canals and setting field officers, fifty or sixty thousand clerks and soldiers, nibbling away until the land joined the Xiongnu on the north.
translations.idiomatic: Han bridged the Yellow River from Shuofang to Lingju, dug irrigation ditches, and parked fifty or sixty thousand colonists on fields that crept toward the nomad line.

sentences.zh: 初，漢兩將大出圍單于，所殺虜八九萬，而漢士物故者亦萬數，漢馬死者十餘萬匹。

translations.literal: Earlier, when Han's two generals went out in force and surrounded the chanyu, those killed and captured were eighty or ninety thousand, but Han soldiers lost to death also in the ten thousands, and Han horses died more than a hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The paired expeditions had killed perhaps ninety thousand Xiongnu but cost Han tens of thousands of men and more than a hundred thousand horses.

sentences.zh: 匈奴雖病，遠去，而漢馬亦少，無以復往。

translations.literal: Although the Xiongnu were stricken and gone far, Han horses were also few and there was no means to go again.
translations.idiomatic: Both sides were crippled—Han simply had no mounts for another surge.

sentences.zh: 單于用趙信計，遣使好辭請和親。

translations.literal: The chanyu used Zhao Xin's plan, sending envoys with fair words requesting marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu took Zhao Xin's advice and sent smooth envoys begging for heqin again.

sentences.zh: 天子下其議，或言和親，或言遂臣之。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven sent the proposal down for deliberation; some said marriage alliance, some said then make them subjects.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Di laid the letters before his court: some urged peace marriage, others outright vassalage.

sentences.zh: 丞相長史任敞曰：「匈奴新困，宜使為外臣，朝請於邊。

translations.literal: Chief Clerk of the Chancellor Ren Chang said: "The Xiongnu are newly straitened; it is fitting to make them outer subjects attending court at the border."
translations.idiomatic: Ren Chang argued they were weak enough to be reduced to "outer vassals" reporting at the passes.

sentences.zh: 」漢使敞使於單于。

translations.literal: Han sent Chang as envoy to the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent Ren Chang north to deliver the ultimatum.

sentences.zh: 單于聞敞計，大怒，留之不遣。

translations.literal: When the chanyu heard Chang's proposal he was greatly angry and detained him, not sending him back.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu heard the demand and threw Ren Chang in chains.

sentences.zh: 先是，漢亦有所降匈奴使者，單于亦輒留漢使相當。

translations.literal: Earlier Han too had detained Xiongnu envoys; the chanyu likewise left Han envoys in matching numbers.
translations.idiomatic: Han had already held Xiongnu hostages; the chanyu now balanced the ledger.

sentences.zh: 漢方復收士馬，會票騎將軍去病死，於是漢久不北擊胡。

translations.literal: Han was meanwhile again gathering soldiers and horses, but it happened that General of Agile Cavalry Qubing died of illness, whereupon Han long did not strike north at the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Han was rebuilding armies when Huo Qubing died; northern offensives stalled for years.

sentences.zh: 數歲，伊稚斜單于立十三年死，子烏維立為單于。

translations.literal: Several years later Yizhixie Chanyu died in the thirteenth year of his establishment; his son Wuwei was established as chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Thirteen years into his reign Yizhixie died; his son Wuvie succeeded.

sentences.zh: 是歲，元鼎三年也。

translations.literal: This year was the third year of Yuanding.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Yuanding 3.

sentences.zh: 烏維單於立，而漢武帝始出巡狩郡縣。

translations.literal: When Wuwei Chanyu was established, Han Emperor Wu began going out on inspection tours to commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: Wuvie's accession coincided with Wu Di's first great tours of the provinces.

sentences.zh: 其後漢方南誅兩越，不擊匈奴，匈奴亦不入邊。

translations.literal: Afterward Han was then punishing the two Yue in the south and did not strike the Xiongnu; the Xiongnu also did not enter the border.
translations.idiomatic: While Han swallowed Minyue and Nanyue, neither side tested the northern wall.

sentences.zh: 烏維立三年，漢已滅兩越，遣故太僕公孫賀將萬五千騎出九原二千餘里，至浮苴井，從票侯趙破奴萬餘騎出令居數千里，至匈奴河水，皆不見匈奴一人而還。

translations.literal: In the third year of Wuwei's establishment Han had already destroyed the two Yue and sent former Grand Coachman Gongsun He to lead fifteen thousand riders from Jiuyuan more than two thousand li to Fuzujing; Marquis of Congpiao Zhao Ponu with more than ten thousand riders from Lingju several thousand li to the Xiongnu River, all saw not a single Xiongnu person and returned.
translations.idiomatic: In Wuvie's third year Gongsun He rode fifteen thousand horse from Jiuyuan to Fuzujing, Zhao Ponu ten thousand from Lingju to the Xiongnu River—both found empty grass and turned home.

sentences.zh: 是時，天子巡邊，親至朔方，勒兵十八萬騎以見武節，而使郭吉風告單于。

translations.literal: At that time the Son of Heaven toured the border in person, reaching Shuofang, drilling eighteen thousand riders to display military majesty, and sent Guo Ji to inform the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then rode to Shuofang, paraded eighteen thousand cavalry as a show of force, and sent Guo Ji to lecture the chanyu.

sentences.zh: 既至匈奴，匈奴主客問所使，郭吉卑體好言曰：「吾見單于而口言。

translations.literal: Having reached the Xiongnu, the Xiongnu host-guest official asked the purpose of the mission; Guo Ji lowered his body with fair words, saying, "I shall see the chanyu and speak by mouth."
translations.idiomatic: At the Xiongnu court the master of guests asked his errand; Guo Ji bowed low and said he would speak only to the chanyu.

sentences.zh: 」單于見吉，吉曰：「南越王頭已懸於漢北闕下。

translations.literal: When the chanyu saw Ji, Ji said, "The king of Nanyue's head already hangs below Han's north portal."
translations.idiomatic: Face to face Guo Ji announced that Nanyue's king already hung at the Changan gate.

sentences.zh: 今單于即能前與漢戰，天子自將兵待邊；

translations.literal: "If now the chanyu can advance and fight Han, the Son of Heaven himself leads troops waiting at the border;"
translations.idiomatic: " If you mean to fight, the Son of Heaven waits on the frontier in person."

sentences.zh: 即不能，亟南面而臣子漢。

translations.literal: "if he cannot, then hastily face south and be son and subject to Han."
translations.idiomatic: " If not, face south and submit at once."

sentences.zh: 何但遠走，亡匿於幕北寒苦無水草之地為？

translations.literal: "Why only flee far, hiding in the cold bitter desert north of the Gobi without water and grass?"
translations.idiomatic: " Why cower beyond the sand in a land without grass or water?"

sentences.zh: 」語卒，單于大怒，立斬主客見者，而留郭吉不歸，遷辱之北海上。

translations.literal: When the words ended the chanyu was greatly angry and immediately executed the host-guest official who had seen him, detained Guo Ji and did not send him back, and transferred him in disgrace to the north sea.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu beheaded his own master of guests in fury, kept Guo Ji, and banished him to the northern sea.

sentences.zh: 而單于終不肯為寇於漢邊，休養士馬，習射獵，數使使好辭甘言求和親。

translations.literal: Yet the chanyu to the end was not willing to raid Han's border, rested and nurtured soldiers and horses, practiced archery and hunting, and repeatedly sent envoys with fair words and sweet speech seeking marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: Still he would not strike Han; he rebuilt his herds, drilled his men, and kept sending honeyed pleas for heqin.

sentences.zh: 漢使王烏等窺匈奴。

translations.literal: Han sent Wang Wu and others to spy on the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Han dispatched Wang Wu and others to probe the court.

sentences.zh: 匈奴法，漢使不去節、不以墨黥其面，不得入穹廬。

translations.literal: By Xiongnu law, Han envoys who did not discard their staffs or did not tattoo their faces with ink could not enter the round tent.
translations.idiomatic: Xiongnu law barred envoys from the royal yurt unless they dropped their batons and accepted a face tattoo.

sentences.zh: 王烏，北地人，習胡俗，去其節，黥面入廬。

translations.literal: Wang Wu was a man of Beidi, practiced Hu custom, discarded his staff, tattooed his face, and entered the tent.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Wu, a Beidi native used to steppe ways, submitted to the ritual and entered.

sentences.zh: 單于愛之，陽許曰：「吾為遣其太子入質於漢，以求和親。」

translations.literal: The chanyu loved him and falsely promised, saying, "I shall for that send my heir son as hostage to Han, seeking marriage alliance."
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu favored him and lied that he would send the crown prince south as a hostage.

sentences.zh: 漢使楊信使於匈奴。

translations.literal: Han sent Yang Xin as envoy to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Han next sent Yang Xin.

sentences.zh: 是時，漢東拔濊貉、朝鮮以為郡，而西置酒泉郡以隔絕胡與羌通之路。

translations.literal: At that time Han had seized Mohe and Chosŏn in the east to make commanderies, and in the west set Jiuquan commandery to cut off the route by which Hu and Qiang could communicate.
translations.idiomatic: Han had annexed the eastern Mohe and Chosŏn as commanderies and sliced the west with Jiuquan to sever Hu from Qiang.

sentences.zh: 又西通月氏、大夏，以翁主妻烏孫王，以分匈奴西方之援。

translations.literal: They also opened passage west to Yuezhi and Bactria, marrying a princess to the Wusun king to split the Xiongnu western allies.
translations.idiomatic: Caravans now ran to Yuezhi and Bactria, and a princess wed the Wusun king to peel away Xiongnu allies.

sentences.zh: 又北益廣田至眩雷為塞，而匈奴終不敢以為言。

translations.literal: They also extended plowed fields north to Xuanlei as a barrier, yet the Xiongnu to the end dared not speak of it.
translations.idiomatic: Han pushed military farms north to Xuanlei; the Xiongnu swallowed the insult in silence.

sentences.zh: 是歲，翕侯信死，漢用事者以匈奴已弱，可臣從也。

translations.literal: That year Marquis of Xi Xin died; Han's men in power thought the Xiongnu had already weakened and could be made subjects.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhao Xin died, Han ministers decided the Xiongnu could be forced into vassalage.

sentences.zh: 楊信為人剛直屈強，素非貴臣也，單于不親。

translations.literal: Yang Xin was by nature rigid, blunt, and stubborn, never a noble minister by rank; the chanyu did not favor him.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xin was stiff-necked and low in rank; the chanyu disliked him.

sentences.zh: 欲召入，不肯去節，乃坐穹廬外見楊信。

translations.literal: Wishing to summon him inside, he would not discard his staff, so he sat outside the round tent to see Yang Xin.
translations.idiomatic: He refused the yurt ritual, so the chanyu met him outside the felt wall.

sentences.zh: 楊信說單于曰：「即欲和親，以單于太子為質於漢。

translations.literal: Yang Xin persuaded the chanyu, saying, "If you wish marriage alliance, send the chanyu's heir son as hostage in Han."
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xin demanded a crown-prince hostage for any new treaty.

sentences.zh: 」單于曰：「非故約。

translations.literal: The chanyu said, "That is not the old covenant."
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu snapped that this broke precedent.

sentences.zh: 故約，漢常遣翁主，給繒絮、食物有品，以和親，而匈奴亦不復擾邊。

translations.literal: "In the old covenant Han always sent a princess, supplying floss silk and foodstuffs in grades, thereby making marriage alliance, and the Xiongnu also did not again disturb the border."
translations.idiomatic: " The old bargain was a Han princess, graded silks and food, and quiet borders."

sentences.zh: 今乃欲反古，令吾太子為質，無幾矣。

translations.literal: "Now you desire to reverse antiquity and make my heir son hostage—there is little chance of that."
translations.idiomatic: " Now you want my heir in Chang'an—forget it."

sentences.zh: 」匈奴俗，見漢使非中貴人，其儒生，以為欲說，折其辭辯；

translations.literal: By Xiongnu custom, when they saw Han envoys who were not court favorites, if they were Ru scholars they thought them wanting to discourse and broke their rhetoric;
translations.idiomatic: Their etiquette said: if the envoy was a pedantic scholar, humiliate his rhetoric;

sentences.zh: 少年，以為欲刺，折其氣。

translations.literal: if young they thought them wanting to assassinate and broke their spirit.
translations.idiomatic: if he was young, treat him as an assassin and cow him.

sentences.zh: 每漢兵入匈奴，匈奴輒報償。

translations.literal: Whenever Han troops entered the Xiongnu, the Xiongnu always repaid in kind.
translations.idiomatic: Every Han raid drew a matching reprisal.

sentences.zh: 漢留匈奴使，匈奴亦留漢使，必得當乃止。

translations.literal: Han detained Xiongnu envoys; the Xiongnu also detained Han envoys, stopping only when they had a matching number.
translations.idiomatic: Hostage envoys were traded one for one until the counts evened.

sentences.zh: 楊信既歸，漢使王烏等如匈奴。

translations.literal: After Yang Xin returned, Han sent Wang Wu and others to the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: When Yang Xin came home, Wang Wu carried the next round of talks north.

sentences.zh: 匈奴復諂以甘言，欲多得漢財物，紿王烏曰：「吾欲入漢見天子，面相結為兄弟。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu again flattered with sweet speech, wishing to obtain much Han goods, deceiving Wang Wu, saying, "We wish to enter Han to see the Son of Heaven and face to face covenant as sworn brothers.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu spun honeyed lies to milk more gifts, telling Wang Wu they would visit the emperor and swear brotherhood in person.

sentences.zh: 」王烏歸報漢，漢為單于築邸於長安。

translations.literal: " Wang Wu returned and reported to Han; Han built a residence for the chanyu in Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: " Wang Wu sold the story in Chang'an; the court built a guest mansion for the chanyu."

sentences.zh: 匈奴曰：「非得漢貴人使，吾不與誠語。

translations.literal: " The Xiongnu said, "Unless we get a noble Han envoy, We will not speak sincerely."
translations.idiomatic: " They demanded a high-ranking envoy or no straight talk."

sentences.zh: 」匈奴使其貴人至漢，病，服藥欲愈之，不幸而死。

translations.literal: " The Xiongnu sent their noble to Han; he fell ill; they gave medicine wishing to cure him, but unluckily he died.
translations.idiomatic: " A Xiongnu noble died under Han doctors in the capital."

sentences.zh: 漢使路充國佩二千石印綬使，送其喪，厚幣直數千金。

translations.literal: Han sent Lu Chongguo bearing the two-thousand-picul seal and ribbon as envoy, escorting his funeral, with thick gifts worth several thousand in gold.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Chongguo, a two-thousand-picul official, escorted the body home with lavish funeral gifts.

sentences.zh: 單于以為漢殺吾貴使者，乃留路充國不歸。

translations.literal: The chanyu thought Han had killed Our noble envoy, therefore detained Lu Chongguo and did not send him back.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu decided Han had murdered his man and held Lu Chongguo hostage.

sentences.zh: 諸所言者，單于特空紿王烏，殊無意入漢、遣太子來質。

translations.literal: All that was said the chanyu had merely idly deceived Wang Wu; he had absolutely no intent to enter Han or send the heir son as hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Every promise to Wang Wu had been empty: no royal visit, no crown-prince hostage.

sentences.zh: 於是匈奴數使奇兵侵犯漢邊，漢乃拜郭昌為拔胡將軍，乃浞野侯屯朔方以東，備胡。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Xiongnu repeatedly sent odd troops to violate Han's border; Han thereupon appointed Guo Chang general who pulls up the Hu, and Marquis of Zhuoye encamped east of Shuofang to guard against the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Raids multiplied until Han named Guo Chang "general who uproots the Hu" and posted Marquis Zhuoye east of Shuofang.

sentences.zh: 烏維單于立十歲死，子詹師廬立，年少，號為皃單于。

translations.literal: Chanyu Wuvie died in the tenth year of his establishment; his son Zhanshilü was established, young in years, titled the Juvenile Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Wuvie died after ten years; his young son Zhanshilü succeeded as the "Boy Chanyu."

sentences.zh: 是歲，元封六年也。

translations.literal: This year was the sixth year of Yuanfeng.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Yuanfeng 6.

sentences.zh: 自是後，單于益西北，左方兵直雲中，右方兵直酒泉、敦煌。

translations.literal: From this on the chanyu shifted further northwest; the left wing's troops faced Yunzhong, the right wing's faced Jiuquan and Dunhuang.
translations.idiomatic: The court moved northwest, anchoring its left on Yunzhong and its right on Jiuquan-Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 皃單于立，漢使兩使，一人吊單于，一人吊右賢王，欲以乖其國。

translations.literal: When the Juvenile Chanyu was established, Han sent two envoys, one to condole the chanyu, one to condole the right Wise King, wishing thereby to divide their state.
translations.idiomatic: Han sent paired envoys—one to mourn the chanyu, one the right Wise King—to sow discord.

sentences.zh: 使者入匈奴，匈奴悉將致單于。

translations.literal: When the envoys entered the Xiongnu, the Xiongnu brought them all to the chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu herded both parties to one audience.

sentences.zh: 單于怒而悉留漢使。

translations.literal: The chanyu was angry and detained all Han envoys.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu, furious, jailed every Han messenger.

sentences.zh: 漢使留匈奴者前後十餘輩，而匈奴使來漢，亦輒留之相當。

translations.literal: Han envoys detained by the Xiongnu numbered more than ten parties in succession, and when Xiongnu envoys came to Han, Han likewise detained them in matching numbers.
translations.idiomatic: Hostage diplomacy escalated: more than ten Han missions held north, each answered in kind.

sentences.zh: 是歲，漢使貳師將軍西伐大宛，而令因杅將軍築受降城。

translations.literal: That year Han sent General of the Second Corps west to attack Dayuan, and ordered General Yangan to build the Surrender Acceptance city.
translations.idiomatic: The same year Li Guangli marched on Dayuan while Yangan general built Fort Accept-Surrender.

sentences.zh: 其冬，匈奴大雨雪，畜多饑寒死，皃單于年少，好殺伐，國中多不安。

translations.literal: That winter the Xiongnu had great rain and snow; many beasts died of cold and hunger; the Juvenile Chanyu was young and fond of killing and campaigning, and the state was much unsettled.
translations.idiomatic: A blizzard starved the herds; the boy chanyu loved war, and the realm seethed.

sentences.zh: 左大都尉欲殺單于，使人間告漢曰：「我欲殺單于降漢，漢遠，漢即來兵近我，我即發。

translations.literal: The left grand commandant wished to kill the chanyu, sending someone secretly to tell Han: "I wish to kill the chanyu and surrender to Han; Han is far—if Han will come with troops near me, I will rise."
translations.idiomatic: The left grand commandant plotted regicide and asked Han to move an army within supporting distance.

sentences.zh: 」初漢聞此言，故築受降城。

translations.literal: " When Han first heard these words, it therefore built the Surrender Acceptance city.
translations.idiomatic: " That is why Han had raised Accept-Surrender fort."

sentences.zh: 猶以為遠。

translations.literal: Still they thought it too far.
translations.idiomatic: Even that outpost seemed too distant.

sentences.zh: 其明年春，漢使浞野侯破奴將二萬騎出朔方北二千餘里，期至浚稽山而還。

translations.literal: The next spring Han sent Marquis of Zhuoye Ponu with twenty thousand riders out from Shuofang north more than two thousand li, fixing a rendezvous to reach Mount Junji and return.
translations.idiomatic: Next spring Zhao Ponu, marquis of Zhuoye, took twenty thousand cavalry from Shuofang toward Junji Mountain.

sentences.zh: 浞野侯既至期，左大都尉欲發而覺，單于誅之，發兵擊浞野侯。

translations.literal: When Zhuoye had reached the appointed time, the left grand commandant wished to rise but was discovered; the chanyu executed him and raised troops to strike Zhuoye.
translations.idiomatic: The plot leaked; the chanyu killed the conspirator and fell on Ponu.

sentences.zh: 浞野侯行捕首虜數千人。

translations.literal: Marquis of Zhuoye on the march captured several thousand heads and captives.
translations.idiomatic: Ponu still bagged a few thousand heads on the march.

sentences.zh: 還，未至受降城四百里，匈奴八萬騎圍之。

translations.literal: Returning, not yet four hundred li from Surrender Acceptance city, eighty thousand Xiongnu riders surrounded him.
translations.idiomatic: Eighty thousand horse surrounded him four hundred li short of the fort.

sentences.zh: 浞野侯夜出自求水，匈奴生得浞野侯，因急擊其軍。

translations.literal: Marquis of Zhuoye went out by night himself seeking water; the Xiongnu captured Zhuoye alive and urgently struck his army.
translations.idiomatic: Ponu slipped out for water at night, was captured, and his command collapsed under the rush.

sentences.zh: 軍吏畏亡將而誅，莫相勸而歸，軍遂沒於匈奴。

translations.literal: The army officers feared execution for losing the general and none urged each other to return; the army was thereupon swallowed by the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Officers feared decapitation for losing their chief and scattered; the division vanished into captivity.

sentences.zh: 單于大喜，遂遣兵攻受降城，不能下，乃侵入邊而去。

translations.literal: The chanyu was greatly pleased, thereupon sent troops to attack Surrender Acceptance city, could not take it, then invaded the border and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Elated, he struck Accept-Surrender, failed to storm it, and withdrew after border raids.

sentences.zh: 明年，單于欲自攻受降城，未到，病死。

translations.literal: The next year the chanyu wished himself to attack Surrender Acceptance city; before arriving he fell ill and died.
translations.idiomatic: He planned a second siege but died on the road.

sentences.zh: 皃單于立三歲而死。

translations.literal: The Juvenile Chanyu died after three years of establishment.
translations.idiomatic: The Boy Chanyu reigned only three years.

sentences.zh: 子少，匈奴乃立其季父烏維單于弟右賢王句黎湖為單于。

translations.literal: The son was young; the Xiongnu thereupon established his paternal uncle, the younger brother of Chanyu Wuvie, the right Wise King Julihou, as chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: With the heir a child, the nobles raised Wuvie's brother Julihou, the right Wise King.

sentences.zh: 是歲，太初三年也。

translations.literal: This year was the third year of Taichu.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Taichu 3.

sentences.zh: 句黎湖單于立，漢使光祿勳徐自為出五原塞數百里，遠者千里，築城障列亭至盧朐，而使游擊將軍韓說、長平侯衛伉屯其旁，使強弩都尉路博德築居延澤上。

translations.literal: When Julihou Chanyu was established, Han sent Superintendent of the Imperial Household Xu Ziwei out from Wuyuan barrier several hundred li, in distant places a thousand li, building walls, barriers, and linked posts to Luqu, while sending General of Roving Attack Han Shuo and Marquis of Changping Wei Kang to encamp beside them, and ordered Commandant of Strong Crossbow Lu Bode to build on the Juyan marsh.
translations.idiomatic: Julihou's accession brought Xu Ziwei north from Wuyuan to build a picket line to Luqu, flanked by Han Shuo and Wei Kang, while Lu Bode walled the Juyan oasis.

sentences.zh: 其秋，匈奴大人云中、定襄、五原、朔方，殺略數千人，敗數二千石而去，行壞光祿所築亭障。

translations.literal: That autumn the Xiongnu greatly entered Yunzhong, Dingxiang, Wuyuan, and Shuofang, killing and plundering several thousand, defeating several two-thousand-picul officials and departing, tearing down the posts and barriers the superintendent had built.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn they stormed four commanderies, killed thousands, routed Han magistrates, and tore down Xu's new posts.

sentences.zh: 又使右賢王入酒泉、張掖，略數千人。

translations.literal: They also sent the right Wise King into Jiuquan and Zhangye, plundering several thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The right Wise King raided Jiuquan and Zhangye for more captives.

sentences.zh: 會任文擊救，盡復失其所得而去。

translations.literal: It happened that Ren Wen struck in relief and wholly recovered what had been lost, and they departed.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Wen counterattacked and recovered the loot.

sentences.zh: 聞貳師將軍破大宛，斬其王還，單于欲遮之，不敢，其冬病死。

translations.literal: Hearing that the Second Corps general had broken Dayuan and beheaded its king returning, the chanyu wished to intercept him but did not dare; that winter he died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Learning Li Guangli was returning from Dayuan, Julihou thought of ambush, thought better of it, and died that winter.

sentences.zh: 句黎湖單于立一歲死，其弟左大都尉且鞮侯立為單于。

translations.literal: Julihou Chanyu died after one year of establishment; his younger brother, the left grand commandant Qiedihou, was established as chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Julihou ruled one year; his brother Qiedihou, the left grand commandant, succeeded.

sentences.zh: 漢既誅大宛，威震外國，天子意欲遂困胡，乃下詔曰：「高皇帝遺朕平城之憂，高後時單于書絕悖逆。

translations.literal: After Han had executed Dayuan, its awesomen shook foreign states; the Son of Heaven's intent wished then to exhaust the Hu, and issued an edict saying: "The High Ancestor left Us the worry of Pingcheng; in Empress Gao's time the chanyu's letter was utterly contrary and rebellious.
translations.idiomatic: After the Dayuan campaign Wu Di vowed to finish the Xiongnu, citing Gaozu's shame at Pingcheng and Modu's insult to Empress Lü.

sentences.zh: 昔齊襄公復九世之讎，《春秋》大之。

translations.literal: " Formerly Duke Xiang of Qi avenged a nine-generation feud; the 《Spring and Autumn》 magnified it."
translations.idiomatic: " The 《Spring and Autumn》 praised Qi Xiang's nine-generation vengeance."

sentences.zh: 」是歲，太初四年也。

translations.literal: " This year was the fourth year of Taichu.
translations.idiomatic: " The edict closed the year Taichu 4."

sentences.zh: 且鞮侯單于初立，恐漢襲之，盡歸漢使之不降者路充國等於漢。

translations.literal: When Qiedihou Chanyu had just been established, fearing Han would strike him, he returned all Han envoys who had not surrendered, such as Lu Chongguo, to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing invasion, Qiedihou sent every detained Han envoy who had not defected home, including Lu Chongguo.

sentences.zh: 單于乃自謂：「我兒子，安敢望漢天子！

translations.literal: The chanyu then said of himself: "I am a child—how dare I look toward the Han Son of Heaven!
translations.idiomatic: He styled himself "only a child" before Han's majesty.

sentences.zh: 漢天子，我丈人行。

translations.literal: " The Han Son of Heaven is my elder by a generation."
translations.idiomatic: " The Han emperor is my elder."

sentences.zh: 」漢遣中郎將蘇武厚幣賂遺單于，單于益驕，禮甚倨，非漢所望也。

translations.literal: " Han sent Palace Gentleman Su Wu with thick gifts to bribe the chanyu; the chanyu grew prouder still, his courtesy very arrogant—not what Han had hoped.
translations.idiomatic: Han answered with gifts and Su Wu; the chanyu grew haughtier than ever.

sentences.zh: 明年，浞野侯破奴得亡歸漢。

translations.literal: The next year Marquis of Zhuoye Ponu escaped and returned to Han.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Zhao Ponu escaped from captivity.

sentences.zh: 其明年，漢使貳師將軍將三萬騎出酒泉，擊右賢王於天山，得首虜萬餘級而還。

translations.literal: The next year Han sent the Second Corps general with thirty thousand riders out from Jiuquan, striking the right Wise King at the Celestial Mountains, obtaining more than ten thousand heads and ranks and returning.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guangli struck the right Wise King at the Tian Shan with thirty thousand horse and ten thousand counted kills.

sentences.zh: 匈奴大圍貳師，幾不得脫。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu greatly surrounded the Second Corps; he nearly could not escape.
translations.idiomatic: The nomads nearly encircled and destroyed him.

sentences.zh: 漢兵物故什六七。

translations.literal: Han soldiers were lost six or seven tenths to death.
translations.idiomatic: Six or seven men in ten never came home.

sentences.zh: 漢又使因杅將軍出西河，與強弩都尉會涿邪山，亡所得。

translations.literal: Han also sent the Yangan general out from Xihe, joining the commandant of strong crossbow at Mount Zhuoye, obtaining nothing.
translations.idiomatic: The Yangan column from Xihe met Lu Bode at Zhuoye Mountain and found no enemy.

sentences.zh: 使騎都尉李陵將步兵五千人出居延北千餘里，與單于會，合戰，陵所殺傷萬餘人，兵食盡，欲歸，單于圍陵，陵降匈奴，其兵得脫歸漢者四百人。

translations.literal: They sent Commandant of Cavalry Li Ling to lead five thousand foot soldiers out from Juyan north more than a thousand li, meeting the chanyu; joining battle, Ling killed and wounded more than ten thousand; when arms and food were exhausted he wished to return, the chanyu surrounded Ling, Ling surrendered to the Xiongnu, and of his troops who escaped and returned to Han there were four hundred men.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ling marched five thousand infantry beyond Juyan, killed ten thousand, ran out of supplies, and surrendered when surrounded; four hundred stragglers reached Han.

sentences.zh: 單于乃貴陵，以其女妻之。

translations.literal: The chanyu thereupon honored Ling, marrying his daughter to him.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu ennobled him and married him to a princess.

sentences.zh: 後二歲，漢使貳師將軍六萬騎、步兵七萬，出朔方；

translations.literal: Two years later Han sent the Second Corps general sixty thousand riders and seventy thousand foot out from Shuofang;
translations.idiomatic: Two years later Li Guangli led sixty thousand horse and seventy thousand foot from Shuofang;

sentences.zh: 強弩都尉路博德將萬餘人，與貳師會，游擊將軍說步兵三萬人，出五原；

translations.literal: Commandant of Strong Crossbow Lu Bode led more than ten thousand men to join the Second Corps; General of Roaming Attack Han Shuo thirty thousand foot, out from Wuyuan;
translations.idiomatic: Lu Bode added ten thousand to meet him; Han Shuo took thirty thousand foot from Wuyuan;

sentences.zh: 因杅將軍敖將騎萬，步兵三萬人，出雁門。

translations.literal: Yangan General Ao led ten thousand riders and thirty thousand foot, out from Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Ao led ten thousand cavalry and thirty thousand foot from Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 匈奴聞，悉遠其累重於余吾水北，而單于以十萬待水南，與貳師接戰。

translations.literal: When the Xiongnu heard it, they moved all their baggage far to the north shore of the Yuwu River, while the chanyu with a hundred thousand awaited south of the water, joining battle with the Second Corps.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu parked their wagons north of the Yuwu while the chanyu met Li Guangli south of the river with a hundred thousand.

sentences.zh: 貳師解而引歸，與單于連鬥十餘日，游擊亡所得。

translations.literal: The Second Corps broke free and withdrew, fighting the chanyu in succession more than ten days; the roving attack found nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guangli disengaged after ten days of fighting; Han Shuo found empty steppe.

sentences.zh: 因杅與左賢王戰，不利，引歸。

translations.literal: Yangan joined battle with the left Wise King, fared badly, and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Ao lost to the left Wise King and pulled back.

sentences.zh: 明年，且鞮侯單于死，立五年，長子左賢王立為狐鹿姑單于。

translations.literal: The next year Qiedihou Chanyu died; having been established five years, the eldest son, the left Wise King, was established as Hulugu Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Qiedihou died after five years; his heir, the left Wise King, became Chanyu Hulugu.

sentences.zh: 是歲，太始元年也。

translations.literal: This year was the first year of Taishi.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Taishi 1.

sentences.zh: 初，且鞮侯兩子，長為左賢王，次為左大將，病且死，言立左賢王。

translations.literal: Earlier Qiedihou had two sons: the eldest was left Wise King, the second was left grand general; when ill and about to die he said to establish the left Wise King.
translations.idiomatic: Qiedihou had willed the throne to his elder son, the left Wise King.

sentences.zh: 左賢王未至，貴人以為有病，更立左大將為單于。

translations.literal: The left Wise King had not arrived; the nobles thought him ill and instead established the left grand general as chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: Before the heir arrived the council assumed sickness and raised the younger brother.

sentences.zh: 左賢王聞之，不敢進。

translations.literal: When the left Wise King heard it, he did not dare advance.
translations.idiomatic: The true heir halted, fearing a trap.

sentences.zh: 左大將使人召左賢王而讓位焉。

translations.literal: The left grand general sent men to summon the left Wise King and yield the position to him.
translations.idiomatic: The new chanyu summoned him to take the throne.

sentences.zh: 左賢王辭以病，左大將不聽，謂曰：「即不幸死，傳之於我。

translations.literal: The left Wise King declined citing illness; the left grand general would not listen, saying to him, "If you unluckily die, pass it to me."
translations.idiomatic: The elder pleaded illness; the younger insisted, "If you die, I inherit."

sentences.zh: 」左賢王許之，遂立為狐鹿姑單于。

translations.literal: " The left Wise King promised; thereupon he was established as Hulugu Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: " The left Wise King accepted and became Hulugu."

sentences.zh: 狐鹿姑單于立，以左大將為左賢王，數年病死，其子先賢撣不得代，更以為日逐王。

translations.literal: When Hulugu Chanyu was established, he made the left grand general the left Wise King; after several years he died of illness; his son Xianxian Chan could not succeed in his place, and he was instead made Rizhu king.
translations.idiomatic: Hulugu made his benefactor left Wise King; when that man died, his son Xianxian Chan was passed over for the lower rank of Rizhu king.

sentences.zh: 日逐王者，賤於左賢王。

translations.literal: The Rizhu king is lower than the left Wise King.
translations.idiomatic: Rizhu ranks below the eastern heir-apparent.

sentences.zh: 單于自以其子為左賢王。

translations.literal: The chanyu himself appointed his son left Wise King.
translations.idiomatic: Hulugu named his own son left Wise King.

sentences.zh: 單于既立六年，而匈奴入上谷、五原，殺略吏民。

translations.literal: Six years after the chanyu was established, the Xiongnu entered Shanggu and Wuyuan, killing and plundering clerks and commoners.
translations.idiomatic: In his sixth year raiders hit Shanggu and Wuyuan again.

sentences.zh: 其年，匈奴復入五原、酒泉，殺兩部都尉。

translations.literal: That year the Xiongnu again entered Wuyuan and Jiuquan, killing the commandants of both divisions.
translations.idiomatic: The same year they murdered the commandants of Wuyuan and Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: 於是漢遣貳師將軍七萬人出五原，御史大夫商丘成將三萬餘人出西河，重合侯莽通將四萬騎出酒泉千餘里。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han dispatched the Second Corps general seventy thousand men out of Wuyuan, Imperial Counsellor Shangqiu Cheng more than thirty thousand out of Xihe, and Marquis of Chonghe Mang Tong forty thousand riders out of Jiuquan more than a thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Han answered with Li Guangli at seventy thousand from Wuyuan, Shangqiu Cheng from Xihe, and Mang Tong's forty thousand horse from Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: 單于聞漢兵大出，悉遣其輜重，徙趙信城北邸郅居水。

translations.literal: When the chanyu heard Han troops were going out in force, he sent away all his baggage, moving north of Zhao Xin's fort to the Zhiqi waters.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu sent his wagons deep north of Zhao Xin's city to the Zhiqi River.

sentences.zh: 左賢王驅其人民度余吾水六七百里，居兜銜山。

translations.literal: The left Wise King drove his people across the Yuwu River six or seven hundred li and dwelt at Mount Douxian.
translations.idiomatic: The left Wise King herded his clans six or seven hundred li across the Yuwu to Mount Douxian.

sentences.zh: 單于自將精兵左安侯度姑且水。

translations.literal: The chanyu personally led crack troops; the Marquis of Zuo'an forded the Guqie River.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu led veterans while the Marquis of Zuo'an crossed the Guqie.

sentences.zh: 御史大夫軍至追邪徑，無所見，還。

translations.literal: The imperial counsellor's army reached Zhuoying Path, saw nothing, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Shangqiu Cheng's column found no fight at Zhuoying Path.

sentences.zh: 匈奴使大將與李陵將三萬餘騎追漢軍，至浚稽山合，轉戰九日，漢兵陷陳卻敵，殺傷虜甚眾。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu sent a great general with Li Ling leading more than thirty thousand riders pursuing Han troops; reaching Mount Junji they joined, turning and fighting nine days; Han troops broke formations and repelled the enemy, killing, wounding, and capturing very many.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ling led thirty thousand Xiongnu horse against Han at Junji; nine days of battle cost both sides heavily until the nomads broke off at Punu River.

sentences.zh: 至蒲奴水，虜不利，還去。

translations.literal: Reaching Punu River, the captives fared badly and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: At Punu the Xiongnu gave up the pursuit.

sentences.zh: 重合侯軍至天山，匈奴使大將偃渠與左右呼知王將二萬餘騎要漢兵，見漢兵強，引去。

translations.literal: Marquis of Chonghe's army reached the Celestial Mountains; the Xiongnu sent great general Yanqu with the left and right Huzhi kings leading more than twenty thousand riders to intercept Han troops; seeing Han troops strong, they led away.
translations.idiomatic: Mang Tong reached Tian Shan; Yanqu and the Huzhi kings shadowed him with twenty thousand horse but withdrew when Han looked too strong.

sentences.zh: 重合侯無所得失。

translations.literal: Marquis of Chonghe gained and lost nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Mang Tong came home empty-handed.

sentences.zh: 是時，漢恐車師兵遮重合侯，乃遣闓陵侯將兵別圍車師，盡得其王民眾而還。

translations.literal: At that time Han feared Cheshi troops would block Marquis of Chonghe, therefore sent Marquis of Kanling to lead troops separately to besiege Cheshi, wholly obtaining its king, people, and host and returning.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing Cheshi would ambush Mang Tong, Han sent the Marquis of Kailing to take the kingdom of Cheshi whole.

sentences.zh: 貳師將軍將出塞，匈奴使右大都尉與衛律將五千騎要擊漢軍於夫羊句山狹。

translations.literal: As the Second Corps general was about to go out of the barrier, the Xiongnu sent the right grand commandant with Wei Lu leading five thousand riders to ambush Han troops in the defile of Mount Fuyangju.
translations.idiomatic: On Li Guangli's exit the right grand commandant and Wei Lu hit him at Fuyangju gorge with five thousand horse.

sentences.zh: 貳師遣屬國胡騎二千與戰，虜兵壞散，死傷者數百人。

translations.literal: The Second Corps sent two thousand dependent-state Hu riders to fight them; the captives' troops broke and scattered, several hundred dead and wounded.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guangli threw two thousand allied Hu cavalry into the gap and broke the ambush for a few hundred kills.

sentences.zh: 漢軍乘勝追北，至范夫人城，匈奴奔走，莫敢距敵。

translations.literal: Han troops rode victory pursuing north, reaching Lady Fan's city; the Xiongnu fled in rout, none daring to withstand the enemy.
translations.idiomatic: Han pursued to Lady Fan's fort and cleared the field.

sentences.zh: 會貳師妻子坐巫蠱收，聞之憂懼。

translations.literal: It happened that the Second Corps' wife and children sat for witchcraft and were arrested; hearing it he was anxious and afraid.
translations.idiomatic: Then word came that his family had been arrested in the witchcraft purge.

sentences.zh: 其掾胡亞夫亦避罪從軍，說貳師曰：「夫人室家皆在吏，若還不稱意，適與獄會，郅居以北可復得見乎？

translations.literal: His clerk Hu Yafu also joined the army fleeing blame and persuaded the Second Corps, saying, "Your wife and household are all in the clerks' hands; if you return and it does not please them, you will just meet prison—can you see north of Zhiqi again?"
translations.idiomatic: His aide Hu Yafu whispered that returning to face the witchcraft court meant death.

sentences.zh: 」貳師由是狐疑，欲深入要功，遂北至郅居水上。

translations.literal: " The Second Corps for this reason hesitated, wishing to go deep to seize merit, therefore went north to the Zhiqi waters.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guangli wavered, then drove deeper for a redeeming victory.

sentences.zh: 虜已去，貳師遣護軍將二萬騎度郅居之水。

translations.literal: The captives had gone; the Second Corps sent the colonel-protector with twenty thousand riders to cross the Zhiqi water.
translations.idiomatic: He sent his colonel-protector across the Zhiqi after a fleeing foe.

sentences.zh: 一日，逢左賢王左大將，將二萬騎與漢軍合戰一日，漢軍殺左大將，虜死傷甚眾。

translations.literal: One day they met the left Wise King's left grand general leading twenty thousand riders; they joined battle one day with Han troops; Han troops killed the left grand general, and the captives' dead and wounded were very many.
translations.idiomatic: They collided with the left Wise King's left grand general at twenty thousand horse, killed him, and piled Xiongnu dead.

sentences.zh: 軍長史與決眭都尉煇渠侯謀曰：「將軍懷異心，欲危眾求功，恐必敗。

translations.literal: The army chief clerk plotted with Marquis Huiqu of Juesui commandant, saying, "The general harbors a different heart, wishing to endanger the host to seek merit—We fear he will certainly be defeated."
translations.idiomatic: The chief clerk and Huiqu marquis decided Li Guangli was risking the army for personal glory.

sentences.zh: 」謀共執貳師。

translations.literal: " They plotted together to seize the Second Corps.
translations.idiomatic: " They moved to arrest him."

sentences.zh: 貳師聞之，斬長史，引兵還至速邪烏燕然山。

translations.literal: When the Second Corps heard it, he beheaded the chief clerk and led troops back to Suye Wuyanran Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guangli executed the chief clerk and retreated toward Yanyran.

sentences.zh: 單于知漢軍勞倦，自將五萬騎遮擊貳師，相殺傷甚眾。

translations.literal: The chanyu knew Han troops were weary and personally led fifty thousand riders to intercept and strike the Second Corps; they killed and wounded each other very many.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing Han exhausted, the chanyu hit him with fifty thousand fresh horse.

sentences.zh: 夜塹漢軍前，深數尺，從後急擊之，軍大亂敗，貳師降。

translations.literal: By night they trenched before Han troops several feet deep, urgently struck them from behind; the army was in great chaos and defeated, and the Second Corps surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: They dug a trench before the Han camp, struck from the rear, and broke Li Guangli, who surrendered.

sentences.zh: 單于素知其漢大將貴臣，以女妻之，尊寵在衛律上。

translations.literal: The chanyu had long known he was Han's great general and noble minister; he married his daughter to him and honored him above Wei Lu.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu knew his prize: he married Li Guangli to a daughter and ranked him above Wei Lu.

sentences.zh: 其明年，單于遣使遺漢書云：「南有大漢，北有強胡。

translations.literal: The next year the chanyu sent envoys presenting a letter to Han saying, "South is great Han, north is mighty Hu.
translations.idiomatic: The next year a Xiongnu letter boasted: "South lies mighty Han, north stands mighty Hu."

sentences.zh: 胡者，天之驕子也，不為小禮以自煩。

translations.literal: " The Hu are heaven's favored sons; they do not burden themselves with petty rites."
translations.idiomatic: " The Hu are heaven's spoiled children—we scorn petty etiquette."

sentences.zh: 今欲與漢闓大關，取漢女為妻，歲給遺我糵酒萬石，稷米五千斛，雜繒萬匹，它如故約，則邊不相盜矣。

translations.literal: " Now We wish with Han to open the great passes broadly, take a Han woman as wife, yearly supply Us ten thousand piculs of malt wine, five thousand hu of millet, ten thousand bolts of mixed silks, other matters as the old covenant—then the border will not rob each other."
translations.idiomatic: " Open the gates wide, send a bride, ten thousand piculs of ale, five thousand hu of grain, ten thousand bolts of silk yearly as of old, and the raids stop."

sentences.zh: 」漢遣使者報送其使，單于使左右難漢使者，曰：「漢，禮義國也。

translations.literal: " Han sent envoys to answer and escort their envoy; the chanyu had those at his side embarrass Han envoys, saying, "Han is a state of rites and duty.
translations.idiomatic: " Han returned an embassy; Xiongnu courtiers sneered, "You are the kingdom of ritual."

sentences.zh: 貳師道前太子發兵反，何也？

translations.literal: " The Second Corps led the former heir apparent in raising troops to rebel—how is that?"
translations.idiomatic: " Yet your Second Corps helped the old crown prince rebel—how righteous is that?"

sentences.zh: 」使者曰：「然。

translations.literal: " The envoy said, "Yes."
translations.idiomatic: " The Han envoy admitted it."

sentences.zh: 乃丞相私與太子爭鬥，太子發兵欲誅丞相，丞相誣之，故誅丞相。

translations.literal: It was the chancellor privately quarreling with the heir apparent; the heir apparent raised troops wishing to execute the chancellor; the chancellor slandered him, therefore the chancellor was executed.
translations.idiomatic: It was a private feud between chancellor and crown prince: the prince drew arms, the chancellor cried treason, and the minister died for it.

sentences.zh: 此子弄父兵，罪當笞，小過耳。

translations.literal: This was the son playing with his father's soldiers—the crime deserved flogging, a small fault.
translations.idiomatic: The heir only "borrowed" his father's guards—a flogging offense at worst.

sentences.zh: 孰與冒頓單于身殺其父代立，常妻後母，禽獸行也！

translations.literal: How does that compare to Chanyu Modu personally killing his father to replace him, constantly marrying his junior wives after him—the conduct of birds and beasts!
translations.idiomatic: Compare that to Modu murdering his father, taking his stepmothers—that is bestial.

sentences.zh: 」單于留使者，三歲乃得還。

translations.literal: " The chanyu detained the envoy three years before he could return.
translations.idiomatic: " The Xiongnu held the envoy three years before release."

sentences.zh: 貳師在匈奴歲餘，衛律害其寵，會母閼氏病，律飭胡巫言先單于怒，曰：「胡故時祠兵，常言得貳師以社，今何故不用？

translations.literal: The Second Corps had been among the Xiongnu over a year; Wei Lu envied his favor; it happened the mother Yan wife fell ill; Lu ordered a Hu shaman to say the former chanyu was angry, saying, "In Hu former times when sacrificing to the army they always said if they obtained the Second Corps they would offer him at the earth altar—why now do they not use him?"
translations.idiomatic: After a year in the north Wei Lu grew jealous of Li Guangli; when the queen mother fell ill, Wei Lu bribed a shaman to say the late chanyu demanded Li Guangli for a blood sacrifice to the army spirits.

sentences.zh: 」於是收貳師，貳師罵曰：「我死必滅匈奴！

translations.literal: " Thereupon they seized the Second Corps; the Second Corps cursed, saying, "When I die I will surely destroy the Xiongnu!"
translations.idiomatic: " They arrested Li Guangli; he cursed, "My ghost will wipe you out."

sentences.zh: 」遂屠貳師以祠。

translations.literal: " Thereupon they butchered the Second Corps for sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: " They dismembered him for the altar."

sentences.zh: 會連雨雪數月，畜產死，人民疫病，谷稼不熟，單于恐，為貳師立祠室。

translations.literal: It happened that rain and snow continued several months; livestock died, the people sickened with plague, grain did not ripen; the chanyu was afraid and built a shrine chamber for the Second Corps.
translations.idiomatic: Months of blizzard followed—herds died, people sickened, crops failed; the chanyu, terrified, built Li Guangli a spirit hall.

sentences.zh: 自貳師沒後，漢新失大將軍士卒數萬人，不復出兵。

translations.literal: After the Second Corps perished, Han had newly lost a great general and several tens of thousands of soldiers and did not again send troops.
translations.idiomatic: Li Guangli's end cost Han tens of thousands of men and froze major offensives.

sentences.zh: 三歲，武帝崩。

translations.literal: Three years later Emperor Wu died.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later Wu Di died.

sentences.zh: 前此者，漢兵深入窮追二十餘年，匈奴孕重惰殰，罷極苦之。

translations.literal: Before this Han troops had gone deep in relentless pursuit more than twenty years; the Xiongnu, pregnant and heavy, weary and stillborn, were exhausted and bitterly suffered it.
translations.idiomatic: Two decades of deep strikes had left the Xiongnu herds barren, women miscarrying, and the whole nation exhausted.

sentences.zh: 自單于以下常有欲和親計。

translations.literal: From the chanyu downward there were often those who wished to plan marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: Even the chanyu's court talked peace again.

sentences.zh: 後三年，單于欲求和親，會病死。

translations.literal: Three years later the chanyu wished to seek marriage alliance; it happened he fell ill and died.
translations.idiomatic: Three years on he sued for heqin—then died of illness.

sentences.zh: 初，單于有異母弟為左大都尉，賢，國人鄉之，母閼氏恐單于不立子而立左大都尉也，乃私使殺之。

translations.literal: Earlier the chanyu had a younger half-brother who was left grand commandant, worthy, and the people of the state turned to him; the mother Yan wife feared the chanyu would not establish his son but establish the left grand commandant, therefore secretly sent to kill him.
translations.idiomatic: Hulugu had a brilliant half-brother, the left grand commandant; the queen mother had him murdered so her son would inherit.

sentences.zh: 左大都尉同母兄怨，遂不肯復會單于庭。

translations.literal: The left grand commandant's full brother by the same mother resented it and therefore would not again attend the chanyu court.
translations.idiomatic: That man's full brother nursed a grudge and boycotted court.

sentences.zh: 又單于病且死，謂諸貴人：「我子少，不能治國，立弟右谷蠡王。

translations.literal: Also when the chanyu was ill and about to die he told the nobles, "My son is young and cannot govern the state; establish my younger brother the right Guli king."
translations.idiomatic: Dying, Hulugu asked the nobles to pass the throne to his brother the right Guli king—his son was too young.

sentences.zh: 」及單于死，衛律等與顓渠閼氏謀，匿單于死，詐矯單于令，與貴人飲盟，更立子左谷蠡王為壺衍鞮單于。

translations.literal: " When the chanyu died, Wei Lu and others plotted with Yan wife Zhuanqu, hid the chanyu's death, forged a false chanyu order, drank covenant with the nobles, and changed the establishment to the son, the left Guli king, as Chanyu Huyandi.
translations.idiomatic: " After his death Wei Lu and the Zhuanqu queen hid the corpse, forged orders, and crowned the boy left Guli king as Huyandi."

sentences.zh: 是歲，始元二年也。

translations.literal: This year was the second year of Shiyuan.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Shiyuan 2.

sentences.zh: 壺衍鞮單于既立，風謂漢使者，言欲和親。

translations.literal: After Huyandi Chanyu was established, he hinted to Han envoys, saying he wished marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: The new Huyandi hinted through envoys that he wanted peace.

sentences.zh: 左賢王、右谷蠡王以不得立怨望，率其眾欲南歸漢。

translations.literal: The left Wise King and right Guli king, because they were not established, bore resentment, leading their hosts wishing to go south and submit to Han.
translations.idiomatic: The left Wise King and right Guli king, passed over for the throne, meant to defect south.

sentences.zh: 恐不能自致，即脅盧屠王，欲與西降烏孫，謀擊匈奴。

translations.literal: Fearing they could not deliver themselves, they then coerced King Lutü, wishing with him to go west and surrender to Wusun, plotting to strike the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: They strong-armed King Lutü into a plot to flee west to Wusun and stab the confederation.
