sentences.zh: 盧屠王告之，單于使人驗問，右谷蠡王不服，反以其罪罪盧屠王，國人皆冤之。

translations.literal: King Lutü reported it; the chanyu sent men to investigate; the right Guli king would not submit and instead charged Lutü with the crime; the people of the state all thought Lutü wronged.
translations.idiomatic: Lutü broke ranks and told the chanyu; the right Guli king reversed the blame onto him and outraged the tribes.

sentences.zh: 於是二王去居其所，未嘗肯會龍城。

translations.literal: Thereupon the two kings dwelt apart in their places and never would attend Dragon City.
translations.idiomatic: The two princes camped apart and boycotted the annual Dragon City muster.

sentences.zh: 後二年秋，匈奴入代，殺都尉。

translations.literal: Two years later in autumn the Xiongnu entered Dai and killed the commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Two autumns later they hit Dai and killed its commandant.

sentences.zh: 單于年少初立，母閼氏不正，國內乖離，常恐漢兵襲之。

translations.literal: The chanyu was young and newly established; the mother Yan wife was not upright; the state was split within and he constantly feared Han troops would strike him.
translations.idiomatic: The boy chanyu faced a dissolute queen mother and a split court, dreading Han attack.

sentences.zh: 於是衛律為單于謀：「穿井築城，治樓以藏谷，與秦人守之。

translations.literal: Thereupon Wei Lu schemed for the chanyu: "Dig wells, build walls, raise towers to store grain, and have Qin people guard them.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Lu advised walls, wells, granaries, and Han turncoat garrisons.

sentences.zh: 漢兵至，無奈我何。

translations.literal: " When Han troops arrive they cannot do anything to us."
translations.idiomatic: " Han cavalry would break on stone."

sentences.zh: 」即穿井數百，伐材數千。

translations.literal: " They then dug several hundred wells and felled several thousand timbers.
translations.idiomatic: " They sank hundreds of wells and felled thousands of logs."

sentences.zh: 或曰胡人不能守城，是遺漢糧也，衛律於是止，乃更謀歸漢使不降者蘇武、馬宏等。

translations.literal: Someone said the Hu cannot guard cities—this would be leaving Han grain; Wei Lu thereupon stopped, and instead plotted to return Han envoys who had not surrendered, such as Su Wu and Ma Hong.
translations.idiomatic: Critics said nomads cannot hold walls—that would only feed Han sieges; Wei Lu dropped the plan and offered to return Su Wu and Ma Hong instead.

sentences.zh: 馬宏者，前副光祿大夫王忠使西國，為匈奴所遮，忠戰死，馬宏生得，亦不肯降。

translations.literal: Ma Hong had been deputy to Superintendent of the Household Wang Zhong on mission to the western states; he was intercepted by the Xiongnu; Zhong died fighting; Ma Hong was captured alive and likewise would not surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Hong had followed Wang Zhong west, was cut off, saw Zhong die fighting, and refused to yield in captivity.

sentences.zh: 故匈奴歸此二人，欲以通善意。

translations.literal: Therefore the Xiongnu returned these two men, wishing thereby to convey good intent.
translations.idiomatic: Returning the two hostages was a gesture of goodwill.

sentences.zh: 是時，單於立三歲矣。

translations.literal: At that time the chanyu had been established three years.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu was three years on the throne.

sentences.zh: 明年，匈奴發左右部二萬騎，為四隊，並入邊為寇。

translations.literal: The next year the Xiongnu raised twenty thousand riders of the left and right wings in four columns, together entering the border as raiders.
translations.idiomatic: Next year they sent four columns of twenty thousand horse to raid.

sentences.zh: 漢兵追之，斬首獲虜九千人，生得甌脫王，漢無所失亡。

translations.literal: Han troops pursued them, beheading and capturing nine thousand, taking alive the Outuo king; Han had no losses.
translations.idiomatic: Han pursuit killed nine thousand, captured the Outuo king, and took no losses.

sentences.zh: 匈奴見甌脫王在漢，恐以為道擊之，即西北遠去，不敢南逐水草，發人民屯甌脫。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu, seeing the Outuo king was in Han, feared he would be used as a guide to strike them, thereupon fled far northwest, not daring to pursue water and grass southward, mobilizing people to garrison Outuo.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing the Outuo king would guide Han raids, they pulled northwest and garrisoned the buffer belt themselves.

sentences.zh: 明年，復遣九千騎屯受降城以備漢，北橋余吾，令可度，以備奔走。

translations.literal: The next year they again sent nine thousand riders to encamp at Surrender Acceptance city to prepare against Han, bridging the Yuwu northward so it could be crossed, to prepare for flight.
translations.idiomatic: They posted nine thousand men at Accept-Surrender and spanned the Yuwu for a retreat route.

sentences.zh: 是時，衛律已死。

translations.literal: At that time Wei Lu had already died.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Lu was dead by then.

sentences.zh: 衛律在時，常言和親之利，匈奴不信，及死後，兵數困，國益貧。

translations.literal: While Wei Lu lived he always spoke of the profit of marriage alliance; the Xiongnu did not believe; after he died troops were repeatedly trapped and the state grew poorer.
translations.idiomatic: Alive, Wei Lu had preached heqin to deaf ears; after his death defeats and poverty mounted.

sentences.zh: 單于弟左谷蠡王思衛律言，欲和親而恐漢不聽，故不肯先言，常使左右風漢使者。

translations.literal: The chanyu's younger brother the left Guli king recalled Wei Lu's words, wished marriage alliance but feared Han would not listen, therefore would not speak first, constantly having those at his side hint to Han envoys.
translations.idiomatic: The left Guli king remembered Wei Lu's counsel and nudged Han envoys toward peace without speaking first.

sentences.zh: 然其侵盜益希，遇漢使愈厚，欲以漸致和親，漢亦羈縻之。

translations.literal: Yet their invasions and pillaging grew rarer; meeting Han envoys they grew ever more generous, wishing thereby gradually to reach marriage alliance; Han also held them on leading strings.
translations.idiomatic: Raids thinned, gifts to envoys fattened, and Han played along with loose reins.

sentences.zh: 其後，左谷蠡王死。

translations.literal: Afterward the left Guli king died.
translations.idiomatic: Then the left Guli king died.

sentences.zh: 明年，單于使犁汙王窺邊，言酒泉、張掖兵益弱，出兵試擊，冀可復得其地。

translations.literal: The next year the chanyu sent King Liwu to spy on the border, saying Jiuquan and Zhangye troops were growing weaker, to send troops on a trial strike in hope of recovering that territory.
translations.idiomatic: He sent King Liwu to scout whether Jiuquan and Zhangye could be retaken.

sentences.zh: 時漢先得降者，聞其計，天子詔邊警備。

translations.literal: At that time Han had first obtained a defector and heard their plan; the Son of Heaven ordered the border on alert.
translations.idiomatic: A defector warned Han; the emperor put the garrisons on alert.

sentences.zh: 後無幾，右賢王、犁汙王四千騎分三隊，入日勒、屋蘭、番和。

translations.literal: Before long the right Wise King and King Liwu with four thousand riders in three columns entered Rile, Wulan, and Fanhe.
translations.idiomatic: Soon four thousand horse in three files hit Rile, Wulan, and Fanhe.

sentences.zh: 張掖太守、屬國都尉發兵擊，大破之，得脫者數百人。

translations.literal: The Zhangye grand administrator and dependent-state commandant raised troops and struck them, greatly defeating them; several hundred escaped.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangye officials counterattacked, shattering the raid; a few hundred escaped.

sentences.zh: 屬國千長義渠王騎士射殺犁B028王，賜黃金二百斤，馬二百匹，因封為犁汙王。

translations.literal: A dependent-state thousand-chief of the Yiqu king's riders shot and killed King Liwu; he was granted two hundred jin of gold and two hundred horses, and therefore was enfeoffed King Liwu.
translations.idiomatic: A Yiqu thousand-chief slew King Liwu and was rewarded with gold, horses, and the Liwu title.

sentences.zh: 屬國都尉郭忠封成安侯。

translations.literal: Dependent-state Commandant Guo Zhong was enfeoffed marquis of Cheng'an.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Zhong became marquis of Cheng'an.

sentences.zh: 自是後，匈奴不敢入張掖。

translations.literal: From this on the Xiongnu did not dare enter Zhangye.
translations.idiomatic: Zhangye saw no more Xiongnu columns.

sentences.zh: 其明年，匈奴三千餘騎入五原，略殺數千人，後數萬騎南旁塞獵，行攻塞外亭障，略取吏民去。

translations.literal: The next year more than three thousand Xiongnu riders entered Wuyuan, plundering and killing several thousand; afterward several tens of thousands of riders hunted south beside the barrier, advancing to attack posts and barriers beyond the barrier, plundering and taking clerks and commoners away.
translations.idiomatic: Next year three thousand hit Wuyuan; later tens of thousands "hunted" along the wall, snapping up outposts and captives.

sentences.zh: 是時，漢邊郡烽火候望精明，匈奴為邊寇者少利，希復犬已塞。

translations.literal: At that time Han border commanderies' beacon watch was keen and clear; the Xiongnu gained little profit as border raiders and rarely again violated the barrier.
translations.idiomatic: Sharp beacon discipline made raids unprofitable; the wall was rarely breached.

sentences.zh: 漢復得匈奴降者，言烏桓嘗發先單于塚，匈奴怨之，方發二萬騎擊烏桓。

translations.literal: Han again obtained a Xiongnu defector who said the Wuhuan had once dug up a former chanyu's tomb and the Xiongnu resented it, and were just then raising twenty thousand riders to strike the Wuhuan.
translations.idiomatic: A turncoat reported a Xiongnu expedition to punish Wuhuan for grave-robbing.

sentences.zh: 大將軍霍光欲發兵邀擊之，以問護軍都尉趙充國。

translations.literal: Grand General Huo Guang wished to raise troops to intercept them and asked Colonel-protector Zhao Chongguo.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Guang asked Zhao Chongguo whether to ambush the column.

sentences.zh: 充國以為：「烏桓間數犬已塞，今匈奴擊之，於漢便。

translations.literal: Chongguo thought: "The Wuhuan in recent years has several times entered the barrier; now the Xiongnu strike them—it is convenient for Han.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Chongguo argued that the Wuhuan had repeatedly crossed inside the wall and that letting the Xiongnu punish them served Han's interest on the border.

sentences.zh: 又匈奴希寇盜，北邊幸無事。

translations.literal: " Moreover the Xiongnu rarely pillage now; the northern border fortunately has no affairs.
translations.idiomatic: " Xiongnu raids have slackened; the north is quiet."

sentences.zh: 蠻夷自相攻擊，而發兵要之，招寇生事，非計也。

translations.literal: " For barbarians to attack each other and we raise troops to intercept them summons raiders and creates trouble—it is not a good plan."
translations.idiomatic: " Cutting in on tribal war only invites new trouble."

sentences.zh: 」光更問中郎將范明友，明友言可擊。

translations.literal: " Guang again asked Palace Gentleman Fan Mingyou; Mingyou said they could be struck.
translations.idiomatic: " Huo Guang asked Fan Mingyou, who said strike anyway."

sentences.zh: 於是拜明友為度遼將軍，將二萬騎出遼東。

translations.literal: Thereupon he appointed Mingyou general who crosses the Liao, leading twenty thousand riders out from Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Mingyou took twenty thousand cavalry from Liaodong as "Cross-Liao" general.

sentences.zh: 匈奴聞漢兵至，引去。

translations.literal: When the Xiongnu heard Han troops had arrived, they led away.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu heard and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 初，光誡朋友：「兵不空出，即後匈奴，遂擊烏桓。

translations.literal: Earlier Guang had warned Mingyou: "Troops must not go out for nothing; if you miss the Xiongnu, then strike the Wuhuan."
translations.idiomatic: Huo Guang had ordered him not to return empty-handed—if the Xiongnu escaped, hit the Wuhuan.

sentences.zh: 」烏桓時新中匈奴兵，明友既後匈奴，因乘烏桓敝，擊之，斬首六千餘級，獲三王首，還，封為平陵侯。

translations.literal: " The Wuhuan had then newly suffered Xiongnu troops; Mingyou, having missed the Xiongnu, therefore took advantage of the Wuhuan's exhaustion, struck them, beheading more than six thousand ranks, obtaining three kings' heads, returned, and was enfeoffed marquis of Pingling.
translations.idiomatic: The Wuhuan were already bloodied; Mingyou slaughtered six thousand, took three royal heads, and won the Pingling marquisate.

sentences.zh: 匈奴由是恐，不能出兵。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu thereupon feared and could not send troops.
translations.idiomatic: The scare left the Xiongnu unable to mount another host.

sentences.zh: 即使使之烏孫，求欲得漢公主。

translations.literal: Even if sending envoys to Wusun, they sought to obtain a Han princess.
translations.idiomatic: They still pressed Wusun for a Han princess.

sentences.zh: 擊烏孫，取車延、惡師地。

translations.literal: They struck Wusun, taking the lands of Cheyan and Wushi.
translations.idiomatic: They seized Cheyan and Wushi from Wusun.

sentences.zh: 烏孫公主上書，下公卿議救，未決。

translations.literal: The Wusun princess memorialized upward; it went down to the dukes and ministers to deliberate rescue, undecided.
translations.idiomatic: The Wusun princess begged for rescue; the court debated and stalled.

sentences.zh: 昭帝崩，宣帝即位，烏孫昆彌復上書言：「連為匈奴所侵削，昆彌願發國半精兵人馬五萬匹，盡力擊匈奴，唯天子出兵，哀救公主！

translations.literal: Emperor Zhao died; Emperor Xuan took the throne; the Wusun Kunmi again memorialized, saying, "We have continuously been invaded and whittled away by the Xiongnu; the Kunmi wishes to raise half the state's picked troops, fifty thousand men and horses, to strike the Xiongnu with full force—only let the Son of Heaven send troops and pity us to save the princess!"
translations.idiomatic: When Zhao Di died and Xuan Di rose, the Wusun Kunmi pleaded for fifty thousand allied horse if Han would march to save the princess.

sentences.zh: 」本始二年，漢大發關東輕銳士，選郡國吏三百石伉健習騎射者，皆從軍。

translations.literal: " In the second year of Ben shi Han greatly raised light sharp troops from east of the passes, selecting from commanderies and kingdoms clerks of three hundred piculs and upward who were stalwart and practiced riding and archery, all to follow the army.
translations.idiomatic: " In Ben shi 2 Han drafted fit archers above three hundred piculs from the eastern commanderies."

sentences.zh: 遣御史大夫田廣明為祁連將軍，四萬餘騎，出西河；

translations.literal: They sent Imperial Counsellor Tian Guangming as Qilian general with more than forty thousand riders out from Xihe;
translations.idiomatic: Tian Guangming led forty thousand from Xihe as Qilian general;

sentences.zh: 度遼將軍范明友三萬餘騎，出張掖；

translations.literal: General who crosses the Liao Fan Mingyou with more than thirty thousand riders out from Zhangye;
translations.idiomatic: Fan Mingyou took thirty thousand from Zhangye;

sentences.zh: 前將軍韓增三萬餘騎，出雲中；

translations.literal: General of the Front Han Zeng with more than thirty thousand riders out from Yunzhong;
translations.idiomatic: Han Zeng took thirty thousand from Yunzhong;

sentences.zh: 後將軍趙充國為蒲類將軍，三萬餘騎，出酒泉；

translations.literal: General of the Rear Zhao Chongguo as Pulei general with more than thirty thousand riders out from Jiuquan;
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Chongguo marched as Pulei general from Jiuquan;

sentences.zh: 雲中太守田順為虎牙將軍，三萬餘騎，出五原：凡五將軍，兵十餘萬騎，出塞各二千餘里。

translations.literal: Yunzhong Grand Administrator Tian Shun as Tigerya general with more than thirty thousand riders out from Wuyuan: in all five generals, more than a hundred thousand riders, each going out of the barrier more than two thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shun led thirty thousand from Wuyuan as Tigerya general—five columns, over a hundred thousand horse, each beyond two thousand li.

sentences.zh: 及校尉常惠使護髮兵烏孫西域，昆彌自將翕侯以下五萬餘騎從西方入，與五將軍兵凡二十餘萬眾。

translations.literal: And Colonel Chang Hui was sent to direct and protect Wusun troops in the Western Regions; the Kunmi personally led more than fifty thousand riders from Marquis Xi downward from the west to enter; together with the five generals' troops they were more than two hundred thousand in all.
translations.idiomatic: Chang Hui took charge of the Wusun and western auxiliaries; the Kunmi led fifty thousand from the Xi marquis down from the west to join the Han columns for over two hundred thousand in all.

sentences.zh: 匈奴聞漢兵大出，老弱奔走，驅畜產遠遁逃，是以五將少所得。

translations.literal: When the Xiongnu heard Han troops were going out in force, old and weak fled, driving herds far to escape; therefore the five generals gained little.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu drove noncombatants and herds away; the five Han columns found thin pickings.

sentences.zh: 度遼將軍出塞千二百餘里，至蒲離候水，斬首捕虜七百餘級，鹵獲馬、牛、羊萬餘。

translations.literal: The general who crosses the Liao went out of the barrier more than twelve hundred li to Pulihou water, beheading and capturing more than seven hundred ranks, plundering more than ten thousand horses, cattle, and sheep.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Mingyou reached Pulihou water for seven hundred kills and ten thousand head of stock.

sentences.zh: 前將軍出塞千二百餘里，至烏員，斬首捕虜，至候山百餘級，鹵馬、牛、羊二千餘。

translations.literal: The general of the front went out of the barrier more than twelve hundred li to Wuyuan, beheading and capturing to Mount Hou more than a hundred ranks, plundering more than two thousand horses, cattle, and sheep.
translations.idiomatic: Han Zeng reached Wuyuan and Mount Hou for a hundred-odd kills and two thousand animals.

sentences.zh: 蒲類將軍兵當與烏孫合擊匈奴蒲類澤，烏孫先期至而去，漢兵不與相及。

translations.literal: The Pulei general's troops were to join Wusun in jointly striking the Xiongnu at Pulei marsh; Wusun arrived ahead of time and departed, and Han troops did not catch up.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Chongguo was to link with Wusun at Pulei Lake, but the allies left early and Han missed the rendezvous.

sentences.zh: 蒲類將軍出塞千八百餘里，西去候山，斬首捕虜，得單于使者蒲陰王以下三百餘級，鹵馬、牛、羊七千餘。

translations.literal: The Pulei general went out of the barrier more than eighteen hundred li, west to Mount Hou, beheading and capturing more than three hundred ranks from the chanyu's envoy King Puyin downward, plundering more than seven thousand horses, cattle, and sheep.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Chongguo still took three hundred heads including King Puyin and seven thousand beasts west of Mount Hou.

sentences.zh: 聞虜已引去，皆不至期還。

translations.literal: Hearing the captives had already led away, all did not reach the appointed time in returning.
translations.idiomatic: Learning the enemy had gone, columns turned home short of their deadlines.

sentences.zh: 天子蒲其過，寬而不罪。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven pardoned their fault, being lenient and not punishing.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan Di forgave the missed deadlines.

sentences.zh: 祁連將軍出塞千六百里，至雞秩山，斬首捕虜十九級，獲牛、馬、羊百餘。

translations.literal: The Qilian general went out of the barrier sixteen hundred li to Mount Jizhi, beheading and capturing nineteen ranks, obtaining more than a hundred cattle, sheep, and horses.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Guangming reached Jizhi Mountain for nineteen kills and a hundred-odd animals.

sentences.zh: 逢漢使匈奴還者冉弘等，言雞秩山西有虜眾，祁連即戒弘，使言無虜，欲還兵。

translations.literal: He met Han envoys returning from the Xiongnu such as Ran Hong, who said west of Jizhi Mountain there was a mass of captives; the Qilian thereupon warned Hong, making him say there were no captives, wishing to return troops.
translations.idiomatic: He met returnee Ran Hong, who reported Xiongnu west of Jizhi; Tian Guangming silenced him to mask the enemy and withdraw.

sentences.zh: 御史屬公孫益壽諫，以為不可，祁連不聽，遂引兵還。

translations.literal: Imperial clerk Gongsun Yishou remonstrated that it could not be done; the Qilian would not listen and thereupon led troops back.
translations.idiomatic: Clerk Gongsun Yishou protested in vain; Tian Guangming retreated anyway.

sentences.zh: 虎牙將軍出塞八百餘里，至丹余吾水上，即止兵不進，斬首捕虜千九百餘級，鹵馬、牛、羊七萬餘，引兵還。

translations.literal: The Tigerya general went out of the barrier more than eight hundred li to the Dan Yuwu waters, then halted troops and did not advance, beheading and capturing more than nineteen hundred ranks, plundering more than seventy thousand horses, cattle, and sheep, and led troops back.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Shun stopped at Dan Yuwu, still claiming nineteen hundred kills and seventy thousand animals.

sentences.zh: 上以虎牙將軍不至期，詐增鹵獲，而祁連知虜在前，逗留不進，皆下吏自殺。

translations.literal: The sovereign because the Tigerya general had not reached the appointed time and falsely increased plunder, and because the Qilian knew captives were ahead yet dallied and did not advance, sent both down to clerks and they committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor jailed both generals for false counts and cowardice; they killed themselves in prison.

sentences.zh: 擢公孫益壽為侍御史。

translations.literal: He promoted Gongsun Yishou to attendant clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Yishou was raised to attendant clerk.

sentences.zh: 校尉常惠與烏孫兵至右谷蠡庭，獲單于父行及嫂、居次、名王、犁汙都尉、千長、將以下三萬九千餘級，虜馬、牛、羊、驢、騾、橐駝七十餘萬。

translations.literal: Colonel Chang Hui with Wusun troops reached the right Guli king's court, capturing the chanyu's father's generation, sister-in-law, junior wives, famous kings, Liwu commandant, thousand-chiefs, generals downward more than thirty-nine thousand ranks, and captive horses, cattle, sheep, donkeys, mules, and camels more than seven hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Chang Hui and Wusun stormed the right Guli court for thirty-nine thousand counted dead and seven hundred thousand head of livestock.

sentences.zh: 漢封惠為長羅侯。

translations.literal: Han enfeoffed Hui as marquis of Changluo.
translations.idiomatic: Han made Chang Hui marquis of Changluo.

sentences.zh: 然匈奴民眾死傷而去者，及畜產遠移死亡不可勝數。

translations.literal: Yet of the Xiongnu people wounded and dead who fled, and livestock moved far that died, the count could not be reckoned.
translations.idiomatic: Unnumbered Xiongnu and beasts died in the stampede beyond the tally.

sentences.zh: 於是匈奴遂衰耗，怨烏孫。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Xiongnu was weakened and exhausted and resented Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: The confederation staggered and blamed Wusun.

sentences.zh: 其冬，單于自將萬騎擊烏孫，頗得老弱，欲還。

translations.literal: That winter the chanyu personally led ten thousand riders to strike Wusun, taking quite a few old and weak, wishing to return.
translations.idiomatic: That winter the chanyu struck Wusun for captives, then turned for home.

sentences.zh: 會天大雨雪，一日深丈餘，人民畜產凍死，還者不能什一。

translations.literal: It happened that heaven sent great rain and snow one day more than a zhang deep; people and livestock froze to death; of those who returned not one in ten.
translations.idiomatic: A day's blizzard deeper than three meters froze nine men and beasts in ten.

sentences.zh: 於是丁令乘弱攻其北，烏桓入其東，烏孫擊其西。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Dingling, taking advantage of weakness, attacked their north; the Wuhuan entered their east; Wusun struck their west.
translations.idiomatic: Dingling hit them from the north, Wuhuan from the east, Wusun from the west.

sentences.zh: 凡三國所殺數萬級，馬數萬匹，牛、羊甚眾。

translations.literal: In all the three states killed several tens of thousands of ranks, several tens of thousands of horses, and very many cattle and sheep.
translations.idiomatic: The three allies stacked tens of thousands of kills and beasts.

sentences.zh: 又重以餓死，人民死者什三，畜產什五，匈奴大虛弱，諸國羈屬者皆瓦解，攻盜不能理。

translations.literal: Moreover famine piled on; of the people three tenths died, of livestock five tenths; the Xiongnu was greatly hollowed and weak; all states in leading strings broke apart, and attack and pillage could not be managed.
translations.idiomatic: Starvation added to the toll—thirty percent of the people, fifty percent of the herds; tributary tribes revolted and order collapsed.

sentences.zh: 其後漢出三千餘騎，為三道，並入匈奴，捕虜得數千人還。

translations.literal: Afterward Han sent out more than three thousand riders in three columns, together entering the Xiongnu, capturing several thousand and returning.
translations.idiomatic: Later Han sent three columns of three thousand horse to snatch thousands of captives.

sentences.zh: 匈奴終不敢取當，茲欲鄉和親，而邊境少事矣。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu to the end did not dare take them on; from this they wished to incline toward marriage alliance, and border affairs were few.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu would not risk battle and edged toward heqin while the border grew quiet.

sentences.zh: 壺衍鞮單于立十七年死，弟左賢王立，為虛閭權渠單于。

translations.literal: Huyandi Chanyu died after seventeen years of establishment; his younger brother the left Wise King was established as Chanyu Xulüquanqu.
translations.idiomatic: Huyandi died after seventeen years; his brother the left Wise King became Xulüquanqu.

sentences.zh: 是歲，地節二年也。

translations.literal: This year was the second year of Dijie.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Dijie 2.

sentences.zh: 虛閭權渠單于立，以右大將女為大閼氏，而黜前單于所幸顓渠閼氏。

translations.literal: When Xulüquanqu Chanyu was established, he took the right grand general's daughter as great Yan wife and dismissed the former chanyu's favored Zhuanqu Yan wife.
translations.idiomatic: Xulüquanqu married the right general's daughter as chief queen and demoted Zhuanqu.

sentences.zh: 顓渠閼氏父左大且渠怨望。

translations.literal: The Zhuanqu Yan wife's father, the left grand Daqu, bore resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuanqu's father, the left grand Daqu, nursed a grudge.

sentences.zh: 是時，匈奴不能為邊寇，於是漢罷外城，以休百姓。

translations.literal: At that time the Xiongnu could not raid the border; thereupon Han dismantled the outer walls to rest the common people.
translations.idiomatic: With the Xiongnu unable to raid, Han tore down forward outposts to spare the people.

sentences.zh: 單于聞之喜，召貴人謀，欲與漢和親。

translations.literal: When the chanyu heard it he was pleased, summoned nobles to deliberate, and wished to make marriage alliance with Han.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu rejoiced and called a council to pursue heqin.

sentences.zh: 左大且渠心害其事，曰：「前漢使來，兵隨其後，今亦效漢發兵，先使使者入。

translations.literal: The left grand Daqu inwardly resented the affair, saying, "Formerly when Han envoys came, troops followed behind them; now let us likewise imitate Han by raising troops, first sending envoys in."
translations.idiomatic: The left grand Daqu blocked the plan: "Han envoys always brought armies; we should send envoys first, then strike like Han."

sentences.zh: 」乃自請與呼盧訾王各將萬騎南旁塞獵，相逢俱入。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he himself requested with King Huluzi each to lead ten thousand riders to hunt south beside the barrier, meeting and together entering.
translations.idiomatic: " He and King Huluzi each took ten thousand horse to "hunt" along the wall and meet inside."

sentences.zh: 行未到，會三騎亡降漢，言匈奴欲為寇。

translations.literal: Before they had gone, it happened that three riders fled and surrendered to Han, saying the Xiongnu wished to raid.
translations.idiomatic: Three scouts defected first and warned Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 於是天子詔發邊騎屯要害處，使大將軍軍監治眾等四人將五千騎，分三隊，出塞各數百里，捕得虜各數十人而還。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Son of Heaven ordered border cavalry to encamp at critical points, sending four army supervisors of the grand general such as Zhi Zhong each leading five thousand riders in three columns out of the barrier several hundred li each, capturing several tens of captives each and returning.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor rushed border horse to chokepoints and sent four grand-general supervisors with five thousand riders each in three sweeps beyond the wall.

sentences.zh: 時匈奴亡其三騎，不敢入，即引去。

translations.literal: At that time the Xiongnu had lost those three riders and did not dare enter, thereupon led away.
translations.idiomatic: Missing three scouts, the Xiongnu called off the thrust.

sentences.zh: 是歲也，匈奴饑，人民畜產死十六七。

translations.literal: In this year too the Xiongnu starved; of people and livestock six or seven tenths died.
translations.idiomatic: Famine killed six or seven of every ten people and beasts that year.

sentences.zh: 又發兩屯各萬騎以備漢。

translations.literal: They also raised two encampments of ten thousand riders each to prepare against Han.
translations.idiomatic: They still posted two ten-thousand camps against Han.

sentences.zh: 其秋，匈奴前所得西嗕居左地者，其君長以下數千人皆驅畜產行，與甌脫戰，所戰殺傷甚眾，遂南降漢。

translations.literal: That autumn those the Xiongnu had earlier taken from the western tribes dwelling in the left zone—from chiefs downward several thousand—all drove livestock on the march, fought the Outuo, killed and wounded very many in the fighting, and thereupon went south to surrender to Han.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn thousands of western captives the Xiongnu had parked in the east broke out, fought their Outuo guards, and defected south to Han.

sentences.zh: 其明年，西域城郭共擊匈奴，取車師國，得其王及人眾而去。

translations.literal: The next year the walled states of the Western Regions jointly struck the Xiongnu, took the state of Cheshi, obtained its king and people and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Next year the Tarim city-states allied against the Xiongnu, seized Cheshi, and carried off its king.

sentences.zh: 單于復以車師王昆弟兜莫為車師王，收其餘民東徙，不敢居故地。

translations.literal: The chanyu again made Doumo, younger brother of the Cheshi king, king of Cheshi, gathered the remaining people and moved them east, not daring to dwell in the old land.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu installed the king's brother Doumo and shifted the survivors east of their old pastures.

sentences.zh: 而漢益遣屯士分田車師地以實之。

translations.literal: And Han increasingly sent garrison colonists to divide and farm Cheshi lands to fill them.
translations.idiomatic: Han poured in military farmers to colonize Cheshi.

sentences.zh: 其明年，匈奴怨諸國共擊車師，遣左右大將各萬餘騎屯田右地，欲以侵迫烏孫西域。

translations.literal: The next year the Xiongnu resented the states for jointly striking Cheshi, sending left and right grand generals each with more than ten thousand riders to farm-colonize the western zone, wishing thereby to press Wusun and the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the Xiongnu sent two grand generals west to garrison-farm and squeeze Wusun.

sentences.zh: 後二歲，匈奴遣左右奧鞬各六千騎，與左大將再擊漢之田車師城者，不能下。

translations.literal: Two years later the Xiongnu sent left and right Aojian six thousand riders each, again with the left grand general striking Han's colonists at Cheshi city, but could not take it.
translations.idiomatic: Two years on a joint strike on Han's Cheshi fort failed.

sentences.zh: 其明年，丁令比三歲入盜匈奴，殺略人民數千，驅馬畜去。

translations.literal: The next year the Dingling for three years running entered to pillage the Xiongnu, killing and plundering several thousand people and driving off horses and herds.
translations.idiomatic: The Dingling raided three years straight, taking thousands of heads and herds.

sentences.zh: 匈奴遣萬餘騎往擊之，無所得。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu sent more than ten thousand riders to strike them and obtained nothing.
translations.idiomatic: A Xiongnu counterforce came home empty-handed.

sentences.zh: 其明年，單于將十萬餘騎旁塞獵，欲入邊寇。

translations.literal: The next year the chanyu led more than a hundred thousand riders hunting beside the barrier, wishing to enter and raid.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the chanyu massed a hundred thousand horse along the wall for another raid.

sentences.zh: 未至，會其民題除渠堂亡降漢言狀，漢以為言兵鹿奚盧侯，而遣後將軍趙充國將兵四萬餘騎屯緣邊九郡備虜。

translations.literal: Before arriving, it happened that his subject Tichu Qutang fled and surrendered to Han explaining the situation; Han thereupon enfeoffed him with a marquis title for his report and sent General of the Rear Zhao Chongguo with more than forty thousand riders to encamp along nine border commanderies to prepare against the captives.
translations.idiomatic: Before the blow fell a herdsman named Tichu Qutang defected with the plan; Han rewarded him with a marquisate for the warning and posted Zhao Chongguo with forty thousand cavalry across nine border commanderies.

sentences.zh: 月余，單于病歐血，因不敢入，還去，即罷兵。

translations.literal: After more than a month the chanyu vomited blood from illness; therefore he did not dare enter, led away, and thereupon dismissed troops.
translations.idiomatic: A month later the chanyu spat blood, abandoned the campaign, and demobilized.

sentences.zh: 乃使題王都犁胡次等入漢，請和親，未報，會單于死。

translations.literal: He then sent King Duli Huci and others into Han to request marriage alliance; before there was answer, it happened the chanyu died.
translations.idiomatic: He sent envoys to beg peace but died before an answer came.

sentences.zh: 是歲，神爵二年也。

translations.literal: This year was the second year of Shenjue.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Shenjue 2.

sentences.zh: 虛閭權渠單于立九年死。

translations.literal: Xulüquanqu Chanyu died after nine years of establishment.
translations.idiomatic: Xulüquanqu died in his ninth year.

sentences.zh: 自始立而黜顓渠閼氏，顓渠閼氏即與右賢王私通。

translations.literal: From the time he was first established he had dismissed Yan wife Zhuanqu; Yan wife Zhuanqu thereupon committed adultery with the right Wise King.
translations.idiomatic: He had demoted queen Zhuanqu; she took the right Wise King as lover.

sentences.zh: 右賢王會龍城而去，顓渠閼氏語以單于病甚，且勿遠。

translations.literal: The right Wise King attended Dragon City and was about to leave; Yan wife Zhuanqu told him the chanyu was very ill—do not go far yet.
translations.idiomatic: At the annual Dragon City rite she begged him to stay, claiming the chanyu was dying.

sentences.zh: 後數日，單于死。

translations.literal: Several days later the chanyu died.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu died days later.

sentences.zh: 郝宿王刑未央使人召諸王，未至，顓渠閼氏與其弟左大且渠都隆奇謀，立右賢王屠耆堂為握衍朐鞮單于。

translations.literal: King Haosu Xing Weiyang of the right sent men to summon the various kings; before they arrived, Yan wife Zhuanqu plotted with her younger brother the left grand Daqu Dulongqi and established the right Wise King Tuqitang as Chanyu Woyanqudi.
translations.idiomatic: Before the princes gathered, Zhuanqu and her brother Dulongqi crowned the right Wise King Tuqitang as Woyanqudi.

sentences.zh: 握衍朐鞮單于者，代父為右賢王，烏維單于耳孫也。

translations.literal: Woyanqudi Chanyu had succeeded his father as right Wise King; he was a great-grandson of Chanyu Wuvie.
translations.idiomatic: Woyanqudi was Wuvie's descendant who had inherited the right Wise kingship.

sentences.zh: 握衍朐鞮單于立，復修和親，遣弟伊酋若王勝之入漢獻見。

translations.literal: When Woyanqudi Chanyu was established, he again cultivated marriage alliance, sending his younger brother King Yiqure Shengzhi into Han to present tribute and audience.
translations.idiomatic: Woyanqudi reopened heqin and sent his brother Shengzhi to Chang'an with tribute.

sentences.zh: 單于初立，凶惡，盡殺虛閭權渠時用事貴人刑未央等，而任用顓渠閼氏弟都隆奇，又盡免虛閭權渠子弟近親，而自以其子弟代之。

translations.literal: When the chanyu had first been established he was fierce and cruel, killing to the last the powerful nobles such as Xing Weiyang who had held office under Xulüquanqu, while employing Zhuanqu Yan wife's younger brother Dulongqi, also wholly dismissing Xulüquanqu's sons and younger kin near the throne and replacing them with his own sons and younger kin.
translations.idiomatic: His accession was a blood purge of Xulüquanqu's ministers, a rise for Dulongqi, and a swap of the royal household for his own kin.

sentences.zh: 虛閭權渠單于子稽侯犬冊既不得立，亡歸妻父烏禪幕。

translations.literal: The son of Xulüquanqu Chanyu, Jihou Shansi, having failed to be established, fled to his father-in-law Wuchanmu.
translations.idiomatic: Xulüquanqu's heir Jihou Shansi fled to his father-in-law Wuchanmu when passed over.

sentences.zh: 烏禪幕者，本烏孫、康居間小國，數見侵暴，率其眾數千人降匈奴，狐鹿姑單于以其弟子日逐王姊妻之，使長其眾，居右地。

translations.literal: Wuchanmu had originally been a small state between Wusun and Kangju, several times seeing invasion and violence; he led his host of several thousand to surrender to the Xiongnu; Chanyu Hulugu had married him to the younger sister of the Rizhu king's son and ordered him to grow his host, dwelling in the western zone.
translations.idiomatic: Wuchanmu led a small people from between Wusun and Kangju into Xiongnu protection; Hulugu married him to Rizhu royalty and let him rule the western tribes.

sentences.zh: 日逐王選賢撣，其父左賢王當為單于，讓狐鹿姑單于，狐鹿姑單于許立之。

translations.literal: The Rizhu king chose Xianxian Chan; his father the left Wise King ought to have been chanyu but yielded to Chanyu Hulugu, and Chanyu Hulugu promised to establish him.
translations.idiomatic: The Rizhu king had picked Xianxian Chan whose father had yielded the throne to Hulugu with a promise of succession.

sentences.zh: 國人以故頗言日逐王當為單于。

translations.literal: For this reason the people of the state often said the Rizhu king ought to be chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: The tribes still whispered that the Rizhu line deserved the throne.

sentences.zh: 日逐王素與握衍朐鞮單于有隙，即率其眾數萬騎歸漢。

translations.literal: The Rizhu king had long been at odds with Woyanqudi Chanyu, thereupon leading his host of several tens of thousands of riders to submit to Han.
translations.idiomatic: At feud with Woyanqudi, the Rizhu king brought tens of thousands of horse to Han.

sentences.zh: 漢封日逐王為歸德侯。

translations.literal: Han enfeoffed the Rizhu king as marquis of Guide.
translations.idiomatic: Han made him marquis of Guide.

sentences.zh: 單于更立其從兄薄胥堂為日逐王。

translations.literal: The chanyu again established his elder male cousin Boxutang as Rizhu king.
translations.idiomatic: Woyanqudi replaced him with a cousin, Boxutang, as Rizhu king.

sentences.zh: 明年，單于又殺先賢撣兩弟。

translations.literal: The next year the chanyu again killed two younger brothers of Xianxian Chan.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he executed two of Xianxian Chan's brothers.

sentences.zh: 烏禪幕請之，不聽，心恚。

translations.literal: Wuchanmu pleaded for them; he would not listen; Wuchanmu was resentful in heart.
translations.idiomatic: Wuchanmu's pleas failed and he nursed a grudge.

sentences.zh: 其後左奧鞬王死，單于自立其小子為奧鞬王，留庭。

translations.literal: Afterward when the left Aojian king died, the chanyu set up his own young son as Aojian king, keeping him at court.
translations.idiomatic: When the left Aojian king died, Woyanqudi kept the title for a toddler at court.

sentences.zh: 奧鞬貴人共立故奧鞬王子為王，與俱東徙。

translations.literal: The Aojian nobles jointly established the former Aojian king's son as king and moved east with him.
translations.idiomatic: The Aojian nobles crowned the old king's son and marched east.

sentences.zh: 單于遣右丞相將萬騎往擊之，失亡數千人，不勝。

translations.literal: The chanyu sent the right chancellor leading ten thousand riders to strike them, losing several thousand dead and missing, not victorious.
translations.idiomatic: His punitive column lost thousands and failed.

sentences.zh: 時單于已立二歲，暴虐殺伐，國中不附。

translations.literal: At that time the chanyu had already been established two years, cruel and violent in killing, and the state did not cleave to him.
translations.idiomatic: Two years of tyranny had cost him the realm's loyalty.

sentences.zh: 及太子、左賢王數讒左地貴人，左地貴人皆怨。

translations.literal: Moreover the heir apparent and left Wise King repeatedly slandered the nobles of the left zone, and the left-zone nobles all resented it.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince and left Wise King slandered the eastern lords until every eastern noble hated the throne.

sentences.zh: 其明年，烏桓擊匈奴東邊姑夕王，頗得人民，單于怒。

translations.literal: The next year the Wuhuan struck the Xiongnu eastern border king Guxi, taking quite a few people; the chanyu was angry.
translations.idiomatic: Wuhuan raiders hit King Guxi and enraged the chanyu.

sentences.zh: 姑夕王恐，即與烏禪幕及左地貴人共立稽侯犬冊為呼韓邪單于，發左地兵四五萬人，西擊握衍朐鞮單于，至姑且水北。

translations.literal: King Guxi feared, thereupon with Wuchanmu and the left-zone nobles jointly established Jihou Shansi as Chanyu Hanxie, raising forty or fifty thousand troops of the left zone, striking west at Woyanqudi Chanyu, reaching north of the Guqie River.
translations.idiomatic: Guxi, Wuchanmu, and the eastern lords raised Jihou Shansi as Hanxie with forty or fifty thousand men and marched on Woyanqudi.

sentences.zh: 未戰，握衍朐鞮單于兵敗走，使人報其弟右賢王曰：「匈奴共攻我，若肯發兵助我乎？

translations.literal: Before battle, Woyanqudi Chanyu's army was defeated and fled; he sent someone to report to his younger brother the right Wise King, saying, "The Xiongnu jointly attack me—will you be willing to raise troops to aid me?"
translations.idiomatic: Woyanqudi's line broke before the clash; he begged his brother the right Wise King for rescue.

sentences.zh: 」右賢王曰：「若不愛人，殺昆弟諸貴人。

translations.literal: " The right Wise King said, "You do not love people, killing younger brothers and the various nobles.
translations.idiomatic: " The right Wise King answered, "You slaughtered your kin and nobles without mercy."

sentences.zh: 各自死若處，無來污我。

translations.literal: " Each die in his own place—do not come and defile me."
translations.idiomatic: " Die where you deserve—do not drag me into your filth."

sentences.zh: 」握衍朐鞮單于恚，自殺。

translations.literal: " Woyanqudi Chanyu was resentful and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Woyanqudi killed himself in rage.

sentences.zh: 左大且渠都隆奇亡之右賢王所，其民眾盡降呼韓邪單于。

translations.literal: The left grand Daqu Dulongqi fled to the right Wise King's place; his people all surrendered to Chanyu Hanxie.
translations.idiomatic: Dulongqi fled to the western wing while the eastern tribes went over to Hanxie.

sentences.zh: 是歲，神爵四年也。

translations.literal: This year was the fourth year of Shenjue.
translations.idiomatic: The year was Shenjue 4.

sentences.zh: 握衍朐鞮單于立三年而敗。

translations.literal: Woyanqudi Chanyu was defeated after three years of establishment.
translations.idiomatic: Woyanqudi had lasted only three years.
